observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management to:

provide enough storage space wherein unserviceable properties could be properly secured and safeguarded against the elements and instruct all offices to surrender their reported unserviceable properties in preparation for their ultimate disposal;

direct the GSO Designate in coordination with the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to finalize the Inspection and Inventory Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) with provision of reasonable appraisal value of the unserviceable properties and promptly cause the disposal of these properties; and

advise the concerned personnel to submit the IIRUP to the Accounting Office upon completion of the disposal activity as it will be the basis for adjusting the balances of the affected PPE accounts.
observations.managementAction:
Management planned to construct or provide a storage room for unserviceable properties for safekeeping, however, funding for the purpose is not yet available. 

The GSO has coordinated with the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to finalize the preparation of IIRUP and to come up with reasonable appraisal value of the unserviceable properties for their eventual disposal.

The GSO has started the preparation of the IIRUP and will submit the same to the Accounting Office upon completion of the disposal of unserviceable properties for the preparation of appropriate JEV to effect the disposed PPEs.
observations.reason:
No fund and/or space available for the purpose.

The IIRUP is not yet completed, hence the disposal activity was not yet conducted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to comply with the posting requirements to the PhilGEPS on all its procurements. 


Also, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that the printout of the PhilGEPS postings of the NOA and NTP be among the supporting documents for the payment of procured goods, services and infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction:
The BAC has started complying with the posting requirements to the PhilGEPS on procurements conducted through the alternative modes of procurement with amount above ₱50,000.00.


The Municipal Accountant has  required that the printout of PhilGEPS postings of the NOA and NTP be attached in the payment of infrastructure projects as among the supporting documents.
observations.reason:
Only the NOAs are uploaded in the PhilGEPS

Only the printout of the NOA that was uploaded in the PhilGEPS was attached in the DV.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Assessor to prioritize the titling of the 35 parcels of land and/or to register under the Torrens Title System in accordance with Section 148 of the Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property Management in the LGUs as prescribed under COA Circular No. 92-386 by allocating appropriate funds in its proposed budget to facilitate titling of these parcels of land.
observations.managementAction:
The Acting Municipal Assessor has started the needed procedures in the titling of the 35 parcels of land and/or register under the Torrens Title System.

The Municipal Mayor also required the Municipal Budget Officer to provide appropriation to facilitate the titling of these parcels of land.
observations.reason: There is only one parcel of land that was titled. The Management will appropriate additional funds for the titling of the remaining parcels of land, and the Acting Municipal Assessor will cause their titling.

observations.managementAction: The Management has implemented some of the PPAs embodied in the MDP
observations.reason: One project on the Construction of Public Comfort Rooms remained unimplemented to date.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to cause the immediate return of the unexpended/ unutilized balances totaling P56,714.37 to the Source Agency.
Henceforth, return all balances of fully implemented project funded from NGAs, thereby allowing the SA/NGAs to reappropriate the same for other priority projects that will benefit stakeholders and to avoid carrying out the balances and frequent preparation of the schedule of unreturned funds under the Due to NGAs account.
observations.managementAction:
Review of the financial statements and supporting schedules disclosed P40,903.66 out of the P56,714.37 were not yet returned to the Source Agency.
Post-audit disclosed that balances of the seven completed infrastructure projects in CY 2024 were returned to the Source Agency in June 2024.
observations.reason: Review disclosed that P40,903.66 out of P56,714.37 were not yet returned to the Source Agency. Henceforth, return all balances of fully implemented project funded from NGAs. Post-audit disclosed that balances of the seven completed infrastructure projects in CY 2024 were returned to the Source Agency in June 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the Municipal Engineer to furnish the AT list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year as mandated on the aforementioned COA Circular in order that the AT could conduct timely validation and monitoring of the implementation of infrastructure projects and to inform management promptly of any deficiency for the latter’s immediate and appropriate action.
observations.managementAction: Records showed that the Municipal Engineer was not able to submit the list of all on-going and to be implemented PPAs of the Municipal Government.
observations.reason: Records showed that the Municipal Engineer was not able to submit the list of all on-going and to be implemented PPAs of the Municipal Government.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require all concerned Department Heads to facilitate the prompt implementation of all projects/ programs funded out of the tobacco excise taxes in order to give the constituents, especially the tobacco farmers who worked hard for the generation of the funds, the early enjoyment of benefits they rightfully deserve.

observations.reason: Review disclosed that there were 66 unimplemented projects/programs as of December 31, 2024. Reiterated in Observation No. 16 of this report.

observations.reason: No separate trial balance or financial statements was prepared for the 20% DF.

observations.recommendation:

Establish internal rules for the suitable criteria or appropriate guidelines in the selection of deserving employees who actually made exemplary suggestions, innovations or accomplishments that contributed to the efficiency, economy or improvement of govt. operations, as evidenced by the awards received by the LGU.

Submit subsequent amendments made in the PRAISE guidelines to the CSC for their evaluation and approval.

The LGU to confer with the Civil Service Personnel regarding the criteria or appropriate guidelines in the selection of deserving employees.



The LGU has submitted the amendment twice in the CSC.

The new rules and guidelines were not yet finalized; hence the approved guidelines prior finalization of amendment was used.

Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the end-user Department Heads to fast-track the completion of the required supporting documents by communicating with the concerned departments, taking actions to address the cause of the delay in the process, and strictly monitor all unpaid obligations to ensure that deficiencies are addressed promptly and paid in a timely manner, in accordance with the terms agreed upon in the PO or contracts and the standard operating procedures provided in the system procedures and/or CC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer, in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and with assistance of the different department heads, involved with the proposed infrastructure project to conduct detailed engineering to ensure optimal utilization of funds and avoid inclusion of projects prone to discontinuance caused by inadequate procurement planning.
