observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer, in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and with the assistance of the different department heads involved with the proposed infrastructure projects under the 20% DF, to meticulously plan, including the considerations for unforeseen circumstances such as the rainy season, to adequately prepare for such occurrences. Additionally, identify projects that are feasible to ensure the optimal utilization of the fund.
observations.managementAction: The Construction of the concrete pathway leading to Budahao Falls was already completed on May 10, 2024, and paid on May 28, 2024 with check number 523575.
observations.reason: The PPA titled Construction of Concrete Pathway was completed. No similar observation was noted during the year under audit.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

Municipal Health Officer to utilize the DPRI as a reference when determining the prices of medicines in their PPMPs in order to avoid incurring excessive price quotations; and

BAC-TWG to review the prices of medicines specified in the purchase requests or PPMPs. In cases where the latest published DPRI does not provide a reference price for certain essential medicines, they may consider using the previous year's awarded price as the ABC Alternatively, they may conduct a market survey to identify and select the most cost-effective price available in the market.
observations.managementAction:
PPMP 2024 for the items not yet procured were revised accordingly. This was also applied in the 2025 PPMP.

BAC-TWG checked the revised 2024 PPMP and the 2025 PPMP using the latest published DPRI and the previously awarded prices as bases.
observations.reason:
No similar observation was noted during the year under audit. 

Implemented

DPRI was used as reference in the revised PPMP of the MHO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in close coordination with the Municipal Engineer, to ensure that all infrastructure projects with work items of embankment, portland cement concrete pavement and stone masonry be supported with field density test, compressive strength test, slump test, and result on test analysis of materials used such as cement, sand, boulders and gravels, respectively, as required in the General Specification of the bidding documents, thereby ensuring the overall structural integrity of implemented projects.
observations.managementAction: The required material testing was conducted. The results were attached to the payment of infrastructure projects.
observations.reason: The material test results were attached to the DVs for infrastructure projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management to prepare a reasonable work plan based from the result of the M&E activities to attain sustainability of the SALINTUBIG Projects and its eventual conversion as a local economic enterprise.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Supply, Sewerage and Sanitation Sector Master Plan was already submitted. The said plan covers the sustainability of SALINTUBIG Projects.
observations.reason: The submitted plan includes sustainability measures for the SALINTUBIG Projects of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the members of the BAC and TWG to:

Ensure that bidders adhere to the eligibility criteria stipulated in the relevant procurement rules and regulations, particularly regarding the validity of the PCAB license, including the ARCC, at the time of bid submission; and

Stop the recommending of the award of contracts in favor of unqualified or ineligible bidders.
observations.managementAction:
Management complied with the audit recommendation.

Management complied with the audit recommendation.
observations.reason:
Scrutiny of pertinent documents showed substantial compliance.

Management was compliant with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

the Municipal Accountant revert to Cash in Bank- LCCA account and recognize the liability for the equivalent amount of stale checks totaling ₱159,424.26 in compliance with Item 2 of Section 59, Volume I of NGAS Manual for LGU;

If the checks are about to be considered as stale, the Municipal Treasurer send written notice to the payee on the existence of the check one month before the check becomes stale; and

If the check is already stale at the hands of the payee and presented for replacement, cancel the check on its face and shall be presented on the RCI after the last check issued for the period indicated in the report, and issue a replacement check accordingly.
observations.managementAction: Necessary adjustments have been made.

observations.recommendation:
We, therefore, recommended that the Municipal Accountant stop recording the fund transfer transactions under the Cash in Local Treasury account to avoid misstatements and to be consistent with the cashbook of the Municipal Treasurer.

On the other hand, the management may resolve the inconsistency by using “Office of the Municipal Treasurer-LGU Burgos” as the payee for the succeeding fund transfer transactions. Consequently, the Municipal Treasurer must issue official receipts to acknowledge the transactions and support that the fund transfer has actually transpired and to be consistent with the Accounting Office who record Cash in Local Treasury account to recognize the fund transfer.
observations.managementAction: The management adopted the recommendation that the payee will be The Municipal Treasurer and that a corresponding OR will be issued.

Collectors are now maintaining individual cash books wherein they recorded their daily collections and submitted daily to the Liquidating Officer.
In addition, Collector’s Report of Collection and Deposits (RCD) are submitted daily to the Liquidating Officer (LO) and collections are deposited the next banking day.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MPDC continue to initiate close coordination with the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant for the proper monitoring of project implementation and the provision of all the necessary information in the Status Report of Government PPAs before submission to the Audit Team. Likewise, the Municipal Engineer maintains timely implementation of PPAs in coordination with the MPDC under their responsibility at all times.

We recommended that management rectify the defects particularly on stationings from Sta. 0+099 – Sta. 0+118; and Sta. 0+156 – Sta. 0+165 with total equivalent damage cost of ₱89,465.60 along with the 25 lines (half lane) of temperature cracks in the portland cement in order to extend the lifespan of the road and update the Audit Team for any action taken, otherwise, the computed cost of damage will become deficiency that may cause issuance of a Notice of Disallowance.

We recommended that management remove and replace the road particularly on the section 4 stationings from 0+0000 to 0+003 with equivalent cost of ₱9,598.125 in order to extend the lifespan of the road and update the Audit Team for any action taken, otherwise, the computed cost of damage will become deficiency that may cause issuance of a Notice of Disallowance.

We recommended that management rectify the defects particularly on the section 2 stationings from Sta. 0+0000 to 0+028; 0+153 – Sta. 0+231; and  0+295 – Sta. 0+301 with total equivalent damage cost of ₱365,050.00 along with the 26 lines (half lane) of temperature cracks in the Portland cement in order to extend the lifespan of the road and update the Audit Team for any action taken, otherwise, the computed cost of damage with become deficiency that may cause issuance of a Notice of Disallowance.
We recommended that the Municipal Engineer inform the contractor to carry out asphalt sealant application to maintain the durability of the concrete pavement.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer initiate the repair to include repainting of reflectorized thermoplastic pavement yellow on bridge pavement in accordance with the inspection report of the Technical Audit Group.
We recommended that management provide additional supports at the bottom of the tank in order to withstand the load and to maintain stability/integrity of the structure.

(a) make the necessary adjusting entries to remove from the Inventory account the items already issued to end-users; (b) and the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer (GSO) prepare and maintain the prescribed forms, registries, and reports mentioned in COA Circular No. 2022-004 to strengthen controls over the semi-expendable properties; and to segregate semi-expendable properties as low-valued and high-valued items.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already made the necessary adjusting entries and strictly followed the proper classification. The Municipal General Services Officer (GSO) prepare and maintain the prescribed forms, registries and reports mentioned in COA Circular No. 2022-004.

observations.recommendation: That the Municipal Accountant draw the necessary adjusting journal entries to reflect the correct amount of the affected accounts, use appropriate accounts for recording their transactions, and properly observe the substance over form policy to ensure the accuracy and reliability of reported balances in the Financial Statements at year-end.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entries were already drawn and the affected accounts were properly classified to its appropriate accounts.

observations.recommendation: That the Municipal Accountant draw the necessary adjusting journal entries to reflect the correct amount of the affected accounts and use appropriate accounts in the ensuing year for payment of bonuses to ensure the accuracy and reliability of reported balances in the Financial Statements as at year-end.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accounting Office has been strictly following the proper classification of the expenses and the recording for these accounts.

observations.recommendation: That the Municipal Accountant, together with the Municipal Assessor, verify their historical data or records that could shed light on the acquisition costs of the properties and, if available, make an assessment so that the necessary adjusting entries shall be made to record all land owned by the Municipality, in consonance with Section 111 of Presidential Decree 1445 and International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) Nos. 1 and 17.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Accountant already reconciled their records and adjusting entries were already made.

observations.recommendation: That the Municipal Accountant drop the amount of the distributed donated carabaos in the LGU’s books of accounts and draw adjusting journal entries to reflect the correct balance of the affected account in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entries were already made and the affected accounts were reconciled.

observations.recommendation: (a) Municipal Accountant recognize first as inventory in the books of accounts all the office supplies procured by the LGU and only be recorded as an expense upon issuance to end-users; (b) a supplies ledger card (SLC) for each item of supplies and/or other inventory items shall be maintained to record the acquisition, receipt, and issuance pursuant to the Manual on the NGAS; and the GSO to consolidate the issuances, supported with RIS, on a monthly basis in the SSMI to be submitted to the Municipal Accountant, who shall compute the cost of supplies issued and ending inventory using the moving average method as prescribed under Section 121 of the same Manual.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accounting Office already started recognizing inventory and preparing necessary reports such as RIS, SSMI, and SLC to ensure proper recording of the procured office supplies.

observations.recommendation: (a) GSO maintain PPE Cards, and the Accounting Office prepare and maintain PPE Ledgers Cards and ensure that reconciliation of their records is done before the preparation of the annual financial statements; and (b) that the Municipal Accountant, Property Custodian, and Inventory Committee complete the reconciliation of the RPCPPE with the Accounting Office records to validate the accuracy of the PPEs.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant, GSO and Property Custodian were able to start the process of complying with the guidelines prescribed in the COA Circular. They assured that they will continue with implementation and that reconciliation will be done in the current year.

observations.recommendation: That the Municipal Accountant monitor and perform constant review and appraisal of transactions to deter errors and omissions; and reconcile and effect adjustments to reflect correct balances of Due from/to Other Funds and Due from/to Special Accounts in the financial statements, pursuant to Section 111(2) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: All the concerned accounts were already reconciled and liquidations were submitted to the Office of the Auditor within the required timeframe.

observations.recommendation: (a) Municipal Management institute action against the defaulting NGOs/POs pursuant to the provisions of the COA Circular 2007-001; and (b) the Accounting Office send demand letters to NGOs/POs and requesting them to submit the Final Utilization Report together with the inspection report and certificate of project completion, and the list of beneficiaries with their acceptance/acknowledgment of the project/fund/goods/service received.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Management already sent a demand letter to the defaulting NGOs/POs. The Accounting Office has sent demand letters to NGOs/POs requesting submission of final utilization reports and supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: That the Sangguniang Bayan and the end-user of the LDRRM Fund ensure that realignment/reprogramming is always supported by an appropriation ordinance and strictly adhere to Section 336 of RA 7160 to ensure the validity and legality of the newly programmed PPAs and their implementation.
observations.managementAction: The Management instructed all end-users to ensure that any changes in the Annual Budget must be supported by an Appropriation Ordinance.

observations.recommendation: That the LDRRM Officer prepare programs that are achievable and take into consideration the actual and/or real needs of their constituents to ensure full utilization and effective implementation of the corresponding programs, projects, and activities.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRM Officer committed to propose programs that are achievable and utilize or maximize the usage of the said funds.

observations.recommendation: That the LDRRM Officer not release goods for stockpiling/prepositioning to ineligible beneficiaries and remind them that such items will only be released during emergency cases or already stored for a long time but before their expiration dates, supported with proper documentation.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRM Officer stopped releasing goods for stockpiling to ineligible beneficiaries and reminded end-users that such items will only be released during emergency cases and will be stored with proper documentation.

observations.recommendation: That the Municipal Treasurer not use the collections for the vales and cash advances, immediately deposit all the undeposited collections to the depository bank, allot sufficient budget for traveling expenses, and instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to monitor regularly to avoid repetition of the same deficiency.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer already made countermeasures and committed to timely deposit undeposited collections and allocate sufficient budget for traveling expenses.

observations.recommendation: That the Municipal Treasurer enforce and adhere to the existing regulation on unremitted collections and remittances and report daily collections to the Municipal Treasurer to reflect correct income and cash balances in the financial statements; and oblige the erring collector to restore/refund the embezzled amount, if any.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer has already made measures to enforce timely remittance and reporting by collectors and committed that this will not be repeated.

observations.recommendation: (a) Municipal Accountant thoroughly review the supporting documents in all the transactions before certifying cost and transfer the title of the procured lots; (b) allot enough budget to process the requirements to transfer the ownership of procured lots by prioritizing the transfer of the name in the tax declaration.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant stated that they were able to allot enough budget to process the transfer of one lot to the LGU’s name and will prioritize transfer of the other lots, but supporting documents for some lots remain unavailable.
observations.reason: Not supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: That the MPDO strictly comply with the provisions set forth under Section 2 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and the applicable provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184; require the contractors to expedite the completion of the above infrastructure projects; and require the Municipal Engineering Office to closely monitor the work accomplishment of all the contractors with projects that have “ongoing” or “not implemented” status.
observations.managementAction: All the concerned projects were completed in CY 2024.

observations.recommendation: That the Municipal Accountant and accountable officers observe strictly the submission of Statement of Receipts and Disbursements, Report of Checks Issued, Report of Disbursements together with the vouchers and their complete supporting documents to the Implementing Agency’s Auditor within the required timeframe as provided for in COA Circular No. 94-013; and submit the required documents and/or other reports to the Source Agency for liquidation.
observations.managementAction: All the concerned liquidations were submitted to the Office of the Auditor within the required timeframe as provided for in COA Circular No. 94-013.

observations.recommendation: (a) Municipal Accounting Office make use of the free accounting system provided by the Commission on Audit by writing a letter of intent addressed to the Commission on Audit and accomplishing the Technical Requirements Checklist for an efficient and effective computerized accounting system that will generate a more reliable and accurate balance of accounts; (b) moreover, allot enough budget for the related expenses in the implementation of the said system.
observations.managementAction: The LGU still had a problem with the budget and the procurement process for an accounting system but made preparations (letter of intent, technical checklist) and will comply with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer deposit his collections regularly and intact in a cost-efficient way to prevent misappropriation of funds and possible loss.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer committed to deposit all his collections intact.
observations.reason:
Cash examination conducted last December 27, 2023 showed that deposits were still not deposited intact. Due to non-strict monitoring of collections and deposits, an over deposit worth ₱44,395.74 (₱22,197.87 each under GF and SEF). The over deposit should be receipted and be treated as miscellaneous income
of the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to investigate whether there were any misrepresentations made by the contractor with regard to its declaration on the personnel requirement for the government project. We further recommended management to instruct the BAC and its TWG to conduct thorough verification on the documents provided by the bidders to enable the agency to award projects to qualified bidders and guaranty the efficient implementation of the projects.
observations.managementAction: Management committed through its BAC to thoroughly verify the submitted documents of the bidders.
observations.reason: Auditorial contract review of the awarded infrastructure projects disclosed that BAC fall short on verification of the bidder’s qualification on their personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to collect the fees embodied in the Municipal Revenue Code and to establish strong coordination with the Agriculture Office for immediate identification whom to collect.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Assessor to prioritize the titling of the 35 parcels of land and/or to register under the Torrens Title System in accordance with Section 148 of the Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property Management in the LGUs as prescribed under COA Circular No. 92-386 by allocating appropriate funds in its proposed budget to facilitate titling of these parcels of land.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to prioritize the titling of the parcels of land.

appropriate the savings of funds derived from the previous implementation of PPAs to new PPAs that are  aligned to the purpose of MDF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer to see to it that all collectors shall turn-over their collections at the end of each day. Maria, Ilocos Sur, hence nearer, more accessible depository bank thereby saving travel time and expenses.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to: (a) observe the Perpetual Inventory Method of accounting for inventories; (b) maintain supplies ledger cards; and (c) reconcile regularly with the records maintained by the Municipal Treasurer.




Require the Municipal Treasurer/Acting GSO to (a) maintain stock cards; (b) prepare and submit the SSMI together with the original copy of the RIS to the Municipal Accountant as basis in recording the issuances of inventory items; and (c) regularly reconcile with the inventory records maintained by the Accounting Office.




Instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of inventories every year and submit the corresponding report to the Auditor not later January 31 for verification.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Complied.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE designate a personnel in-charge, who can easily be trained to perform the posting requirements or at least possesses a technical background of the task, and consider sending him for training to become knowledgeable in posting the complete bidding documents in the PhilGEPS and of bid opportunities and notices in the agency website. Likewise, we recommended that the procuring entity strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of the RA No. 9184, especially on the posting requirements in the PhilGEPS and agency website, to ensure transparency and accountability in the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: Partially complied.

observations.recommendation:

Require the Municipal Treasurer to maintain an individual ledger for each construction contractor to record and monitor their gross receipts and tax payment during the year. Moreover, adhere to the provisions of Municipal Ordinance No. 03, series of 2022 on the assessment and collection of business tax from construction contractors.




Direct the Municipal Accountant to draw journal entries to properly record subsequent collections of business taxes during the current year by debiting Cash in Bank-LCCA and crediting Other Deferred Credits.




Instruct the BAC Secretariat to inform the Municipal Treasurer of the awarded construction contractors with their ongoing and upcoming projects including the contract amounts which will serve as his basis in the computation of business tax to be collected.

No action taken by management.








Complied.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the implementing offices to coordinate and strategize the implementation of all the programs/ projects embodied under the 20% DF Annual Investment Plan to attain desirable socio-economic targets and outcomes for the Municipality and intended beneficiaries. Moreover, require them to review the composition of the 20% DF, identify the savings from fully implemented projects and those which are no longer prioritized and, subject to the provisions of the Local Government Code and the DILG-DBM JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020, for reappropriation to current/existing priority development projects.




In addition, we also recommended management to program only projects which are attainable and implementable within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: Partially complied.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE cause the prompt implementation of all projects/ programs funded out of the tobacco excise tax in order to give the constituents, especially the tobacco farmers the early benefits that could be derived therefrom. observations.managementAction: Partially complied.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor order the GSO-Designate to maintain and update records regarding acquisition, description, custody, estimated life, rate of depreciation, disposal, and other information of PPE based on the source documents of the transactions using the Property Cards and reconcile the same with the Accounting Records.

We also recommended that the GSO-Designate, in coordination with the Inventory Committee shall prepare a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) to complete the physical inventory within the prescribed period for the purpose of one-time cleansing of the PPE accounts, and to undertake the disposal of the non-existing PPEs and unserviceable PPEs, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall prepare and update the record of acquisition/disposal/transfer of PPEs in the PPELCs and ensure that the total balance of PPELCs tally with the balances of controlling PPE accounts in General Ledger.

Furthermore, we recommended the Municipal Accountant to trace the acquisition date and assess the remaining useful life of the PPEs and compute retroactively the corresponding depreciation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality will activate the Inventory Committee.
observations.reason: Updating and reconciliation were still ongoing. | Reiterated. Refer to Observation No. 1, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:

prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to reclassify the completed projects from the CIP to the appropriate PPE account, and henceforth effect the reclassification of the completed projects upon completion as shown in the Certificate of Completion; and

recognize the corresponding depreciation of the PPE from the date of completion. Depreciation shall be for one month if the PPE is available for use on or before the 15th of the month, and on the succeeding month if the PPE is available for use after the 15th of the month.

Reclassify the amounts representing Municipal Aid projects for the barangay projects which were included in the CIP account, and submit JEV together with the complete supporting documents to ascertain the nature and existence of the transactions.
observations.reason: Reiterated. Refer to Observation No. 2, Part II of this report.

CR: Prior Years Adjustment* Pxxx (*to represent surplus adjustment)
