observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the GSO Designate to update his records and prepare property transfer report and for the Municipal Accountant to derecognize the properties issued to public schools upon transfer.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
provide enough storage space wherein unserviceable properties could be properly secured and safeguarded against the elements and instruct all offices to surrender their reported unserviceable properties in preparation for their ultimate disposal;
direct the GSO Designate in coordination with the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to finalize the Inspection and Inventory Report of Unserviceable Property with provision of reasonable appraisal value of the unserviceable properties and promptly cause the disposal of these properties; and
advise the concerned personnel to submit the IIRUP to the Accounting Office upon completion of the disposal activity as it will be the basis for adjusting the balances of the affected PPE accounts.

3.8 We recommended and Management agreed to conduct regular review of fund and property accountabilities of AOs, including those officers who are authorized signatories and counter signatories in the issuance of checks and approval of DVs, to determine the appropriate amount of their fidelity bonds.
3.9  We further recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to apply for the increase in the fidelity bond of the AOs to commensurate their accountabilities to provide adequate protection on the Municipality’s resources.

4.4 We recommended that Management require the BAC to comply with the posting requirements to the PhilGEPS on all its procurements.
4.5  Also, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that the printout of the PhilGEPS postings of the NOA and NTP be among the supporting documents for the payment of procured goods, services and infrastructure projects.

5.6 We recommended and Management agreed to require the contractors to have the materials be tested by duly accredited DOST/BRS laboratories and submit the result of test(s) analysis to ensure the quality of materials used.

6.4 We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all DVs are properly supported with complete documentation and in no case shall be paid without the necessary supporting documents.
6.5  Also, immediately submit to COA the lacking supporting documents for proper evaluation.

7.4 We recommended and Management agreed to require the MBO in coordination with the Municipal Engineer to review the unobligated appropriations of PPAs carried over as of CY 2023 Continuing Appropriations to determine which are eligible of continuing PPAs and thereafter, revert the unobligated appropriations of completed PPAs to be used for other potential development projects.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to file a request for authority to write-off the identified dormant receivables, supported with documents enumerated in Annex 8 of the COA Circular No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023 in order to present fairly the receivables and government equity accounts reflected in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of its Property, Plant and Equipment and Inventories and to submit immediately the required RPCI and RPCPPE to the AT not later than January 31 of each year as mandated in the aforementioned Section 124, Volume I of the MNGAS for LGUs. Furthermore, observe strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 in order to reflect reliable and accurate balances of PPE accounts, hence to convey precise information useful in decision making and accountability for these properties.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile the discrepancy of the cash balances between the cashbooks and the ledger to reflect the correct amount of Cash - Local Treasury account in the financial statements, furthermore, reconcile the cashbook and ledger balances at least quarterly as required of the applicable policies on Cash Management, thus, an accurate, complete and reliable cash balance. will be maintained.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare an accounting entry by debiting Due from Officers and Employees and crediting Prior Period Adjustment in the amount of P14,400.00. Moreover, require the Municipal Treasurer to refund the whole amount for being inattentive of his duties and responsibilities. Also, require the exercise of due diligence in the issuance of check by religiously monitoring the cash in bank balances maintained by the municipality in order to avoid the incurrence of bank charges and loss of government funds.

5.5 	We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer to see to it that all collectors shall turn-over their collections at the end of each day. Moreover, require also the Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact daily or in the next banking day all collections turned over to him including his collections in order not to expose the public funds to a possible risk of loss due to theft, loss or misappropriation. Likewise, secure approval from the Sanggunian Bayan to open a current bank account with Landbank Sta. Maria Branch, Sta. Maria, Ilocos Sur, hence nearer, more accessible depository bank thereby saving travel time and expenses.

6.8 	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to see to it that all eligibility requirements are present and valid at the time of submission and opening of bids using a nondiscretionary “pass/fail” criterion in order to ascertain that all government contracts will be awarded only to eligible contractors or suppliers.

7.7 	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the BAC to instruct the BAC Secretariat to upload in the PhilGEPS and agency website the complete set of bidding documents, bid opportunities, notices of award and notices to proceed of all procurements through competitive bidding as required in several provisions of the 2016 IRR of RA 9184 in order to ensure that transparency in the procurement process is observed.

8.6 	We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the BAC to submit justifications/explanations on the aforementioned lapses. Moreover, require the BAC to issue bidding documents only after payment of the required fee unless uploaded in the PhilGEPS or agency websites in which case the bid proposals may be accepted outright after the payment of the required fee, thus impartiality in the procurement process is observed.

9.6 	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to compute and withhold taxes from the payment of BAC honoraria as mandated in BIR Revenue Regulations No. 11-2018 dated January 31, 2018 and remit the same to the BIR within the prescribed period in order not to deprive the National Government of the additional income that could be utilized for various programs, projects and activities.

10.5 	In order for the Municipal Government to secure its funds, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to renew its fidelity bond. Henceforth, see to it that renewal is made annually and before the expiration of the present bond.

11.6 	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to approve employees’ loan applications to GFIs only within their capacity to pay and to disapprove applications for private lending institutions in order to meet the minimum net take home pay as required under Section 56 of General Appropriations Act of 2023. Thus, the employees could possibly meet the basic family needs before the next payroll period which could motivate them to work effectively and efficiently.  Also, the government will not act as collecting agent of the private companies, hence the additional expense of the government will be avoided.

12.8 	We strongly recommended that the municipal government facilitate availment of FINDES or the Financial Data Entry System of the Landbank of the Philippines to reduce cash advances as a way of strengthening internal control on cash management.

We recommended and Management agreed to cause the immediate return of the unexpended/unutilized balances totaling P56,714.37 to the Source Agency. Henceforth, return all balances of fully implemented project funded from NGAs, thereby allowing the SA/NGAs to reappropriate the same for other priority projects that will benefit stakeholders and to avoid carrying out the balances and frequent preparation of the schedule of unreturned funds under the Due to NGAs account.

14.4 	We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the Municipal Engineer to furnish the AT list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year as mandated on the aforementioned COA Circular in order that the AT could conduct timely validation and monitoring of the implementation of infrastructure projects and to inform management promptly of any deficiency for the latter’s immediate and appropriate action.

15.6 	We recommended and the Management agreed to ensure that all vital information are provided in the project billboard, inform the AT in writing on the posting at the project site within ten days after the award of the contract and update the project status portion of the billboard based on the table prescribed in COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 20, 2023 in order to promote transparency in the implementation of infrastructure project, thus the people are well informed on matters of public concern.

16.4 	We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF not later than the 15th day after the end of each month and submit the same to the LDRRMO for the latter to reconcile with his records after which submit the same to the Auditor who will conduct review, verification and monitoring of the sources and utilization of LDRRMF, thus deficiencies if any, will be informed to Management for the latter’s immediate and appropriate corrective action thereof.

17.6 	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor intensify the implementation of the PPAs funded out from the 20% DF and require the MPDC to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to monitor closely the implementation thereof so as to deliver to the constituents’ timely benefits that could be derived from these projects and to achieve a broader scope of development. We also recommended management to reappropriate/realign projects which are not implementable due to some constraints including the amounts saved from projects which were already implemented. In addition, we also recommended management to program only projects which are attainable and implementable within the prescribed period.

18.9 	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require all concerned Department Heads to facilitate the prompt implementation of all projects/programs funded out of the tobacco excise taxes in order to give the constituents, especially the tobacco farmers who worked hard for the generation of the funds, the early enjoyment of benefits they rightfully deserve.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the trial balance and financial statements for the 20% DF to assist the users of the financial statements in making decisions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, together with the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant, to obtain authorization from the SB in the opening of a separate bank account for the 20% DF. Thereafter, transfer the balance of the 20% DF from the bank account where it was deposited.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer, in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and with the assistance of the different department heads involved with the proposed infrastructure projects under the 20% DF, to meticulously plan, including the considerations for unforeseen circumstances such as the rainy season, to adequately prepare for such occurrences. Additionally, identify projects that are feasible to ensure the optimal utilization of the fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
Municipal Health Officer to utilize the DPRI as a reference when determining the prices of medicines in their PPMPs in order to avoid incurring excessive price quotations; and
BAC-TWG to review the prices of medicines specified in the purchase requests or PPMPs. In cases where the latest published DPRI does not provide a reference price for certain essential medicines, they may consider using the previous year's awarded price as the ABC Alternatively, they may conduct a market survey to identify and select the most cost-effective price available in the market.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the LGU-BURGOS-PRAISE Committee to:
Establish internal rules for the suitable criteria or appropriate guidelines in the selection of deserving employees who actually made exemplary suggestions, innovations or accomplishments that contributed to the efficiency, economy or improvement of government operations, as evidenced by the awards received by the municipality;
Submit subsequent amendments made in the PRAISE guidelines to the CSC for their evaluation and approval; and
Submit immediately the annual PRAISE report for CY 2023 to the same, copy furnished the Audit Team, and submit the report on the ensuing years on or before the 30th day of January.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply on the computation of honoraria for lecturers, resource persons, coordinators and facilitators who hail from different government agencies.
We likewise recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the adoption of a clear policy on the computation of honoraria to lecturers, resource persons and facilitators, specifically those who are in private sector such as freelancers and/or foreign speakers, taking into their qualifications and expertise.
Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to impose expanded withholding tax of either five percent or 10 percent relative to withholding of income tax.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in close coordination with the Municipal Engineer, to ensure that all infrastructure projects with work items of embankment, portland cement concrete pavement and stone masonry be supported with field density test, compressive strength test, slump test, and result on test analysis of materials used such as cement, sand, boulders and gravels, respectively, as required in the General Specification of the bidding documents, thereby ensuring the overall structural integrity of implemented projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the end-user Department Heads to fast-track the completion of the required supporting documents by communicating with the concerned departments, taking actions to address the cause of the delay in the process, and strictly monitor all unpaid obligations to ensure that deficiencies are addressed promptly and paid in a timely manner, in accordance with the terms agreed upon in the PO or contracts and the standard operating procedures provided in the system procedures and/or Citizen’s Charter.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Inventory Committee to:
Submit the RPCPPE within the reglementary period; and
Conduct a thorough and complete physical inventory of all properties owned by the agency.
Instruct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Inventory Committee, to reconcile PPE ledger cards with the result of the physical count by PPE classification and immediately make the necessary adjustments, in case of detection of errors, duly supported with complete documentation.
Adopt and implement the one-time cleansing to reconcile PPE accounts and establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books to take-up the three parcels of land unrecorded therein;
Instruct the Municipal Assessor, in coordination with the GSO and Municipal Accountant, to prioritize the titling of the 12 parcels of land by gathering all the required documents to secure Certificate of Title in the name of the Municipality; and
Require the Municipal Budget Officer to allocate funds to cover expenses associated with land registration process.

We recommended that Management instruct the GSO to:
Ensure that all insurable properties, excluding those classified as unserviceable, be covered by GIF to protect them from any damage or loss due to fortuitous events;
Prepare and submit the PIF to include all insurable properties to the Audit Team and the GIF of the GSIS not later than April 30 of each year; and
Include in the Annual Investment Plan the cost of premiums required for the insurance of these properties.

We recommended that Management instruct the members of the BAC and TWG to:
Ensure that bidders adhere to the eligibility criteria stipulated in the relevant procurement rules and regulations, particularly regarding the validity of the PCAB license, including the ARCC, at the time of bid submission; and
Stop the recommending of the award of contracts in favor of unqualified or ineligible bidders.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to:
Continue sending notices to delinquent taxpayers;
Conduct information drive campaigns to raise awareness about the benefits of paying real property taxes in advance and encourage prompt payment; and
Apply the legal remedies available to ensure the collection of delinquent taxes.
We recommended that Management ensure the submission of all the perfected contracts together with the complete supporting documents within the required period.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer, to ensure proper charges to the LDRRMF;
Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that the PPAs included in the budget under the LDRRMF are directly related to DRRM purposes; and
Instruct the MDRRMO to prioritize the implementation of PPAs under the LDRRMF including its Continuing Appropriations.

We recommended that Management instruct the GAD focal person to:
Properly plan the PPAs to be funded under the GAD Fund by creating the GAD Database to determine gender issues within the Municipality;
Prepare and submit within the reglementary period the GAD AR using the prescribed form to facilitate review and evaluation; and
Closely monitor the optimal utilization and implementation of the GAD fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to (a) recognize and record the differences noted in the Accumulated Depreciation as of December 31, 2023 totalling ₱1,111,446.32 in absolute value (See Annex J Summary); and (b) thoroughly review the lapsing schedule and strictly follow the depreciation rules outlined in the notes to the financial statements to avoid inaccuracy.

We recommended that:
the Municipal Accountant revert to Cash in Bank- LCCA account and recognize the liability for the equivalent amount of stale checks totalling ₱159,424.26 in compliance with Item 2 of Section 59, Volume I of NGAS Manual for LGU;
If the checks are about to be considered as stale, the Municipal Treasurer send written notice to the payee on the existence of the check one month before the check becomes stale; and
If the check is already stale at the hands of the payee and presented for replacement, cancel the check on its face and shall be presented on the RCI after the last check issued for the period indicated in the report, and issue a replacement check accordingly.

We recommended that Management exhaust all remedies to validate the existence and propriety of the receivable accounts. Further, coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to facilitate completion of the documents required to make request for write-off of the dormant accounts in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005 for fair presentation of the accounts affected in the financial statements.

We, therefore, recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all his Department Heads in charge to observe the guidelines of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 in formulating PPAs to be funded out of the LDRRMF. Likewise, the MDRRMO must carefully plan the PPAs to be included in the LDRRMP and strive for their timely and full implementation and strictly utilize the funds according to the guidelines to achieve the maximum benefits for the Municipality’s constituents.

We, therefore, recommended that the Municipal Accountant stop recording the fund transfer transactions under the Cash in Local Treasury account to avoid misstatements and to be consistent with the cashbook of the Municipal Treasurer.
On the other hand, the management may resolve the inconsistency by using “Office of the Municipal Treasurer-LGU Burgos” as the payee for the succeeding fund transfer transactions. Consequently, the Municipal Treasurer must issue official receipts to acknowledge the transactions and support that the fund transfer has actually transpired and to be consistent with the Accounting Office who record Cash in Local Treasury account to recognize the fund transfer.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to:
strictly comply with the regulation on the rendition of accounts and financial reports in order not to delay the verification and post-audit activities; and
observe the due dates of submission of the required financial reports and documents to the Audit Team by setting timelines of submission from Treasury Office to Accounting Office. Likewise, maintain processing and payment of transactions with complete supporting documents. Without doing so, delays will affect both the Treasury Office and the Accounting Office on submission of accounts.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), through its secretariat, to submit all contracts, together with all the supporting documents forming part thereof, within 5 days from its perfection to the Auditor to enable the latter to conduct auditorial review, before these contracts are forwarded to COA, Regional Office for technical evaluation.

In order to strengthen the internal control of the agency in safeguarding its assets, we highly recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to exercise the diligence of a good father of a family in supervising the Accountable Officers under his control to prevent the incurrence of loss or misuse of government funds and property or otherwise he shall be jointly and solidarily liable with them in pursuance to Sections 104, 123 and 124 of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445.

We, therefore, recommended that the Municipal Treasurer account for the Seventeen Thousand Two Hundred Thirty-One (17,231) pieces of missing accountable forms and prepare/review in full the monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) before submission to the Office of the Auditor.

We, therefore, recommended that the GFPS:
conduct a gender analysis of their programs and projects through the administration of the HGDG tool to be able to incorporate the PPAs that address gender issues in the GPB as well as mainstreaming in the agency operations so that the formulation, implementation, evaluation, and accounting of results of the LGU’s annual GPB and GAD AR will be in order;
build capabilities through trainings to deepen their knowledge and build their competencies on GAD-related laws and international commitments, gender mainstreaming, gender analysis, gender-responsive planning and budgeting, and GAD-related tools among others; and
submit the DILG-reviewed GPB within the period required by Part V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.

We, therefore, recommended that the MGSO:
regularly monitor the expiration and renewal of registration of all motor vehicles and apply or renew the registration of all serviceable motor vehicles of the Municipality with LTO and contracts of insurance with the GSIS to avoid penalties and payment of damages to third party liabilities in case of vehicular accidents; and
mark all Municipality-owned motor vehicles with the appropriate marking as required by Paragraph V.2 of COA Circular No. 75-6 dated November 7, 1975 to properly monitor the use hereof and gasoline expenditures. After which, furnish the Audit Team with pictures showing compliance with the audit recommendations.

We recommended that the MPDC continue to initiate close coordination with the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant for the proper monitoring of project implementation and the provision of all the necessary information in the Status Report of Government PPAs before submission to the Audit Team. Likewise, the Municipal Engineer maintains timely implementation of PPAs in coordination with the MPDC under their responsibility at all times.

We recommended that management rectify the defects particularly on stationings from Sta. 0+099 – Sta. 0+118; and Sta. 0+156 – Sta. 0+165 with total equivalent damage cost of ₱89,465.60 along with the 25 lines (half lane) of temperature cracks in the portland cement concrete in order to extend the lifespan of the road and update the Audit Team for any action taken, otherwise, the computed cost of damage will become deficiency that may cause issuance of a Notice of Disallowance.

We recommended that management conduct R&R (Remove and Replace) on the road particularly on the section 4 stationings from 0+0000 to 0+003 with equivalent cost of ₱9,598.125 in order to extend the lifespan of the road and update the Audit Team for any action taken, otherwise, the computed cost of damage will become deficiency that may cause issuance of a Notice of Disallowance.

We recommended that management rectify the defects particularly on the section 2 stationings from Sta. 0+0000 to 0+028; 0+153 – Sta. 0+231; and  0+295 – Sta. 0+301 with total equivalent damage cost of ₱365,050.00 along with the 26 lines (half lane) of temperature cracks in the Portland cement in order to extend the lifespan of the road and update the Audit Team for any action taken, otherwise, the computed cost of damage with become deficiency that may cause issuance of a Notice of Disallowance.
We recommended that the Municipal Engineer inform the contractor to carry out asphalt sealant application to maintain the durability of the concrete pavement.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer initiate the repair to include repainting of reflectorized thermoplastic pavement yellow on bridge pavement in accordance with the inspection report of the Technical Audit Group.
We recommended that management provide additional supports at the bottom of the tank in order to withstand the load and to maintain stability/integrity of the structure.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor order the:
GSO-Designate:
to maintain and update Property Cards regarding acquisition, description, custody, disposal, and other information about the PPE, based on the source documents of the transactions, and reconcile with the Accounting Records and RPCPPE.
Inventory Committee, in coordination with the GSO and Municipal Accountant:
To prepare a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) to complete the physical inventory within the prescribed period for one-time cleansing of the PPE accounts.
Prepare a report on the result of the physical count through RPCPPE including list of unserviceable PPEs, missing PPEs, and items found at station.
Any discrepancy between the RPCPPE, PPELCs, and PCs shall be investigated, verified, and adjusted. The total reconciled balances for each PPE account shall be established as the correct balance of PPE at year-end; and
to undertake the disposition of the non-existing PPEs and unserviceable PPEs in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006.
To submit a Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) not later than January of each year and reconcile it with the accounting records.
Municipal Accountant:
Prepare and update the record of acquisition/disposal/transfer of PPEs in the PPELC and ensure that the total balance of PPELCs tall with the balances of controlling PPE accounts in General Ledger.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:
Recognize the corresponding depreciation of the infrastructure assets from the date of completion as indicated in the Certificate of Completion, and from the date of delivery as indicated in the Acceptance and Inspection Report for the machinery and equipment. The depreciation shall be for one month if the PPE is available for use on or before the 15th of the month, and on the succeeding month if the PPE is available for use after the 15th of the month.
For those PPE Items that the date of acquisition cannot be determined with certainty, review and reassess the remaining useful life and depreciate accordingly based on the newly assessed useful life.
The depreciation shall be made in an item-to-item and duly supported with a schedule of depreciated properties or Lapsing Schedule.
Make necessary adjustments in the books to record the unrecognized deprecation and make necessary disclosure as to the PPE accounts that are fully depreciated but still in use.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
the Municipal Treasurer to cancel any unreleased checks that have become stale and report the same to the Municipal Accountant for the preparation of appropriate journal entry voucher; and
the Municipal Accountant to:
request from the bank the Bank Statements for DBP Account No. 0-52224-760-7 and furnish to the Municipal Accountant for the preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statements.
prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement for all bank accounts of the Municipality, and
record the appropriate adjusting journal entries to adjust the identified book reconciling items and cancellation of checks, and to recognize in the books of accounts the unreported bank account of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the following actions:
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to regularly submit the Monthly Billings/Consumer Account Reports to the Office of the Municipal Accountant, indicating therein the consumption and amount due per consumer to the Office of Municipal Accountant.
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to provide a certified true copy of the duly notarized Lease Contract for every existing tenant of the Market Stalls to the Municipal Accountant. Furthermore, ensure the submission of a Monthly List of New and Outgoing Tenants of the Market Stalls supported by either a notarized Lease Contract or a Letter of Cancellation of the Lease Contract, as applicable.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to make adjusting entries to record the uncollected Waterworks System Fees and Rental Fees after verifying the accounts of the consumers and tenants. Additionally, establish monthly receivables and recognize income based on the Monthly Consumer Account Reports and Lease Contracts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor strictly comply with the implementation and utilization timelines provided in the Memorandum of Agreement entered into by the Municipality and Source Agencies. Also, direct:
The Municipal Accountant to:
prepare the appropriate journal entries to reclassify the award or incentives from the Due to NGAs to Trust Liabilities account;
immediately liquidate the fund transfers after the fulfillment of its conditions.
Issue a refund of P18,294.11 from the Trust Fund to the General Fund to settle the remaining fund transfer for the One COVID Allowance.
The Bids and Awards Committee Chairperson to take into account the timeline of project implementation as provided in the Memorandum of Agreement in the scheduling of procurement activities so as not to compromise the timely fulfillment of the purpose of fund transfers.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor obtain the authorization of the Sangguniang Bayan to enter into a Memorandum of Agreement when collaborating with another agency to jointly implement a program to delineate the responsibilities of both parties.  Also direct the:
a)	Municipal Accountant to:
i.	establish the correct balance of the unspent DRRMF and ensure that all unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE shall be transferred to the Special Trust Fund at yearend; and
ii.	disclose all relevant information pertaining the available, utilized and balances of the current year and Special Trust Fund of Local DRRM Fund in the Notes to the Financial Statements.
b)\tMDRRMO to ensure that all programs/projects/activities charged against all sources of the LDRRMF are incorporated in the LDRRMFIP or Revised LDRRMFIP.
c)\tMunicipal Planning and Development Coordinator to prepare a supplemental Annual Investment Program based on the LDRRMFIP or Revised LDRRMFIP, and secure approval of the Sangguniang Bayan prior to the commencement of project implementation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
a)\tRequire the deputy collectors to remit all collections daily and intact to the Municipal Treasurer which should be covered by a certified Summary of Collections and Remittances and supported by the duplicate copies of the issued receipts; and
b)\tIssue a written authority deputizing the Barangay Treasurers to collect Community Tax Certificate on his behalf.  In the absence of said authority, require the collectors to immediately turnover collections and accountable forms to the Municipal Treasurer.  Henceforth, no accountable forms shall be issued to Barangay Treasurers without a written authority as deputy collectors.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the BAC Chairperson, the following:
Require the submission of the latest Business Tax Return within five (5) calendar days from receipt by the bidder of the notice from the BAC that the bidder has the Lowest Calculated Bid or Highest Rated Bid.
Carefully verify, validate, and ascertain all statements made in the documents submitted by the bidder, and shall immediately notify the said bidder in writing of its post-disqualification and the grounds for it, if any, to declare as eligible only those who comply with the provisions of the RIRR of RA 9184.
Submit justification why the contractor was awarded with five (2) new contracts despite having negative slippage of its previous contracts with the Municipality.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor, through the Local Development Council (LDC) and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to prioritize the planning and approval of PPAs that are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready and include only those PPAs in the AIP that are determined priority projects to avoid delays in the implementation due to changing of PPAs and realignment of funds.
We also recommended to the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Budget Officer and the MPDC to include in the annual budget preparation, the review of continuing appropriation unexpended balances and those PPAs that cannot be implemented, so that these could be reverted to the unappropriated surplus and re-appropriated to other priority PPAs embodied in the Annual Investment Program to prevent idle fund.
We further recommended the Municipal Accountant to prepare a report on the unexpended balances of the prior years’ completed projects that have been accumulated in the Bank Account to be reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund. Henceforth, maintain the Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligation (RAAO) for the 20% DF including the Continuing Appropriations for monitoring and for timely reversion to avoid idle of funds.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Engineer ensure that projects are meticulously and judiciously planned and monitor compliance of the contractor to the terms of the contract and accomplishment based on the approved plans and specifications, and any variation shall be authorized through an approved Variation Order, either a Change Order or Extra Work Order, whichever is applicable.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor through the Municipal Assessor establish that the Seller is the true owner of the lot and the appraisal of the value of the lot to be purchased, by requiring the seller to submit the pertinent documents prescribed under the COA Circular No. 2012-001 before entering into a contract of sale.
We likewise recommended that for the purchase of the untitled lot, require the lot owner to post an indemnity or property bond, the amount of which shall be fixed by the Provincial/City/Municipal Treasurer concerned based on the market value as determined by the assessor and as appearing on the current tax declaration of the property.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant comply with the documentary requirements set under COA Circular No. 2012-001 and conduct a pre-audit in the processing of claims and review of supporting documents to determine the completeness of the requirements before effecting payment.
Finally, we recommended to submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Health Officer:
i)\tissue a Special Order covering the assignment of public health workers to hazardous/difficult areas,
ii)\tsecure Certification by the Secretary of the DOH that the place of assignment/travel is a strife-torn/embattled/disease-infested/distress or isolated areas/stations, or areas declared under state of calamity or emergency, or with volcano activity or eruption, and
iii)\tattach the complete supporting documents to the disbursement vouchers.
We likewise recommended the Municipal Accountant certify disbursement vouchers that are supported with complete documentation and strictly observe the guidelines on the grant of hazard pay, subsistence and laundry allowances.
We recommended for the refund of the overpayment received.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) that in the preparation of the Annual Investment Plan (AIP), conduct a thorough review of the Peace and Order and Public Safety (POPS) Plan of the LGU and ensure that the PPAs to be included in the AIP are aligned with the priorities outlined in the POPS Plan and duly supported by the Peace and Order Plan.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the Local Peace and Order Councils include only those PPAs that are directly related to crime prevention and law enforcement activities, capability development for personnel of law enforcement agencies, volunteers/partners, or programs for anti-illegal drug, illegal gambling, counter-insurgency, and/or counter-terrorism efforts, among others that will directly contribute to the maintenance of the peace and order and public safety.

We recommended that the Management directs the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting entries to remove from the Inventory accounts the items already issued to end-users; and the Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer prepare and maintain the prescribed forms, registries and reports mentioned in COA Circular No. 2022-004 to strengthen controls over the semi-expendable properties; and to segregate semi-expendable properties as low-valued and high-valued items.

We recommended that the Accounting Office draws the necessary adjusting journal entries to reflect the correct amount of the affected accounts, use appropriate accounts for recording their transactions, and properly observe the substance over form policy to ensure the accuracy and reliability of reported balances in the Financial Statements at year-end.

We recommended that the Accounting Office draw the necessary adjusting journal entries to reflect the correct amount of the affected accounts and use appropriate accounts in the ensuing year for recording their payment of bonuses to ensure the accuracy and reliability of reported balances in the Financial Statements as at year-end.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant, together with the Municipal Assessor, to verify their historical data or records that could shed light on the acquisition costs of the properties and, if available, make an assessment so that the necessary adjusting entries shall be made to record all land owned by the Municipality, in consonance with Section 111 of Presidential Decree 1445 and International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) No. 1 and 17.

We recommended that the Accounting Office drop the amount of the distributed donated carabaos in the LGU’s books of accounts and draw adjusting journal entries to reflect the correct balance of the affected account in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Management reminds the Municipal Accountant to record in the books of accounts all purchases of supplies, whether or not they are consumed within the accounting period, as Inventory while its issuances are recorded as expenses, with RIS and SSMI as support documents. Further, a supplies ledger card (SLC) for each item of supplies and/or other inventory items shall be maintained to record the acquisition, receipt, and issuance pursuant to the Manual on the NGAS; and the GSO to consolidate the issuances, supported with RIS, on a monthly basis in the SSMI to be submitted to the Municipal Accountant, who shall compute the cost of supplies issued and ending inventory using the moving average method as prescribed under Section 121 of the same Manual.

We recommended that the GSO maintain PPE Cards, and the Accounting Office prepare and maintain PPE Ledger Cards and ensure that reconciliation of their records is done before the preparation of the annual financial statements; and that the Municipal Accountant, Property Custodian, and Inventory Committee to complete the reconciliation of the RPCPPE with the Accounting Office records to validate the accuracy of the PPEs.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive create an Inventory Committee to conduct an annual physical count of local road networks and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network not later that January 31 of each year, requiring the Municipal Accountant to present in the Notes to Financial Statements the required disclosure for local road networks, and to prepare and maintain Local Road Network Ledger Cards for each local road; and require the General Services Officer to prepare the annual Report of Local Road Network, and to prepare and maintain the Local Road Network Property Cards for each local road.

We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Accountant to monitor and perform constant review and appraisal of transactions to deter errors and omissions and to reconcile accounts and effect adjustments to reflect correct balances of Due from/to Other Funds and Due from/to Special Accounts in the financial statements, pursuant to Section 111(2) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

We recommended that the Management institute action against the defaulting NGOs/POs pursuant to the provisions of the COA Circular 2007-001; the Accounting Office send demand letters to NGOs/POs and requesting them to submit the Final Utilization Report together with the inspection report and certificate of project completion, and the list of beneficiaries with their acceptance/acknowledgment of the project/fund/goods/ service received.

We recommended that Management ensure that realignment/reprogramming is always supported by an appropriation ordinance and strictly adhere to Section 336 of RA 7160 to ensure the validity and legality of the newly programmed PPAs and their implementation.

We recommended that the Management require the LDRRM Council to prepare or propose programs that are achievable and take into consideration the actual and/or real needs of their constituents to ensure full utilization of the fund and effective implementation of the corresponding programs, projects, and activities.

We recommended that the Management require the LDRRM Officer not to release stockpiling/prepositioning items to ineligible beneficiaries and remind them that such items will only be released during emergency cases or already stored for a long time but before their expiration dates, supported with proper documentation.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Treasurer not to use the collections for the vales and cash advances, to immediately deposit all the undeposited collections to the depository bank, to allot sufficient budget for the traveling expenses, and to instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to monitor regularly the intact and timely deposit of collections by the Municipal Treasury Office to avoid repetition of the same deficiency.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly enforce and adhere to the existing regulation on collection remittances and reporting to safeguard the funds against risks of loss, theft, and misappropriation and to reflect the correct income and cash balances in the financial statements. Moreover, oblige the erring collector to restore/refund the embezzled amount, if any.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant thoroughly review the supporting documents in all transactions before certifying the completeness and propriety thereof. Also, allot enough budget to process the requirements to transfer the ownership of procured lots by prioritizing the transfer of the name in the tax declaration.

We recommended that the Management strictly comply with the provisions set forth under Section 2 of PD 1445 and the applicable provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184, as cited above; require the contractors to expedite the completion of the above infrastructure projects; and require the Municipal Engineering Office to closely monitor the work accomplishment of all the contractors with projects that have “ongoing” or “not implemented” status.

We recommended that the Management reminds the Municipal Accountant and accountable officers to observe strictly the submission of Statement of Receipts and Disbursements, Report of Checks Issued, Report of Disbursements together with the vouchers and their complete supporting documents to the Implementing Agency’s Auditor within the required timeframe as provided for in COA Circular No. 94-013; and submit the required documents and/or other reports to the Source Agency for liquidation.

We recommended that the Management make use of the free accounting system provided by the Commission on Audit by writing a letter of intent addressed to the Commission on Audit and accomplishing the Technical Requirements Checklist for an efficient and effective computerized accounting system that will generate a more reliable and accurate balance of accounts. Moreover, allot enough budget for the related expenses in the implementation of the said system.

We recommended and management agreed to instruct the Human Resource personnel to strictly monitor the procedure of the taking of leave of absences by officials and employees. Likewise, to work back and reconcile the vacation and sick leave credits of the employees to determine the correct balance of their leave credits to avoid existence of negative leave balances and errors in the payment of terminal leave benefits of officials and employees.
observations.managementAction: Management had issued a memorandum for the strict compliance of the filing of an application for leave. The HR personnel already updated the employee ledger cards to reflect the correct balances.
observations.reason: Based on the logbook presented for the employee leave credit record, it showed that the corrected leave credit balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the GSO to issue the Property Acknowledge Receipt of the identified service Vehicle to the proper Department Head, instead of their respective staff, in order to establish proper accountability over the said property. We further recommended that management stop granting Travel Allowance to department heads whose office was assigned with service vehicle.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive (LCE) had designated a Building Official (BO) apart from the Municipal Engineer. A new Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR) was issued to the newly designated BO for the service vehicle under the office of the BO.
observations.reason: Our validation proved the stated management action.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for management to put the Sanitary Landfill in operation in order to optimize the full benefit of the said facility and to unburden the different LGUs in the disposal of various residual and special wastes.
observations.managementAction: The sanitary landfill is now operational.
observations.reason: Inspection of the site showed that the LGU is already in use of their sanitary landfill.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the excess honoraria in the total amount of ₱767.50 be refunded by the concerned personnel.
observations.managementAction: Management required the accountable officer to refund the excess payment of honoraria.
observations.reason: Official Receipt No. 6959383   dated May 16, 2024 was presented during the exit conference for the refunded excess payment worth ₱767.50.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to determine all items included in the PPE that do not meet the capitalization threshold and reclassify the same to the appropriate semi-expendable inventory account if not yet issued or close it to the Government Equity account if already issued to the end-user.
observations.managementAction: Complied

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct either the BAC Chairman or Municipal Engineer to require the awarded contractor to apply for approval of the CSHP of the project from DOLE upon the award of contract. The Municipal Engineer must also see to it that appropriate safety measures are employed by the contractor so as to ensure protection and welfare of workers and the general public from any untoward incident that may occur during the implementation of the project.
observations.managementAction: Complied.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly enforce direct payments to suppliers/dealers in the procurement of goods in observance of sound internal control on the disbursement process and to ensure the validity of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: Complied.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Present commitments under the “Actual Amounts” of the SCBAA on the period the commitments or award of contract were made.  Since the relevant expense is not yet recognized as at year-end, the commitments will be a reconciling item in the Reconciliation Statement between Statement of Financial Performance and SCBAA to be presented in the Notes to Financial Statements;

Exclude in the current year SCBAA all prior year’s commitments which were only consummated during the year; the difference of the expense and actual amounts are presented as “Reconciling Items”; and

Exclude the consummated commitments as utilized amount of the current year LDRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction:
Page 55
of AAR 2022 | The Municipal Accountant presented commitments under the Actual Amounts in the year the commitment was made and present it as a reconciling item if not yet consummated as of year-end.



The Municipal Accountant vouched compliance with the audit recommendation.



The Municipal Accountant committed to comply with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to deduct the exact amount of personal contributions and loans from the salaries of the employees and officials and the basis thereof of the remittance to be made to the appropriate financial institutions or government agencies.
observations.managementAction:
Page 57
of AAR 2022 | Management reiterated to their employees of their responsibilities to communicate to the HR-designate the availment of new loans.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to stop funding the payment of honoraria out of the regular appropriations of the LGU, instead it should be sourced from the sale of bidding documents, fees from contractors/suppliers, fees charged for copies of minutes of bid openings, BAC resolutions and other BAC documents, and from the agency’s savings subject to the limitations and provisions of the Local Government Code.
observations.managementAction:

of AAR 2022 | The Municipal Mayor ordered to stop funding payment of honoraria out of regular appropriation.

observations.recommendation:
We commended the Municipal Mayor and all personnel concerned in ensuring that identified development projects and utilizations charged to the Development Fund were in adherence to the provisions of Item 3.2.5 of DBM- DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1, dated November 4, 2020.

We likewise recommended the Management to continually make a conscious effort to accomplish the identified development projects efficiently and effectively in accordance with rules and regulations.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to:

X x x
X x x
X x x
X x x
Cause the reduction of number of job- order workers to accommodate the increase of daily rate pursuant to the minimum wage required under Wage Order No. RB VIII-21.

We recommended that the Accounting Office properly and religiously monitor all the records of the LGU to ensure the accuracy and reliability of the reported balances in the Financial Statements and make any necessary adjusting journal entries to reflect the correct amount of the affected accounts.

We recommended that the General Services Officer conduct a physical count of all properties received by the Municipality through donation and furnish the accountant with the inventory list and pertinent donation papers as the basis of the latter in the recording of the donated assets in the books of accounts, and coordinates with the Municipal Assessor’s Office to conduct an appraisal of the donated properties to reflect its fair value measurement in compliance with IPSAS 23. Also for Municipal Accountant prepares a Journal Entry Voucher to record the donated vehicles in the books for proper presentation in the financial statements and ensures that donated assets in the ensuing period, if any, with the Municipality as Donee are properly recognized in the books of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare a Schedule of Unreleased Checks at the end of the year, and immediately submit the said schedule to the Municipal Accounting Office; and the Municipal Accountant draws adjusting journal entries in the books of accounts every year-end for the total amount of unreleased checks, to fairly present the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to submit all Bank Reconciliation Statements, duly reconciled with the Trial Balance and record/adjust necessary reconciling items so that the accuracy and reliability of the CIB account can be validated.

We recommended that the Management stop the practice of charging the 20% development fund expenses which are not among the allowable expenditures as provided under Section 3.0 of DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 01-2020 dated November 4, 2020. The LGU officials should strictly adhere to the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1-2020 of the DBM, DOF, and DILG in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.

We recommended that the Management refrain from using the LDRRMF for payment of administrative costs and strictly utilize the 5% LDRRMF only for disaster risk management activities as provided in the above guidelines to ensure that the objectives outlined in the allocation of the fund are achieved thru proper usage thereof.

We recommended that the Management stop the practice of splitting the procurement of various goods and supplies and consolidate the procurement of similar and related items into one purchase request and subject the same to competitive bidding or its most appropriate method of procurement; and require the end-user for purchases beyond the threshold amount on shopping and small value procurements be done through competitive bidding.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer stop charging expenses without appropriations and over the allotted budget, and regularly monitor the spending against a specific appropriation to avoid the incurrence of expenses above the budget. Also, ensure that the augmentations of appropriation are fully supported with proper documentation.

We recommended that the Management instructs the Municipal Accountant to stop the payment of honorarium to the concerned officers and personnel and strictly adhere to the guidelines set forth by the Department of Budget and Management implementing certain provisions of RA 7160.
We recommended that the Management refrains from granting honorarium or other forms of compensation to any government official or employees except those specifically authorized by law.

We recommended that the Management pay the unpaid cash gift and a year-end bonus of the four elected officials and one employee totaling ₱323,579.90, allot the budget for the monetary benefits of the elected officials during their terms and, Strictly adheres to the provisions of DBM Budget Circular No. 2016-4 dated April 28, 2016, and BMEA Resolution No. 001 series of 2022 dated November 23, 2022, in granting the cash gift, year-end bonus, and CNA Incentive.

We recommended the following, pertinent documents should be prepared by the Disposal Committee to facilitate appraisal review of the unserviceable properties by the COA, including: a properly accomplished Inventory and Inspection Report for unserviceable properties, appraisal Report of unserviceable properties for disposal; and other related documents such as photographs of unserviceable properties for disposal; and file the application for disposal with the COA, pursuant to COA Circular 92-386.

We recommended that the Accounting Office strengthen the internal control system in the Municipal Accounting Office, particularly in the review of computation of taxes from compensation, submit a report to BIR on the over-withheld taxes from compensation and request if a refund can still be made to the affected employees through a monthly deduction from the remittable amount of taxes per month; and collect from the concerned personnel the under-withheld taxes from compensation and immediately remit the same to the BIR to avoid penalties

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepares a Schedule of Unreleased Checks at the end of the year, and immediately submits the said schedule to the Municipal Accounting Office; and the Municipal Accountant draws adjusting journal entries in the books of accounts every year-end for the total amount of unreleased checks, to fairly present the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Accounting Office properly monitors the records of accounts to ensure the accuracy and reliability of reported balances in the Financial Statements and make the necessary adjusting journal entry to reflect the correct amount of the affected accounts.

We recommended that the Accounting Office closely monitor the liquidations of the fund transfers received by the LGU to ensure the accuracy and validity of reported balances in the Financial Statements of Due to National Agencies account and make the necessary adjusting journal entry to reflect the correct amount of the affected accounts.

We recommended that the Accounting Office properly monitors the records of accounts to ensure the accuracy and reliability of reported balances in the Financial Statements and make the necessary adjusting journal entry to reflect the correct amount of the affected accounts.

If such efforts exerted are futile, set up an allowance for impairment on the receivable accounts. observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require: a) The Inventory Committee to complete the conduct of physical count of PPE and attach property tag/sticker for easy identification of government property and ensure its proper usage; reconcile the inventory report with the accounting and property records and adjust records based on valid documents to reflect the correct balances of the PPE accounts. Thereafter, submit the corresponding RPCPPE to the Auditor for verification on the accuracy and existence of the reported PPE. b) The Municipal Accounting Office to maintain complete PPE ledger cards and continue tracing back the details of prior years’ acquisitions to support the recorded PPE. c) The Municipal Treasurer to maintain property cards to facilitate the physical count of PPE items. Determine the end-user officials/employees and issue the corresponding PAR to pinpoint the person responsible/accountable thereof. Moreover, renew all PAR every three years to update the information of the end-user of the equipment listed in the RPCPPE. d) If the existence of PPE in previous years could not be established despite exhaustion of all diligent efforts, Management follow the procedures on the disposition for non-existing PPE items for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances of government agencies as provided under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
observations.managementAction: Members of the Inventory Committee have conflicting schedule and the volume of PPE that needed to be inventoried was overwhelming. PPELCs are being maintained for PPE items which were purchased or constructed starting CY 2021. Complete PPELCs was not maintained for PPEs that were purchased or constructed in prior years. No action taken by Management. Property cards were still not maintained. The Municipality has not yet utilized the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances to establish existence of the PPE items.
observations.reason: The Inventory Committee did not conduct a complete physical count of PPE. PPELCs are being maintained for items purchased or constructed starting CY 2021. Complete PPELCs was not maintained for PPEs that were purchased or constructed in prior years. Property cards were still not maintained. No action taken by Management. The Municipality has not yet utilized the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances to establish existence of the PPE items.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management render a written justification on the non-implementation of the programs/ projects in accordance with the scheduled of the report. observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Engineer to prepare the necessary documents detailing the information relative to replaced parts of road networks and furnish the same to the Municipal Accountant to serve as her basis in determining the appropriate carrying amount of replaced parts of local road networks to be derecognized from the books in order to present fairly the Road Networks account in the financial statements.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to determine all items included in the PPE that do not meet the capitalization threshold and reclassify the same to the appropriate semi-expendable inventory account if not yet issued or close it to the Government Equity account if already issued to the end-user.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to provide allowance for impairment on the dormant and unsubstantiated receivables as provided under IPSAS 29 to present the correct net realizable value of the receivable accounts. After which, comply with the conditions and requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 for the request for write-off of the identified dormant and unsubstantiated receivables, thus receivable accounts would be fairly presented in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Present commitments under the “Actual Amounts” of the SCBAA on the period the commitments or award of contract were made.  Since the relevant expense is not yet recognized as at year-end, the commitments will be a reconciling item in the Reconciliation Statement between Statement of Financial Performance and SCBAA to be presented in the Notes to Financial Statements;
Exclude in the current year SCBAA all prior year’s commitments which were only consummated during the year; the difference of the expense and actual amounts are presented as “Reconciling Items”; and
Exclude the consummated commitments as utilized amount of the current year LDRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to deduct the exact amount of personal contributions and loans from the salaries of the employees and officials and the basis thereof of the remittance to be made to the appropriate financial institutions or government agencies.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer, in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and with assistance of the different department heads involved with the proposed infrastructure project to conduct detailed engineering to ensure optimal utilization of funds and avoid inclusion of projects prone to discontinuance caused by inadequate procurement planning.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to stop funding the payment of honoraria out of the regular appropriations of the LGU, instead it should be sourced from the sale of bidding documents, fees from contractors/suppliers, fees charged for copies of minutes of bid openings, BAC resolutions and other BAC documents, and from the agency’s savings subject to the limitations and provisions of the Local Government Code.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, being the Head of the Procuring Entity, direct the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant to require the contractor to submit standby letter of credit of equivalent value from a commercial bank, a bank guarantee or a surety bond before effecting payment to protect the interest of the government in case the contractor default on the agreement.
Thereafter, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to process claims on advance payment only upon submission to and acceptance by the procuring entity of an irrevocable standby letter of credit, bank guarantee or a surety bond as prescribed in the above provisions of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
The Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to:
Ensure that the LDRRMP and LDRRMFIP includes projects with sufficient detail such as specific project name, location and estimated cost; and
In case of any revisions in the LDRRMP and LDRRMFIP, seek the approval of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council, Local Development Council, Local Chief Executive and Sangguniang Bayan; and
The Bids and Awards Committee and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer, to ensure availability of appropriations for the specific PPA being implemented, otherwise, request the necessary Authorization from the MDRRMC, Local Chief Executive, MDC and Sangguniang Bayan.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Instruct the Municipal Engineer and MPDC to prioritize and properly schedule the implementation of projects funded out of the share of the Municipality from the tobacco excise taxes through maximum use of all available resources so that benefits will be timely delivered to the tobacco farmers/constituents, and
Direct the Municipal Engineer to incorporate all the projects including the RA 7171 projects in the Quarterly Report on Government Projects/Projects/Activities. The report shall reflect the status of implementation of all PPAs incorporated in their Annual Investment Plan and Annual Procurement Plan in adherence to transparency and accountability as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2013-004.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MPDC, being the officer-in-charge for the CBMS operations, and the Municipal Accountant, certifying the completeness of documents, to ensure that sufficient and relevant documents are submitted before effecting payment to establish legality and validity of claims.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to stop the implementation of collecting in advance the business taxes from contractors, instead collect business taxes on or before January 20 of the subsequent year.  For outside contractors, demand payment through official letters in the subsequent year prior to deadline of payment. The Municipal Treasurer shall likewise stop the collection of business taxes from outside suppliers as there are no law allowing its enforcement.
Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to adjust the affected accounts in the financial statements with the following correcting entry:
Business Tax				₱195,152.77
Other Deferred Credits			₱195,152.77

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee to:
Ensure that the:
Purchase Requests contains the detailed specifications of the item and exclude brand name unless the items or parts are compatible with the existing fleet or equipment of the same make and brand which will maintain the performance, functionality and useful life of the equipment; and
Purchase Orders are prepared according to the Section B of COA Circular No. 96-010, specifically, the inclusion of complete detailed item specification and brand name, if any; and
Discontinue requiring the Municipal Treasurer to sign the Requests for Quotations to ensure that accountabilities in procurement and disbursement functions are properly defined.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to:
Stop the practice of using his collections to pay the municipality’s obligations on meals and snacks, AICS and emergency meetings; and
Deposit all his collections intact the full amount not later than the following banking day if possible, with the authorized depository bank to avoid holding excessive cash and to prevent possible misuse or misappropriations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to include the costs of extra work undertaken even these were waived by the contractors to recognize the actual costs of the infrastructure assets and donations in kind.
Also, instruct the Municipal Accountant to adjust the affected accounts in the financial statements with the following correcting entry:

Other Land Improvement                                  273,689.05

        a.  Applies  the  Perpetual  Inventory  System  in  accounting  for
