11.9 We recommended that the Management refrains from granting financial
        assistance  or  other  forms  of  compensation  to  any  government
        official or employees except those specifically authorized by law.

12.8    We recommended that the Management:


        1. Strictly observes the provisions of RA No. 10121 in planning and
           implementation of the projects, programs and activities  of  the
           LDRRM Fund and;


        2. Refrains from using the LDRRMF for  purposes  other  than  those
           authorized under Section 21 of RA 10121 and  Section  3  of  COA
           Circular No. 2012-002.

13.11 We recommended that the Management stops the practice of  charging
        to the 20% development  fund  expenses  which  are  not  among  the
        allowable expenditures as provided under Section  3.0  of  DBM-DOF-
        DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 01-2020 dated November 4,  2020.
        The LGU officials should strictly  adhere  to  the  guidelines  set
        under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1-2020 of the DBM, DOF and DILG
        in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to
        ensure its optimal utilization.

14.9    We recommended that the Management:


        1. Carefully plans the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) to ensure  that
           the development projects identified  are feasible and can be
           implemented within the year with the proposed budget  allocation and;


        2. Ensures that  20%  Development  Fund  and  it’s  PPAs  be  fully
           utilized and monitored in order to  immediately  address  issues
           that will be  encountered  in  the  project  implementation  and
           achieve the optimum desirable socio-economic  and  environmental
           development.

15.6    We recommended that the Management requires  the  MDRRM  Officer
        for immediate submission of Relief Distribution Sheets of  all  the
        related transactions above as proof that all available relief goods
        were actually and fully  received  by  intended  beneficiaries  and
        strictly comply to attach  all  the  supporting  documents  in the
        ensuing transactions.

16.7    We recommended that the Management  directs  those  involved  in
        planning and implementation  to  monitor  closely  and  hasten  the
        implementation of project funded by the other  government  agencies
        so that the intended recipients can enjoy the benefits  that  could
        be derived therefrom.

17.7    We recommended  that  the  Management  directs  the  responsible
        official/s  of  the  Municipality  to  submit  the  Memorandum   of
        Agreements (MOAs) of the above fund transfers. Henceforth,  in  the
        ensuing years that all  MOAs  covering  fund  transfers  should  be
        submitted to the Audit Team within  the  prescribed  period  for  a
        timely and thorough audit.

18.5. We recommended that the MDRRM Officer:


        a. Updates themselves to the  latest  policies  and  guidelines  on
           Donations In-Kind as provided  by  the  relevant  and  competent
           government offices; and


        b. Ensures the on-time preparation and submission of the Report  of
           the Donations Received, Distributed and Balances to  the  Office
           of the Civil Defense and  Auditor’s  Office,  in  compliance  to
           Section 2(b) of COA Circular 2020-009, moving forward.

19.7 We recommended that the Management directs the Municipal Accountant
        to thoroughly review the supporting documents in  all  transactions
        before certifying the completeness and propriety thereof. Moreover,
        require  the  in-charge  of  the  project/program  to  attach   the
        necessary documents as required in the claim.

20.8     We  recommended  that  the  Management  updates  the  list   of
        properties that need to be insured and  allocate  funding  for  the
        insurance coverage of at the least newly constructed  building  and
        purchased motor vehicle/s to  protect  the  same  against  loss  or
        damage which in this case by acts of nature, then the LGU can  have
        at least recover the insurable amount and minimized the risks.

21.5    We recommended that the  Local  Chief  Executive  instructs  the
        concerned officials to ensure the timely submission  of  copies  of
        government contracts and each of all  the  documents  forming  part
        thereof within five (5) working days from execution pursuant to COA
        Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12,  2009,  to  ensure  timely
        review and evaluation of the same, so  that  the  Management  could
        immediately notified of any defects/deficiencies noted thereon.

22.9 We recommended that the Local School Board is  strictly  guided  by
        the provisions of  the  above  Republic  Act  and  Circular  in  the
        preparation  of the  SEF Annual  Budget and   identify   priority
        expenditures pursuant thereto. Then, any issues or conflicts arising
        from its implementation and cases  not  covered  by  the  provisions
        shall be submitted to the DepEd for resolution and  in  consultation
        with the Municipal Government.

23.8    We recommended that the Management; (a)  requires  the  Municipal
        Accountant to verify and ascertain that payments for gasoline,  oil
        and  lubricants  are  duly  supported  with  completely   filled-up
        driver’s trip tickets for proper evaluation on  the  propriety  and
        reasonableness of fuel consumption and (b)  directs  the  concerned
        personnel to prepare and submit to the Auditor, on a monthly basis,
        the Monthly Report of Official Travels and the  Monthly  Report  of
        Fuel  Consumption  for  proper  analysis,  verification   and   the
        necessary audit action on fuel consumption.

We recommended that the Municipality Accountant prepares the Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) for all the funds/bank accounts maintained by the Municipality with the authorized depository banks every month duly reconciled with the cashbooks of the Treasury Office and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was able to prepare and submit 2021 Bank Reconciliation Statements to the Office of the Auditors.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

a. The Municipal Accountant:

• Ensures the setting up of RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year supported with a certified list from the Municipal Treasurer showing the taxpayers’ names and amounts due;

• Establish the beginning RPT/SET Receivables based on Taxpayer’s index card as prescribed under Section 20 Volume 1 of the MNGAS for LGUs;

• Makes necessary adjustment by recognizing the RPT/SET Receivables and Deferred RPT/SET Income based on the list of delinquent taxpayers; and

• Records the penalties for delinquencies to Other Receivables and credit the same to Other Deferred Credits or other appropriate accounts.

b. The Municipal Treasurer provides the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of taxpayers showing the name and the amount due and collectible for the current year as basis in setting up the beginning balance of RPT and SET Receivables.

c. The Local Chief Executive directs the Municipal Assessor, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to cooperate in generating accurate/reliable data on RPT and SET receivable balances by indicating the names of taxpayers and amounts due from them, including penalties therefrom.
observations.managementAction: The Treasury Office still did not submit the list of all taxpayers and its index card to the Accounting Office because the said documents were still not yet updated by the assigned personnel of Treasury Office.
observations.reason: The Treasury Office just updated the Certified List of the taxpayers’ names and its index card and will be submitted to the Accounting Office regularly every first month of the ensuing year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant takes up the necessary adjusting/correcting entries in the books on the advance payments of RPT and SET receivables collected in 2020. Likewise, she should follow the correct accounting entries regarding advance payments.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant commented that she only recorded what the Treasury Office has forwarded to her, but agreed and committed to implement the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office strictly observes compliance with the perpetual inventory method of accounting on the supplies and materials procured for construction projects implementation by administration by recording all purchases under the account Construction Materials Inventory. Also, coordinate with the Municipal GSO, as the construction materials custodian, to implement controls on the receipt and utilization of the same and submit to the Accounting Office a summary of the utilized construction materials. Likewise, effect the necessary adjustment for the number of supplies and materials which remained unutilized for the intended projects or repairs.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office already observed the perpetual inventory in recording all the construction materials.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

a. Requires the Municipal Engineer to provide a complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects;

b. Directs the Municipal Assessor for existing road networks to assist the Municipal Engineer in the assessment of the fair value of the road lot component of local roads under the jurisdiction of the Municipal Government; and

c. Directs the Municipal Accountant to recognize the amount pertaining to the road lot as part of the reported book value of the Road Networks account.
observations.managementAction: The inventory committee is still finalizing inventory count regarding their PPEs and the concerned offices still conducting actual valuation of the components of road networks and no report was submitted.
observations.reason: The inventory committee still did not finalize the inventory count on their PPEs, thus, the LGU will be forming a team to focus solely on the assessment and inventory of components of all the Road Networks which will be headed by the Municipal Engineer.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

a. Instructs the designated Municipal GSO to maintain property cards and prepare property inventory tagging for each property;

b. Instructs the Municipal Accountant to train one of her staff who will be tasked to maintain the required PPE Ledger Card; and

c. Reactivates the Inventory Committee and assure its commitment to religiously exhaust all available resources and conduct the required annual physical count of plant, property and equipment duly documented through incorporating in the Report on Physical Count on Property, Plant and Equipment.
observations.managementAction: Conduct of physical inventory count is still on-going.
observations.reason: Reiterated in AAR 2021 Finding No. 4

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

a. Ensures that request for relief from accountability be filed properly and promptly by the accountable officer/s concerned. After which, effect the necessary dropping of dead animals in the books with proper documentation; and

b. Conducts actual physical inventory count of breeding stocks regularly and submit the prepared report to the Municipal Accountant and copy furnish the Office of Auditor to verify the existence and condition of the same.
observations.managementAction: Recommendations were properly observed and taken.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Accounting Office establish account balances at the time the payment was made and accepted by the payees not when the check was drawn and make an adjusting journal entry to reflect the correct amount of the affected accounts.

For the current year’s erroneous entry, we suggest the below correcting entry:

Gov’t Equity    ₱689,734.69
Due to GSIS       ₱234,504.77
Due to Pag-ibig    417,948.72
Due to Philhealth   37,281.20
observations.managementAction: Recommendations were properly observed and taken.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

Requires the HRMO to monitor the accumulated leave credits of the Master List of the LGU and to furnish a copy to the Accounting Office; After ensuring the reliability of the accumulated leave credit balances of employees as of December 31, 2020, requires the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments to record the Accrued Terminal Leave Benefits due to the retired officials and employees; and

TLB (current year)  xxx
G.E. (prior years)    xxx
LBP                xxx

Directs the MBO to annually appropriate for the accrued terminal leave benefits earned by retiring officials and employees even on a staggered basis depending on the availability of funds.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant was able to set-up accrued terminal leave of all unpaid retired officials and employees of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer establish a strong internal control in the remittance of the collections, and the Municipal Accountant to recognize the income on the exact year when the revenue is collected (when the revenue is due in case of RPT) to avoid misleading information in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: Recommendations were properly observed and taken.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

a. Be more cautious in booking entries to minimize errors and prevent misinformation to third-party users.

b. Adopts Perpetual Inventory System in accounting for inventories.

c. Henceforth, strict observance of the GAM for LGU and other regulations in the proper keeping of accounts is enjoined.
observations.managementAction: Recommendations were properly observed and taken.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

a. Directs the Municipal GSO-Designate to facilitate the maintenance of stock cards for supplies to account for the receipt and disposition of the same and prepare Requisition and Issue Slip for issuance and the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued monthly and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant for recording purposes to fairly present its inventory accounts in the financial statement;

b. Instructs the Municipal Accountant to observe proper adjustment of inventory accounts, prepare supplies ledger card per stock number and make an internal control policy on the maintenance of stock cards of the MDRRMO, MTO and MHO to establish authority on the control of inventory and prepare and submit the distribution lists to the Municipal Accounting Office monthly for recognition of expense; and

c. Reviews and revitalizes the composition of the Inventory Committee and fuel them up to religiously conduct the required annual physical count of unused/undistributed supplies and materials.
observations.managementAction: The LGU is still unable to maintain stock cards for all supplies but they were able to prepare a RIS for all their issuance. They assured that the material supplies inventory item shall be supported with their corresponding stock cards for the ensuing year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

a. Submits justification or explanation why the reprogramming of appropriations was not covered by an approved ordinance covering a supplemental budget; and

b. Ensures that reprogramming of PPAs is done by way of appropriation ordinance covering a supplemental budget and duly submitted to the Sangguniang Panlalawigan for review.
observations.managementAction: Recommendations were properly observed and taken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management utilizes the amount transferred in the special trust fund and should be supported with an approved plan aside from the regular LDRRMFIP of the municipality and the identified programs, projects and activities should prioritize the welfare and safety of its constituents especially during emergency cases.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO properly prepared a separate plan solely for the unexpended LDRRM Fund.
Recommendations were properly observed and taken.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the MDRRM Officer:

a. Updates themselves to the latest policies and guidelines on Donations In-Kind as provided by the relevant and competent government offices; and

b. Ensures the on-time preparation and submission of the Report of the Donations Received, Distributed and Balances to the Office of the Civil Defense and Auditor’s Office, in compliance to Section 2(b) of COA Circular 2020-009, moving forward.
observations.managementAction: Recommendations were properly observed and taken.
Recommendations were properly observed and taken.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

a. Requires the BAC Secretariat to promote transparency in government procurement by ensuring that the Notices of Award and Purchase Orders for future procurements are posted in accordance with the guidelines prescribed.

b. Capacitates the Members of the BAC Secretariat on the laws, rules and regulations on government procurement and;

c. Requires the former BAC Secretariat to ensure compliance with the GPPB Online Portal's posting requirement.
observations.managementAction: The LGU designated a certain personnel who will take in charge in posting the Notice of Award and Contract in the PhilGEPS.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

a. Submits the lacking documents enumerated in Annex A in accordance with the GPPB prescribed forms under the Philippine Bidding Documents, the Revised IRR of RA 9184, and COA Circular 2009-001 in order to facilitate technical review and evaluation;

b. Requires the personnel in-charge in the submission of the contracts and its supporting documents to maintain a checklist of required documents for each of the transactions to serve as a guide to ensure the completeness of the documents submitted to COA for review; and

c. Strictly observes and executes the duties of the BAC and TWG as stipulated in the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: The BAC already submitted all the lacking supporting documents.
The LGU designated a certain personnel who will take in charge in posting the Notice of Award and Contract in the PhilGEPS.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

a. Stops the practice of splitting the procurement of various goods and services;

b. Stops referring to brand names in preparing Purchase Requests (PRs). Instead, mention the relevant specifications and/or performance requirements of the supplies/materials needed; and

c. Instructs the General Services Officer or BAC Secretariat, as the case maybe, to consolidate the procurement of similar and related items into one purchase request and subject the same to competitive bidding pursuant to Rule IV, Section 10 of the revised IRR of RA 9184 to ensure competitiveness and obtain the lowest and most advantageous price.
observations.managementAction: The GSO properly observed and recommendations were well taken.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

a. Submits immediately the identified 239 DVs and its related supporting documents, for a corresponding Notice of Suspension or Disallowance for the amount involved will be issued for non-submission thereof.

b. Refrains from processing DVs with incomplete documentation to ensure that all payments made are adequately supported and valid expenses. and;

c. Requires the Accounting Department to maintain Logbook for all out-going and incoming documents for control purposes to ensure that all documents of the agency will be properly safeguarded against possible loss and unauthorized access.
observations.managementAction: All the lacking disbursements vouchers were submitted to the Office of the Auditors.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

a. Directs the Municipal Accountant to scrutinize carefully the disbursement claims of the leagues of local elective officials for membership dues and contribution and determine if the rates/amounts claimed are strictly in accordance with their respective constitution and by-laws.

b. Instructs the Municipal Treasurer not to release payments to leagues unless commitment letter was attached to the supporting documents and;

c. Requires leagues annual accomplish reports, as a prerequisite for the renewal of membership fees in the ensuing years, to ascertain that government funds have been utilized for lawful and exclusively for public purposes.
observations.managementAction: Recommendations were properly observed and taken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management stops the disbursements without an appropriation and Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) should see to it that proper budget monitoring of each fund is carefully done to avoid and stop the said practices.
observations.managementAction: Recommendations were properly observed and taken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management instructs the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the concerned offices in ensuring that local fees and charges are updated regularly, with the end view of increasing the revenue collections of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The LGU already updated their local revenue code.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

a. Strictly implements actions to strengthen its collection and monitoring procedures; and

b. Directs Local Finance Committee to undertake careful planning and execution of the proposed annual budget on the basis of reasonable and realistic projection of revenues and conduct an analysis of identified revenue accounts with decreasing trend in collection.
observations.managementAction: Annual Budget was well prepared and planned by the Local Finance Committee.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GFPS submits justification or explanation why the reprogramming of appropriations was not covered by an approved ordinance, original and revised GPB should be duly approved and reviewed by the appropriate higher authority and refrain from utilizing the GAD Fund for expenses not within the approved GAD Plan and Budget.
observations.managementAction: All changes in annual budget will be supported not only by resolution but also with an approved ordinance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management directs the GFPS to properly plan and regularly monitor the implementation of GAD PPAs and maximize the utilization of the GAD budget to achieve the objective of the government regarding the institutionalization of GAD in the community.
observations.managementAction: Recommendations were properly observed and taken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management directs the Accounting Office to install a scheme to facilitate the timely remittance of withheld amount of different government institutions and should strictly comply with law so as not to deprive the officials and employees of further benefits and to avoid the burden of any surcharges and interests not due to them.
observations.managementAction: All withheld remittances were fully remitted within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to allocate funds and designate or assign a focal person/official responsible to facilitate the processing of the application for land title with the Land Registration Authority. Necessary actions should be taken to transfer the title of the lot under the name of the LGU to establish the validity and propriety on the acquisition and to avoid the risk of facing future legal issues which may cause additional expense to the government.
observations.managementAction: All lots in the island subject for titled were halted by the Land Registration Authority thus the LGU is still waiting from the development of this issue. The LGU is already gathering all necessary supporting documents for the processing of the application for land title.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to comply with DBM Circular
No. 2004-05A and use the number of projects awarded as the basis of
computing the honoraria of the BAC, TWG and BAC Secretariat, to avoid
erroneous determination of such expenditure. We further recommended that
the excess honoraria in the total amount of P767.50 be refunded by the
concerned personnel.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal School Board
to stop utilizing the SEF on programs and projects not included in the provisions
under RA No. 7160 in order to maximize the benefits that the students and
teachers could avail from this fund.

We recommended that Management stop using the prior years’ appropriation
of MOOE for current year operations and revert the same to the
unappropriated surplus of the general fund in order to avoid erroneous
recognition and utilization of government funds.

We recommended that Management comply with the requirements of
localizing the Safe Spaces Act of 2019 to adopt measures localizing the
applicability and ensure the enforcement of the law, in order to further
promote the general welfare of the people.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer’s
Office collect the fees embodied in the Municipal Revenue Code and establish
strong coordination with the Agriculture Office for immediate identification
whom to collect.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Engineer to update and furnish the Municipal Accountant with list of completed infrastructure projects together with the necessary documents including the Status of Work Accomplished (SWA) and the Certificate of Completion (COC) as basis of the latter in recording the PPE and AP as of year-end to ensure completeness of the recorded balances of the affected accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the following journal entries:
To correct the balances of the Due to Other Funds and Trust Liabilities-DRRMF:
Dr.	Due to Other Funds					xxx
Cr.	Trust Liabilities-DRRMF					xxx
To reverse the transfer of excess amount:
Dr.	Due to Other Funds					xxx
Cr.	Transfer of Unspent CY DRRMF to TF				xxx
To record the transfer of excess amount:
Dr.	Cash in Bank - LCCA						xxx
Cr.	Due to Other Funds						xxx
We further recommended that Management observe the proper accounting entries in the different transactions relating to the LDRRMF as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort to locate records of these receivables. If such efforts exerted are futile, set up an allowance for impairment on the receivable accounts. After which, Management file a request for authority to write-off said accounts, except the accounts Receivables-Disallowances/Charges and Other Receivables arising from cash shortage, to the COA Audit Team Leader and/or Supervising Auditor. The request shall be supported with the applicable documents enumerated in Section 8.3 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the LCE require:
The Inventory Committee to complete the conduct of physical count of PPE and attach property tag/sticker for easy identification of government property and ensure its proper usage; reconcile the inventory report with the accounting and property records and adjust records based on valid documents to reflect the correct balances of the PPE accounts. Thereafter, submit the corresponding RPCPPE to the Auditor for verification on the accuracy and existence of the reported PPE.
The Municipal Accounting Office to maintain complete PPE ledger cards and continue tracing back the details of prior years’ acquisitions to support the recorded PPE.
The Municipal Treasurer to maintain property cards to facilitate the physical count of PPE items. Determine the end-user officials/employees and issue the corresponding PAR to pinpoint the person responsible/accountable thereof. Moreover, renew all PAR every three years to update the information of the end-user of the equipment listed in the RPCPPE.
If the existence of PPE in previous years could not be established despite exhaustion of all diligent efforts, Management follow the procedures on the disposition for non-existing PPE items for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances of government agencies as provided under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Treasurer to refund in his personal capacity the bank charges amounting to ₱75,519.37 to preclude the issuance of Notice of Disallowance. Further, exercise due diligence in protecting government funds against loss or wastage.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the BAC to comply with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 regarding the submission of copies of perfected contracts and purchase orders with their supporting documents to the Auditor within five working days from execution and issuance thereof for review and evaluation so that management will be immediately informed of defects and deficiencies noted, if any and to facilitate the inspection of completed projects and deliveries of goods.

We recommended that Management render a written justification on the non-implementation of the programs/projects in accordance with the scheduled implementation period and furnish the same to the Sanggunian Bayan (SB) of which the same will be embodied in the SB Resolution for the reappropriation of the funds for other priority programs/projects. Moreover, we recommended the prompt implementation of all projects/programs funded out of the tobacco excise taxes and reappropriate the savings from the previous years’ implemented programs/projects in accordance with the authorized uses as prescribed in Republic Act Nos. 7171 and 8240 in order to give the constituents, especially the tobacco farmers the early realization of benefits that could be derived therefrom.

We further recommended that the LCE advise the Local Development Council to ensure that only feasible programs/projects/activities are included in the Annual Investment Program and require regular monitoring of implementation as basis in taking appropriate action.

We recommended that the Management exhaust all available remedies provided under the laws, rules and regulations in enforcing the immediate compliance to the audit suspension as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2009-006 to avoid maturing of the same to disallowance.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the  Municipal
        Accountant to strictly comply with the Revised  Chart  of  Accounts
        for Local Government Units in  recording  and  reporting  financial
        transactions of the municipality as prescribed under  COA  Circular
        No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015; and,


The Municipal Accountant take up the following correcting entries:


        Subsidy from National Government           10,276,565.80
        Subsidy from LGUs                         560,308.25
        Subsidy from GOCCs                          74,900.00
           Grants and Donations in Cash                           74,900.00
                  Grants        and        Donations        in         Kind
   10,836,874.05

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive assign personnel  who
        will be a custodian to maintain a record of inventory of  medicines
        and medical supplies and corresponding issuances to end-users.  The
        custodian should continuously maintain  a  record  for  the  issued
        medicines and supplies  to  end  users  and  prepare  the  RIS  for
        reconciliation with the SSMI  in  order  to  properly  account  the
        dispensed supplies as well as accurately present in  the  financial
        statements the  drugs  and  medicines  for  distribution,  medical,
        dental and laboratory  supplies  and  their  corresponding  expense
        accounts.
     We also recommended the following correcting entries:


        Drugs and Medicines for Distribution                 129,444.00
        Medical, Dental and Laboratory Supplies Invty         51,200.00
           Drugs and Medicines Expense                          129,444.00
           Medical, Dental and Laboratory Supplies Expense       51,200.00

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly  comply  with
        the Revised  Chart  of  Accounts  for  Local  Government  Units  in
        recording and reporting financial transactions of the  municipality
        as prescribed under COA Circular No.  2015-009  dated  December  1,
        2015 by recognizing Due to BIR account for withholding taxes  under
        SEF and Trust Fund books.  We also recommend the immediate transfer
        of funds pertaining to taxes withheld under the SEF and TF books as
        they occur.

We recommended that the  Local  Chief  Executive  ensure  that  the
        utilization of all subsequent receipt of Share  from  RA  7171  and
        8240 (if  applicable)  should  be  supported  by  a  duly  approved
        appropriation ordinance in compliance with Section4(1)  of  PD  No.
        1445 and Section 305(a) of RA No. 7160;


We also recommended that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  direct  the
        Municipal Budget Officer and concurrent acting MPDC to  review  the
        Annual Procurement Plan and confirm that all projects are  included
        in accordance with the provisions of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the GAD  Focal
        Point System  (GFPS)  to  include  additional  programs  which  are
        organization-focused and adopt Projects, Programs,  and  Activities
        which will tackle GAD-related problems arising from the pandemic to
        optimally  utilize  the  GAD  Fund  and   fully   achieve   women’s
        empowerment and gender equality amidst some restrictions.

We recommended that the Local  Chief  Executive  direct  the  Local
        School Board (LSB), through the assistance of the Municipal  Budget
        Officer, to allot the planned procurement of items with a unit cost
        of below ₱15,000.00 under MOOE budget instead of Capital Outlay.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, being  the  Chairman
        of the Local School Board:

        a) continue to convene for an early review and approval of the  SEF
           Budget;

        b) direct the  Municipal  Budget  Officer  to  assist  the  LSB  in
           reviewing  SEF  budget  to  identify   the   proper   PPAs   for
           appropriation;

        c) instruct the BAC to properly plan and schedule  all  procurement
           activities pertaining to educational projects for  their  timely
           implementation; and

        d)  direct  the  Local  School  Board  to   be   aware   of   their
           responsibilities and functions and convene regularly to  address
           issues and concerns for a better public education.

We recommended that Management:
a. Require the GSO to submit the RPCPPE, RPCLRN and RLRN for
the period ending December 31, 2021 for review of the Audit Team;
b. Instruct the GSO to maintain Local Road Network Property Card
upon completion of property records reconciliation;
c. Instruct also the Municipal Accountant to maintain Local Road
Network Ledger Card using the format provided in COA Circular
No. 2015-008;
d. Require the Municipal Accountant to keep subsidiary records for
roads and road components for every road network; and
e. Require the GSO to update all the PARs that were issued more than
three years ago and effect the transfer of accountability to the actual
custodian of the properties transferred from a retired or reassigned
official or employee.

We recommended that Management:
a. Require the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal
Assessor, to continue the review of individual taxpayer records for
the determination of all possible errors and duplications while
waiting for the requested tax mapping operations with the Provincial
Assessor’s Office;
b. Upon reconciliation of Treasury and Assessor’s records, coordinate
with the Provincial Assessor’s Office to update the individual
taxpayer’s information in Pangasinan Real Property Tax System;
and
c. Require the Municipal Treasurer to provide the Municipal
Accountant the updated RPT and SET collectibles based on the
RPTAR.

We reiterated the audit recommendation in previous years that
Management require the Municipal Assessor to fast track the gathering
of all documents and certifications necessary to secure titles or patents
over the parcels of land being exclusively used by the LGU to avoid third
party claims/land dispute and to ensure that the LGU has legal rights
over all recognized assets in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management:
a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to provide an adjusting entry to
reclassify the liabilities erroneously recorded as Accounts Payable;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to verify the subsidiary ledger of
the Accounts Payable with a negative balance for possible
overpayment or erroneous recording;
c. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to require the payees whose check
payments became stale and could no longer be found to submit their
Sworn Statements pertaining thereto, and immediately request the
bank for stop payment of the checks and request for a certification
for non-negotiation thereof;
d. Require the Municipal Accountant to further review the composition
of the Accounts Payable account to determine the claims supported
with complete and proper documentation; and
e. Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to revert all claims which are
not valid to the government equity of the General Fund and SEF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
send written notices to the payees to verify the existence of the stale checks. Replacement check may be issued upon submission of the stale check with reference to set accounting guidelines;
practice the sending of written notice to the payee at least one month before a check becomes stale; and
coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the set up an entry to record the corresponding liability for the cancellation of check and necessary adjustment/s in the books of accounts, if any.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the implementing school at year-end to refund and/or liquidate the transferred funds immediately by sending demand letters requiring the submission of the Fund Utilization/Liquidation Report to ascertain that funds granted were utilized in accordance with the purpose stated in the MOA executed to support the fund transfer.

We also recommended that management revisit the Memorandum of Agreement to expressly include a provision requiring “Monthly Status Report on Fund Balances” from the Implementing Agencies (IAs) and ensure to refund any unexpended balance at year-end to the Cashier/Collecting Officer who will issue the necessary official receipt in accordance with Item 5.8 of the COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

We reiterated our recommendation that management conduct an investigation on the status of these NGOs/POs and various individuals and discuss with the appropriate committee in the Sangguniang Bayan any remedial actions that will be undertaken to the advantage of the Municipality.

We further reiterated the following courses of action:
b.1 	Strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 on the required analysis of dormant accounts by performing the regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of the existence of the receivables and determine the concerned debtors/accountable officers for strict monitoring and disposition of said accounts.
b.2 	Determine the amount of the receivables impaired and provide reasonable allowance for impairment thereof in order to have fair presentation of the financial statements as required under IPSAS 29.
b.3 	Comply with all the conditions and requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 for the possible request for the write-off of the specified dormant receivables account to the Commission on Audit.

We recommended that the Municipal General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant verify the existence of the inventories and prepare the necessary Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI). Issue corresponding Inventory and Custodian Slip (ICS) for items being used and issued to concerned offices for monitoring, control and accountability.

We recommended that Management prepare the Physical Inventory Plan, Inventory Count Form (ICF), and to state clearly in the ICF the condition of the PPEs and the Municipal Accountant to make full-disclosure in the financial statements for the unserviceable properties included in the Property, Plant and Equipment.

Likewise, we recommended that the Property Unit prepare the List of PPEs found at station, and List of Non-existing/Missing PPEs and Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) in consideration for one-time cleansing of PPE account balances as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2020-006.

Finally, we recommended the creation of an Inventory Committee in order to come up with a reliable Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to support the fair presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the following department heads/committee to comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015:
Municipal Engineer
Continue to provide the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects as per Item VII.c.1 of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015.
Inventory Committee
Submit Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network System (RPCLRN) supported with Local Road Network Maps (Annex C format) per Item VI.3 of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015
Municipal Accountant
Submit the required copies of PPE Local Road Network Ledger Cards (Annex D format) per Item VII a.1 of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015;
Update the yearly required disclosures as needed;
Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network;
Prepare a lapsing schedule regularly for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.
Municipal General Services Officer
Maintain and update Local Road Inventory and Road Maps; and
Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Cards for all roads and its components and submit the same to the Audit Team (Annex E format) per Item VII b.2 of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015.

We recommended that the Municipal Agriculturist and the Municipal Accountant conduct a walkthrough of the LGU’s dispersal program in coordination with the Punong Barangays.  Interview those who have direct knowledge of the program and initiate tracing of any existing remnants of the cattle in order to facilitate the update of agricultural produce for distribution.  Evaluate existence of dispersed cattle and its produce for proper recording. When certain as to the existence of the dispersed cattle, conduct a physical count of inventory at least once a year and maintain record thereof and revival of the program.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant adjust the balance of inventory account and reclassify it to Breeding Stocks Account, as it deemed fit for the asset.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to determine the validity of the unpaid obligations or Accounts Payable totaling P340,785.88 which were outstanding for more than two years and if found to have no legitimate claim against the Municipality, cause the reversion of the same to the government equity.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the LGU’s records with that of the Provincial Government of Isabela to establish the correct balances of the reciprocal accounts by recognizing and disclosing the difference of P14,200.00 in the Financial Statements and in the Notes.  In case of doubts, she is recommended to make representations with the Provincial Government of Isabela to initiate reconciliation of their records.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to make representation with the LGU’s remitting branch of GSIS to facilitate reconciliation of records pertaining to the incurrence of abnormal balance in the SL of Due to GSIS account. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant shall initiate immediate adjustments to correct the SL balance of the account, and/or refund any over remittances, if established.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort to locate the documents and other records to determine the claimants and the corresponding amount, otherwise, revert those amounts to its proper accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide the corresponding depreciation expenses of all PPEs with no depreciation, using the determined estimated useful lives and straight-line method of depreciation.

We recommended that the LGU thru the Municipal Accountant make a full disclosure in the Notes to FS in the succeeding report of all donations, subsidies and transfers received and utilized related to COVID-19 outbreak and other related/similar fortuitous events in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014 and COA Circular No. 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make full-disclosure in the financial statements on the impact and overall risk of COVID-19 in LGU’s operations to be faithfully represented and be more reliable.

We recommended that the Municipality stop the practice of payments thru reimbursements instead should draw checks directly in the name of suppliers or creditors in compliance with provisions of Section 93 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and item 2.2 of COA Circular 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

We recommended that the Management support their payments made to the Legal Adviser with written conformity and acquiescence of the Solicitor General (OSG) and the written concurrence of the Commission on Audit and submit the same to the Audit Team in compliance with the provisions of Section 481 of Republic Act (RA) No. 7160 and COA Circular No. 98-002, otherwise, discontinue said payments in order to satisfy first the requirements of the circular.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer, as part of management action, justify the delays noted in the implementation of the projects together with the submission of documents such as request for time extensions, suspension and resumption orders and other related documents for further audit evaluation and Technical Audit Specialist’s reference.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer, as part of management’s action, justify the delays noted in the implementation of the projects and submit the necessary documents such as request for time extensions, suspension and resumption orders, if any, for further audit evaluation and reference.
We recommended that the Municipality compel the contractor to complete the equivalent deficient amount through work rectification otherwise, the same amount shall be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that Management through the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office and Municipal Accountant to:
Monitor the utilization of LDRRMF focusing on programs, projects and activities (PPAs) requiring the highest priority in terms of urgency and/or impact on four (4) thematic areas of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management;
Ensure efficient and effective implementation of all programmed projects identified during the budget year to optimize the utilization of the fund and to warrant that goals and objectives of reducing disaster risk and enhancing disaster preparedness and response capabilities be achieved; and
Submit monthly the Monthly Report on Utilization of LDRRM Fund to COA and DILG.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the MA to derecognize the Plants and Trees account in the FS in accordance with IPSAS 1 and RCA of GAM Volume III to fairly present the financial position of the Municipality as at year-end; and to recognize an asset only if there is future economic benefit that is expected to flow to the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Adjustment to derecognized the recorded assets have been made by the agency was Accountant adjusted to comply with the recommendation

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Engineer to include the CSHP as a separate pay item in the preparation of POW in compliance with DPWH DO No. 56 series of 2005 in relation to DOLE DO No. 13 series of 1998 to ensure the safety of workers during construction.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer had incorporated in the preparation POW of the infrastructure projects of the agency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Tourism Officer- Designate to immediately conduct a thorough evaluation on the sustainability of the Livelihood Development Program considering its profitability and productivity and thereafter submit to the LCE as basis for his decision-making.
observations.managementAction: Management had temporarily stopped the disbursement of funds for the said undertaking. Initial evaluation suggest that the activity is not operating viably.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Development Council to coordinate with the heads of implementing offices to: (1) implement the PPAs embodied in the Municipal Development Plan to pave way for the timely delivery of the benefits and/or services expected from the implementation of the PPAs; (2) revisit the plans and budget embodied in the MDF and identify a more appropriate and responsive PPAs; and (3) appropriate the savings of funds derived from the previous implementation of PPAs to new PPAs that are aligned to the purpose of MDF.
observations.managementAction: Management were able implement majority of the noted program. One project remains unimplemented

observations.recommendation: We reiterated to recommend that: a. After the inventory taking, the GSO-Designate reconciled his records with the accounting records and exert more effort in determining all discrepancies between physical and book inventories, if any. These shall be investigated, cleared and reconciled immediately.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality immediately prepare the implementing guidelines in order to facilitate the proper implementation and collection of fees embodied in the Municipal Tax Code.

observations.reason: The implementation of the audit recommendation is moot and academic because of the new ordinance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management recognize in their book of accounts and in the RPCPPE the cellular phone units availed under a postpaid plan subscription and issue a corresponding PAR to the accountable officer concerned.
observations.managementAction: These cellular phone units were included in the RPCPPE and Mobile phone units were recognized in the accounting records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. Require the Job Orders listed in Annexes C and D (of AAR 2018) to refund immediately the claimed per diems, incidental expenses and travelling allowances on their respective travels.
observations.managementAction: The concerned JOs started the refund. Partial collection were already made from concerned personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to adhere strictly to Section 350 of RA No. 7160. All lawful expenditures and obligations should be charged to the year these were incurred. Require the Municipal Accountant that all expenses at the end of the year when payment could not be effected yet to obligate the same and set up the corresponding payables on the basis of complete supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: Page 36 to 37 of the 2020 AAR | All expenditure and obligations are now being diligently checked.

Concerned employee is trying to learn how to post the bid documents in the PhilGEPS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant cause the immediate submission of the lacking documents to facilitate the technical evaluation of the projects and goods and to avoid supension in audit. observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the BAC to strictly comply with the submission of the APP-CSE in consonance with DBM Circular Letter No. 2013-14 dated November 29, 2013 to ensure the inclusion of useful information for consolidation purposes as a basis in its projection of inventory requirements, scheduling of procurement activities and overall management of the central procurement of common-use goods.
observations.managementAction: Page 45 to 46 of the 2020 AAR | APP-CSE has been prepared and submitted by the management to the DBM-PS.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

direct the Engineering Office to furnish the Accounting Office of the Municipality’s Quarterly Reports on Government PPAs to apprise the Municipal Accountant of the existence of on-going infrastructure projects;

instruct the Municipal Accountant to make sure that all on-going infrastructure projects are recognized as Construction in Progress in the Financial Statements at year-end; and 

coordinate with their contractors for them to provide progress billings/SWA which will be their basis in recognizing the on-going infrastructure projects in the Financial Statements.

Engineering Office to furnish the Quarterly Report of PPAs to the Accounting Office regularly.

Coordination between the Engineering and Accounting Office so that all on-going infrastructure projects will be recorded.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management institute and adopt a sound system of internal control in safeguarding the agency’s assets, particularly Cash through directing:

the Municipal Treasurer to prepare all the necessary documents and transact to the Branch Manager of LBP regarding the intention to avail of the bank’s ATM facility, and 

the Human Resources Management Officer (HRMO) to enroll employees for LBP automated Payroll Clearing System so that once the ATM is installed, the LGU can start using it and reduce the cash accountability of the DO which would eventually reduce or mitigate the risk of loss of government funds. 

We further recommended that the Management effect the increase of the fidelity bonds of the Municipal Treasurer commensurate to their accountabilities, in accordance with the Bureau of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009.
observations.managementAction:
Sangguniang Bayan Resolution and all the necessary documents were sent to LBP-Bauang last June 24, 2021.


LBP-Bauang informed the LGU that enrolment for automated Payroll Clearing System will be made upon approval from the LBP-Central Office of the construction of the ATM booth at the municipal premises.

Management will comply with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:

prepare, consolidate and submit the required Procurement Monitoring Report within the prescribed submission in the GPPB Circular not only to the Department of the Interior and Local Government for purposes of compliance report for the Seal of Good Local Governance but on the obligations of the agency on the full disclosure and sustainability of transparent practice on how the public funds are managed, disbursed and used; and

monitor each procurement activities specified in the Annual Procurement Plan (APP) and reflect the information in the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) as well as the timeline on all procurement activities.
observations.managementAction:
The BAC Chairman with the assistance of the BAC Secretariat will prepare the required PMR and submit the same to GPPB.










PMR monitoring board was created and regularly updated in the Office of the Municipal Engineer/BAC Chairman.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the Local Chief Executive and Solid Waste Management Board to:

prioritize the establishment of sanitary landfill by constant and consistent follow up with the concerned agencies for it to finally materialize and so that they can already gear up for the next step for its construction; 

strictly enforce waste segregation at source in order to achieve the primary objective to protect the environment and public health from substantial, present, and potential hazard of solid wastes; and

promote Solid Waste Management Awareness through proactive Information and Education Campaigns (IEC) in coordination with the Barangay Solid Waste Management Committees and academic institutions.
observations.managementAction:
Management said that they are able to identify a site for the proposed sanitary landfill at Upper Tumapoc but did not push through since there was no social acceptability regarding the construction of sanitary landfill at their place.
Waste segregation is strictly being imposed in the locality. In every barangay, there is a Materials Recovery Facility and they are advocating organic composting.

2020 AAR, Pages 31-32 In coordination with the Municipal General Services Officer, the Municipal Accountant was already able to prepare a schedule of completed projects comprising the said public infrastructure accounts; hence, depreciation cost was also provided.
observations.reason: Depreciation costs were provided for the said PPE accounts on December 31, 2021.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a. Require the Municipal Assessor to fast track the completion of tax mapping operations of the LGU to facilitate the cleansing and reconciliation of their records on RPT and SET;
b. Require the Municipal Treasurer to provide the Municipal Accountant the breakdown of all recorded RPT receivables at the beginning of CYs 2016 to 2020 for proper adjustment; and
c. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to adjust the RPT and SET Receivables according to the records provided by the Municipal Treasurer, while the reconciliation between the records of the Municipal Assessor and Municipal Treasurer is on-going in order to at least fairly present the balances of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: A request was already submitted to the Provincial Assessor’s Office for tax mapping operations. The Municipal Treasury is continuously updating the individual RPTARs. A schedule was provided by the Municipal Treasurer to the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: No action taken by the Management to adjust the RPT and SET receivables; due to the on-going reconciliation of taxpayers records, the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer are yet to determine the correct amount of RPT and SET receivables. Reiterated in Observation No. 2, page 31 of this Report.

observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office has been working on the audit recommendations since year 2021 and committed to continue tracing the subsidiary account balances back to their original entries and supporting documents to determine the validity of the claims.
observations.reason: Verification and review of the recorded transactions under Accounts Payable is still on-going; some checks became stale and payees need to submit sworn statements; reversion of invalid claims not yet made.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated the audit recommendation in previous years that Management require the Municipal Assessor to fast track the gathering of all documents and certifications necessary to secure titles or patents over the parcels of land being exclusively used by the LGU to avoid third party claims/land dispute and to ensure that the LGU has legal rights over all recognized assets in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The ICO- Municipal Assessor started coordinating with concerned agencies for the clearances and certifications needed to process the Land Titles.
observations.reason: Titling of lands will take time; coordination with different agencies for clearances and certifications needed to process Land Titles had already commenced. Reiterated in Observation No. 3, page 33 of this Report.

observations.managementAction: The MSWDO submitted the photocopies of the valid IDs of the SAP beneficiaries. Some of the names who replaced the original recipients were not found in the submitted new set of Certificates of Eligibility.
observations.reason: Some documentary deficiencies remain, e.g., some replacement beneficiaries not supported by Certificates of Eligibility; some revised payrolls not signed by recipients.

observations.managementAction: The BAC made efforts in ensuring that bid documents are properly prepared. The BAC ensured that their reports provide details on the verification made during evaluation and post-qualification. The BAC was able to prepare the said Resolutions relevant to 2021 procurement activities. Some contracts were approved beyond the prescribed 10 days upon receipt of the Notice of Award. Notices to Proceed were posted by the BAC in the PhilGEPS website within the prescribed period. The BAC did not always post the Notice of Award within the prescribed period. The Municipal Accountant submitted some of the lacking documents to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Some documents and procedures still not complied with (e.g., late contract approvals, concurrent posting of NOA and NTP); reiterated in Observation No. 6, page 39 of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a. Require the BAC to judiciously prepare and accomplish all the documents needed in the conduct of procurement including the PO and RFQ;
b. Instruct the BAC secretariat to ensure that the required PhilGEPS posting of procurement opportunities in the agency, and the Notice of Award, Notice to Proceed and contract be strictly observed; and
c. Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents to facilitate further review of said transactions.
observations.managementAction: Relative to 2021 procurements through alternative methods, the BAC properly prepared the POs and RFQs. The lacking documents were submitted by Management to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Lacking documents were submitted; BAC prepared POs and RFQs properly for 2021 procurements. However, some prior deficiencies were reiterated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer and BAC Chairman, to submit the applicable documentary requirements for each time extension granted to the contractor and all the lacking documents for the procurement and payment for further evaluation and technical review and inspection.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted most of the lacking documents to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Some documentary requirements were still not submitted to the Audit Team; thus, further verification pending.

observations.managementAction: Various revisions to the APP were still made in 2021 due to improper and inefficient planning. The BAC made use of the prescribed PPMP format. The BAC prepared and submitted some BAC Resolutions.
observations.reason: Revisions to the APP continued; BAC used prescribed PPMP format; consolidation and timely updating of APP still needs improvement. Reiterated in Observation No. 5, page 37 of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a. Direct the Municipal Engineer to require the contractors, prior to issuance of Certificates of Final Acceptance, to post/submit warranty security with sufficient amount in any acceptable form; and
b. Require the ICO-GSO to ensure that suppliers will submit a special bank guarantee before accepting the goods purchased by the LGU; otherwise, instruct the Municipal Treasurer to deduct the retention money from actual payment equivalent to at least one percent of the contract price.
observations.managementAction: The BAC and ICO-GSO implemented retention money deduction in payments to suppliers through Public Bidding.
observations.reason: Retention money was deducted by the Management from payments made to supplier of goods procured through Public Bidding.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the ICO-GSO to ensure that the AIRs are properly filled up and accomplished to fully establish the compliance of the supplier with the terms of the contract or purchase order before the approval of payment. We further recommended that Management instruct the ICO-GSO to always require the suppliers to issue delivery receipt upon delivery of the goods and sign the acceptance portion thereof as well as the AIR indicating the same date.
observations.managementAction: For 2021 transactions, Management ensured that AIRs were properly accomplished. Delivery receipts were required from suppliers upon delivery.
observations.reason: AIRs and delivery receipts were ensured for 2021 procurements; AIRs were properly filled up and accomplished.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the BAC to strictly observe and monitor the procurement process of the LGU to avoid the practice of requiring advance deliveries.
observations.managementAction: For the year 2021, the Management ensured that no goods are delivered in advance.
observations.reason: Management ensured no advance deliveries in 2021.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a. Require the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare and submit monthly the SAAOB not later than the twentieth day after the end of each month; and
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit Bank Reconciliation Statements and Pre-closing Trial Balances within ten days and twenty days after the end of each month, respectively.
observations.managementAction: Management instructed the concerned offices to ensure that monthly SAAOBs and Bank Reconciliation Statements are prepared and submitted within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: SAAOBs and Bank Reconciliation Statements are prepared and submitted within the prescribed period.

observations.managementAction: The re-evaluation of the revised 10-year SWM Plan was made in April 2022. The newly reconstituted Local Solid Waste Management Board will ensure meetings with barangay officials and other stakeholders will be undertaken. Necessary personal protective equipment and paraphernalia were procured for SWM staff. Personnel assigned as caretaker of the Landfill and his family were asked to vacate the area. Construction of a toilet facility was included in the PPAs under the SWMO.
observations.reason: Re-evaluation and actions underway; however, some measures (e.g., registration provision in agreement with junk shop, full implementation steps) still pending and reiterated in Observation No. 13, page 57 of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to continue sending notices to delinquent taxpayers and making necessary information drive campaign on the awareness of payment in advance of the real property taxes in order to encourage the taxpayers for prompt payment; otherwise, apply the remedies provided under the law in order to generate more revenue that may be appropriated for the implementation of additional programs and projects for the benefit of its constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Treasury Municipal Treasurer continued sending personnel to 14 barangays to conduct tax information dissemination campaign.
observations.reason: Treasury personnel continue information campaigns; however, the Audit Team recommends further effort in collecting delinquent taxes.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to continue sending demand letters and employ aggressive collection strategies to enforce collection of the loans especially to the borrowers related to the employees of the LGU. In case of non-collection thereof, resort to legal action in the collection of overdue receivables to avoid possible loss of government funds. We also recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to expedite the gathering of all the necessary documents to support the request for write-off of the dormant receivable account.
observations.managementAction: The Management is continuously monitoring the payments being made by the remaining borrowers. A request for write-off of receivables from deceased borrowers was submitted; Management was advised some documents still required.
observations.reason: Borrowers are paying remaining obligations except those deceased; request for write-off of some receivables was not completed due to required documents not submitted.

observations.reason: M&E Team created but M&E activities not included in GPB and supporting documents for GAD Accomplishment Report not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to require the suppliers to submit the Charge Sales Invoice for the goods procured by the LGU.
