observations.reason: Reiterated in the Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU thru the Municipal Accountant make a full disclosure in the Notes to FS in the succeeding report of all donations, subsidies and transfers received and utilized related to COVID-19 outbreak and other related/similar fortuitous events in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014 and COA Circular No. 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Accountant was not able to disclose fully in the Notes to FS the complete details on the utilization and PPAs of the subsidies and donations since these were already presented in the SAAOB, and Report of LDRRMF Utilization.
observations.reason: Reiterated in the Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU submit a one-time report on Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances, instead of monthly reports to be supported by the Acknowledgement receipts of the donations in-kind, proof of receipt by and distribution to beneficiaries, and the inventory of remaining undistributed items for verification and validation of proper valuation of balances. Copy of the report should be submitted to the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council and COA within ten (10) days after the end of each quarter.
observations.managementAction: List of Donations Received, distributed was submitted by the MSWDO and GSO to COA-ATL.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require all officials and employees responsible in the preparation of driver’s trip tickets to record the complete detail of the travels undertaken including the odometer readings before and after the trip for all vehicles of the municipality to ascertain the actual distance travelled for each motor vehicle. Implement strictly the provisions of the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles prescribed in COA Cir. No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 for effective control of gasoline, oil and lubricants expenditures. 

Likewise, ascertain its full compliance with the Local Budget Circular (LBC) No. 125 dated April 7, 2020.
observations.managementAction: Upon dialogue with the auditor as to findings, the Office of the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Office of the GSO has implemented for the completion of documents esp. trip tickets to justify the fuel expenses.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Local Sanggunian revisit the locally-enacted resolution if the same are still relevant/reasonable nowadays considering the cheaper, prevailing rates offered by the mobile networks. Otherwise, the elected/municipal officials should decrease the communication allowance to a more conscionable rate using the postpaid plan rates as the benchmarked amounts.  The principle of judicious utilization of government funds as emphasized in Section 2 of PD 1445 must be considered at all times.
observations.managementAction: The communication expenses of all the elected and key officials of the LGU was reduced to P3,000.00 from March, 2021 up to present.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer stamp “PAID” all disbursement vouchers along with all accompanying supporting documents after payment to avoid its possible re-use, thus safeguarding cash from loss thru double payments.
observations.managementAction: The office of the Municipal Treasurer complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LGU compel the contractor to complete the equivalent deficient amount through work rectification otherwise, the same amount shall be disallowed in audit.

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Engineer strictly monitor implementation of awarded projects to ensure that these projects are accomplished in accordance with the approved plans and specifications, and ensure that all projects are inspected on their scheduled completion dates so that percentage of unfinished work is determined as basis in the computation of liquidated damages to be deducted in the final billing.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer submitted all the necessary documents to give clarification on the observation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Collecting Officer in-charge provide a cut-off of her collections during the last working day of the year and allot a time to deposit all collections at the Authorized Government Depository Banks of the Municipality as prescribed under Section 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382 to avoid the exposure of the same to possible risk of loss.	Likewise, the Municipal Accountant ensure the monthly conduct of reconciliation of her Cash-Local Treasury Ledger balances with that of the Cashbook-Collections of the Municipal Treasurer and submit a Statement of Reconciliation to the Audit Team every given date.
observations.managementAction: Deposited the year-end cash balance at the next banking day of the year.

Accounting Office will prepare and maintain subsidiary ledger for each taxpayer.
To make the necessary adjustments once the List of Delinquent taxpayers received.
observations.reason:
Assessment Roll was furnished to the Office of the Municipal Treasurer on February 4, 2019. 
The delay in the preparation of List of Delinquencies was due to implementation of activities related to Pandemic COVID-19 response such as relief distribution and other similar activities.
Once the List of RPT delinquencies was prepared the same will be submitted to Office of the Municipal Accountant and COA. Preparation of Summary of Collections is on-going

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to  the Management the following:

the Municipal Accountant to:

Ensure that the officers and employees with outstanding or unliquidated cash advances should not be granted additional cash advances, until the previous ones are settled, to preclude accumulation of unliquidated cash advances.

Ensure that all regulations pertaining to cash advances should be strictly enforced.

Monitor the outstanding cash advances of officials and employees and demand immediate settlement after its purpose has been served.

Issue demand letter to settle the unliquidated cash advances.

the Municipal Treasurer to:

If warranted, withhold the salaries of those who persistently fail or refuse to comply despite repeated demand for liquidation and/or file appropriate administrative and criminal charges against them as instituted in the sanctions provided for under paragraphs 9.2 and 9.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 to compel liquidation.
observations.reason: Partial settlement only due to instalment payment arrangement made by the accountable personnel and some were separated from the agency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management conduct an investigation on the status of these NGOs/POs and various individuals and discuss with the appropriate committee in the Sangguniang Bayan any remedial actions that will be undertaken to the advantage of the Municipality.  Moreover, please be guided with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-05 dated December 19, 2016 for the write-off of dormant receivables.
observations.managementAction: For the account Due to NGOs/POs the agency was not yet able to complete all the necessary documents for write-off because the agency is still waiting for the response of the Office of the President.  Upon receipt of the Malacañang response, the management will submit it immediately to the Commission.
observations.reason: Reiterated in the Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect the corresponding depreciation as soon as the corresponding useful life of road and road components are identified and make necessary disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements. Moreover, keep and maintain subsidiary records for road and road components for every road network as required in the Circular.
observations.managementAction: Depreciation was already provided in the Financial Statements as of as of December 31, 2020. The Accounting Office is still on process of preparing the subsidiary ledger for Road and Road Complements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that all financial accounts and reports shall be submitted to the Commission on Audit within the prescribed period of ten (10) days from the beginning of the ensuing month. Likewise, the Municipal Mayor consider the possibility of assigning additional personnel in the Treasury and Accounting to facilitate submission of accounts and also improve the Municipality’s operations especially in adherence to pertinent laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Management through the Municipal Treasurer send demand letters to delinquent taxpayers and conduct an information campaign for stricter tax collection measures to all real property owners within the Municipality with emphasis of granting discounts when paid before due dates and remind taxpayers of remedies that can be exercised by the Municipality in case of delinquencies with the possibility of losing their property for not paying at all.
observations.managementAction: Since, RPT is a provincial imposition, the Office of the Municipal Treasurer submitted a List of delinquent Taxpayers to the Office of the Provincial Treasurer and through the Provincial Legal Office, they will send demand letters to the top twenty (20) most delinquent RPT owners. As a result some taxpayers complied and paid. The Office of the Municipal Treasurer will adhere to COA’s recommendation to conduct information campaign for stricter tax collection measures.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management fast track the prompt implementation of all projects/programs funded out of the tobacco excise tax in order to give the constituents early benefits that could be derived therefrom.
observations.managementAction: Delay in the implementation of PPAs was due to COVID-19 response activities. Receipt of NADAI was at the end of the year and continuing appropriations for excise tax fund is pending approval of the realignment by the DBM.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) continue being compliant with the revenue regulations on the withholding of taxes from compensation of employees and creditable taxes for payments to suppliers and contractors and the remittance thereof within the prescribed period; (b) support an explanation through appropriate disclosure in the notes to financial statements and detailed computation showing if the balance reported pertains to unremitted taxes withheld for the month of December 2019 otherwise resolve and reconcile any carry-over discrepancy on the Due to BIR account balance and effect immediately the remittance of the overdue balance.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) support an explanation on the reported balance of Due to GSIS account at year-end as part of disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements; (b) exert further efforts to reconcile employees’ records at GSIS to identify the affected employees on the unremitted accumulated balance of the Due to GSIS account, if any; (c) check with the records whether all Personal Share (PS) deducted from the payroll of employees and Government Share (GS) and Employee’s Compensation Contribution (ECC) for all employees were remitted; and (d) cause the immediate remittance to GSIS for the unremitted premiums and contributions of employees, as warranted by the results of the reconciliation.

We recommended that the Management continue to (a) withhold and remit the mandatory PHIC contributions and (b) direct the Municipal Accountant to support the year-end balance a disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements if it pertains to December 2019 withheld amounts or from prior years balance and conduct review or reconciliation for the noted balance in order that services to be availed of by the employees from Philhealth will not be delayed or affected, if any.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant undertake a thorough review and analysis of the details of the payable accounts and effect the necessary adjustments to revert to the appropriate fund the balance of liability accounts which could not be supported with valid claims.
observations.managementAction: Confirmation Letter will be sent to the payees and thorough analysis and review shall be done. To effect the adjustment of CY2020 Financial Statements
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant support the transaction noted in writing and adopt internal control measures to preclude the incurrence of unnecessary expenditures in the form of bank service charges.
observations.managementAction: To gather documents to support the account and implement sound internal control over cash and cash in bank
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated to recommend that:

Management require the Property Officer – Designate to conduct a complete physical count of all the movable assets belonging to the municipality; and


After the inventory taking, the Property Officer – Designate reconcile the result of the physical count with the accounting records and exert more effort in determining all discrepancies between physical and book inventories, if any. These shall be investigated, cleared and reconciled immediately.
observations.managementAction:
Physical count of all movable properties was conducted by the GSO-Designate.


GSO-Designate have reconciled his records with the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality immediately prepare the implementing guidelines in order to facilitate the proper implementation and collection of fees embodied in the Municipal Tax Code.
observations.managementAction: The subject provisions of the Municipal tax Code were repealed under Municipal Ordinance No. 2021-008 dated May 3, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management recognize in their book of accounts and in the RPCPPE the cellular phone units availed under a postpaid plan subscription and issue a corresponding PAR to the accountable officer concerned.
observations.managementAction: These cellular phone units were included in the RPCPPE but not recognized in the accounting records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management carefully evaluate the viability and sustainability of the Livelihood Development Program by taking into consideration its profitability, productivity and effectiveness to avoid wastage of government funds and discontinue the implementation of the program, if found unsustainable to avoid possible disallowance.
observations.managementAction: No management action was made on the recommendation issued by the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure the effective, efficient and timely implementation of the proposed projects under the 20% MDF through close monitoring and supervision to fully maximize the benefits to the intended recipients.
observations.managementAction: Subject PPAs were implemented in early 2021; however, several PPAs under 20% MDF for CY 2020, CY 2017 and CY 2016 were not yet implemented as of this writing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Stop the practice of allowing these Job Orders to attend trainings and seminars and claim the corresponding travelling allowances and per diems;


Grant only the actual fare whenever they are directed to perform tasks outside their official station; and

Require the Job Orders listed in Annexes C and D (of AAR 2018) to refund immediately the claimed per diems, incidental expenses and travelling allowances on their respective travels.
observations.managementAction:
The Management stopped the practice of allowing these Job Orders to attend trainings and seminars and claim the corresponding travelling allowances and per diems.


Equivalent to actual fare were collected by JOs whenever they are directed to perform tasks outside their official station.


The concerned JOs started the refund.

Inventory Committee is conducting inventory tagging of the properties and preparing required forms and reports.








No action taken by Management.





No action taken by Management.







No action taken by Management.






Management already submitted to the Audit Team some of the required documents.
observations.reason: The LGU was not able to submit their inventory report for CY 2020. Management explained that the conduct of physical count of all properties of the LGU has not been carried out due to the conflicting schedule of the Inventory Committee and the volume of properties to be inventoried. In addition, some properties cannot be traced due to lost/ruined records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort to locate records of these receivables. If such efforts exerted are futile, Management may file a request for authority to write-off said accounts except the accounts Receivables – Disallowances/Charges and Other Receivables arising from cash shortage, to the COA Audit Team Leader and/or Supervising Auditor. The request shall be supported with complete documents enumerated in Section 8.3 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is currently securing documents to support the claims for write-off.
observations.reason:
The receivables were still unsubstantiated as of December 31, 2020. Management has not yet submitted a request for write-off to the Audit Team.

Reiterated in Observation No. 3, page 37 to 38 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the BAC to comply with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 regarding the submission of copies of perfected contracts and purchase orders with their supporting documents to the Auditor within five working days from execution and issuance thereof for review and evaluation so that management will be immediately informed of defects and deficiencies noted, if any and to facilitate the inspection of completed projects and deliveries of goods.
observations.managementAction: Management set a schedule of submission of copy of the perfected contracts on a weekly basis, however, due to some constraints and safety concerns brought about by the COVID-19 pandemic, winning bidders were not able to submit some of the documentary requirements earlier than expected to meet the five-day reglementary period of submission. Nonetheless, copy of perfected contracts and their respective supporting documents were eventually submitted to the Auditor.
observations.reason: Copy of perfected contracts were received by the Audit Team some time after the transmittal of CY 2019 AAR, however, during the latter part of 2020, Management seldom submits copy of contracts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate submission of the lacking documents to facilitate the technical evaluation of the projects and avoid suspension in audit. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant require all the necessary documents pertaining to the implementation of infrastructure projects before payment shall be made to establish the validity and propriety of transactions.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has required the Engineering Department to prepare some of the lacking documents (e.g. Technical Specification).
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant has yet to submit the lacking documents as required by the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE cause the prompt implementation of all projects/programs funded out of the tobacco excise tax in order to provide the constituents, especially the tobacco farmers the early benefits that could be derived therefrom.
observations.managementAction: The implementation of several projects are ongoing, though there are still projects that has no responsive bidders even after posting it several times on websites and conspicuous places.
observations.reason:
Programs/ projects amounting to ₱70,117,788.00 remained unimplemented as of December 31, 2020.

Reiterated in Observation No. 9, page 50 to 51 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and other implementing offices to review the composition of the 20% Development Fund (DF); identify the savings from fully implemented projects and those which are no longer prioritized; and subject to the provisions of the Local Government Code and the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1, reappropriate to current/existing priority development projects. Likewise, instruct the MPDC, Municipal Engineer and other concerned offices to coordinate and strategize to implement fully all the programs/projects under the 20% Development Fund.
observations.managementAction: The different concerned offices have reassessed and reviewed existing 20% DF programs/ projects and was able to reappropriate to priority projects.
observations.reason: Management mentioned that some of the unimplemented projects require technical personnel that is currently hard to source because of the ongoing community quarantine restrictions.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the Head of the Procuring Entity to direct the BAC to:

Submit justification in declaring the contractors as Lowest Calculated Responsive Bidder despite the foregoing deficiencies noted; and

Judiciously conduct post-qualification by verifying, validating, and ascertaining all statements made and documents submitted by the bidder with the Lowest Calculated Bid and ensure that the Post-Qualification Report contains in-depth results on the verification of technical requirements.
observations.managementAction:
The Management overlooked this recommendation.




The Management said that they disqualified some of their suppliers and contractors after post-qualification.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to make a representation with the Provincial Treasurer’s Office to seek assistance in the enforcement of collection of delinquent taxes, especially those long over-due.  Moreover, to ask assistance from the Barangay Officials for them to inform the heirs of the deceased delinquent taxpayers or occupants of the properties to pay their delinquent taxes.
observations.managementAction: The Management is yet to coordinate with the Provincial Treasurer. Nonetheless, some demand letters were sent to the delinquent tax payers with the help of Barangay Officials.
observations.reason: Inquiry reveal that Management has not yet coordinated with the Provincial Treasurer.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management:

Prioritize the installation of the water meters to facilitate the measurement of the volume of water delivered to a household or property;

Monitor and evaluate both the physical and financial aspects of the operation of the SALINTUBIG Projects including its functionality, effectiveness and efficiency for the benefit of the intended end-users; and

Prepare a reasonable work plan based from the result of the M&E activities to attain sustainability of the SALINTUBIG Projects and its eventual conversion as a local economic enterprise.
observations.managementAction: For all of the recommendations, Management said that the non-implementation was due to the restrictions brought by the pandemic. However, an action plan was already prepared to solder its implementation for the coming months.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management strictly adhere to the guidelines of NDRRMC, DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 in the allocation and use of the LDRRM fund and ensure that the fund is allocated and utilized for priority projects and activities that support the goals        under the four (4) thematic areas of the DRRM.
observations.managementAction: The Management asked for gradual implementation.
observations.reason: Similar instance was noted in Observation Number 2 in Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the Local Chief Executive and Solid Waste Management Board to:

Prioritize the establishment of sanitary landfill by constant and consistent follow up with the concerned agencies for it to finally materialize and so that they can already gear up for the next step for its construction; 

Strictly enforce waste segregation at source in order to achieve the primary objective to protect the environment and public health from substantial, present, and potential hazard of solid wastes; and

Promote Solid Waste Management Awareness through proactive Information and Education Campaigns (IEC) in coordination with the Barangay Solid Waste Management Committees and academic institutions.
observations.managementAction:
The Management said that the non-implementation was hampered by the difficulties brought by the pandemic. 




The Management said that due to COVID-19 related tasks, not much attention was dedicated to this matter.



Management said that the pandemic restricted people from gathering, hence, the non-implementation. Nonetheless, Management said that IECs will be conducted next year.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Local GAD Focal Person through the Municipal Planning and Development Office to comply with the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular 2013-01 and 2016-01 on the establishment of a gender responsive monitoring and evaluation system through the GAD M&E Team’s performance of its functions to evaluate the outcomes of the LGU GAD policies, programs, and projects.
observations.managementAction: The Management said that they are yet to implement the recommendation.
observations.reason: The LCE is yet to instruct the GAD Focal Person to comply with the said JMC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management exhaust all possible means to document the legality of ownership over the parcels of land through TCTs in the name of the Municipality to avoid third party claims/land dispute and to ensure that the LGU has legal rights over all recognized assets in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The ICO-Assessor started coordinating with different agencies for the clearances and certifications needed by the surveyor to process the Land Titles.
observations.reason: Inquiry with the Management revealed that they are still in the process of completing the required documents for land titling. Reiterated in Observation No. 5, page 39 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to Management the following courses of actions:

Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer/Supply Officer to reconcile their records and determine the value of the 16 units of unserviceable properties which may facilitate the determination of the current appraisal value of the said properties; and

Require the head of the Inventory Disposal Committee to facilitate the disposal of the remaining unserviceable properties of the agency in accordance with the requirements and procedures provided in Sections 76 and 79 of PD 1445 to generate additional funds for the agency’s operations, prevent said unserviceable properties from further deterioration and loss of value, and allow the agency to use the space occupied by the unserviceable properties.
observations.managementAction: The accounting office inventoried all available records to no avail; hence, they secured the approval of Sanggunian to initiate the request for the accounts’ write-off.
observations.reason:
There were still unserviceable properties with no corresponding values in the report.


Not implemented.
Aside from the vehicles, other unserviceable properties were not yet requested by the Management for disposal.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the immediate submission of the lacking documents to the audit team for verification and audit purposes in accordance with Section 4 (6) of PD 1445 and Section 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 to preclude the issuance of Notice of Suspension.
observations.managementAction: The BAC submitted some of the lacking documents to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Not all required documents were submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC Chairman require the BAC Secretariat to post the Notices of Award and Notices to Proceed together with the approved contracts for the procurement of infrastructure projects, goods and services in the PhilGEPS and agency website, if any, within the prescribed period as required under Sections 37.1.6, 37.4.2 and 54.3 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 for transparency in government procurement.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the Management.
observations.reason: Review of contract documents for the year 2020 disclosed the same deficiency. Reiterated in Observation No. 8, page 48 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the employees concerned to submit the necessary documents to support the payment of honoraria to lecturers, resource persons and facilitators pursuant to Section 5.7.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and DBM Circular No. 2007-1.

We also recommended that Management submit justification citing their basis in granting honoraria to agency officials/employees during the “Formulation of Executive Legislative / Capacity Development Agenda”, otherwise, refund the relative amount of P20,500.00 paid to them.
observations.managementAction:
No action taken by the Management.




Management explained that their tasks during the said affair are outside of their regular functions; hence, the honoraria.
observations.reason:
No documents were submitted to the Audit Team.




Not implemented.
The Audit Team is of view that the explanation provided by the Management is not sufficient to warrant the grant of honoraria.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Local Chief Executive to: 

Require the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare and submit monthly SAAOB not later than the twentieth day after the end of each month;

Instruct the Budget Officer and Accountant to perform quarterly reconciliation of the budget and actual amounts in order to prepare periodically the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts; and

Ensure that the year-end set of financial statements are submitted on or before the 14th day of February of the ensuing year.
observations.managementAction:
No action taken by the Management.




The Budget Officer and Accountant were required by the LCE to conduct quarterly reconciliation of budget and actual amounts.

Management submitted the year-end FS within the prescribed period.
observations.reason:
Review of the periodic reports being submitted by the Budget Officer revealed no monthly SAAOB. Reiterated in Observation No. 15, page 63 of this report.

Not implemented.
Based on the quarterly reports submitted, no SCBAA was prepared by the officials concerned.


Implemented.
The 2021 financial statements were submitted on February 12, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant undertake a regular and thorough review and analysis on the details of payable accounts and effect the necessary adjustments and revert to the appropriate fund the balance of liability accounts, if these could not be supported with valid documents and claimants.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant revisit the valuation of recorded inventories comparing cost against the current replacement cost, whichever is lower.

Likewise, we recommended that the concerned employees record and account all purchases and issuances/uses of inventory items by maintaining Supplies Ledger Card (SLC) pursuant to Sections 182 and 191 of the Government Accounting Manual for Local Government Units, Volume I to ascertain the reliability of inventory account balances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct:
The Municipal Treasurer to send written notices to the payees concerned to remind them of the existence of check/s which became stale. After which he shall issue new checks upon presentation/surrender of the cancelled stale check and the corresponding DVs with supporting documents be returned by him to the Municipal Accountant. The corresponding amounts of cancelled stale checks should be reverted to the book balance.
The Municipal Accountant to furnish the Municipal Treasurer and the Office of the Audit Team Leader copies of the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) relative to the adjustments made and provide Bank Reconciliation Statements between the Municipal Treasurer’s Cashbook for Cash in Bank Accounts and with that of her Cash in Bank-General Ledger Balances showing that the said accounts are reconciled regularly.
The Municipal Accountant to explain in writing the variances noted in cash in bank accounts noted.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive issue demand letters to the concerned accountable officers thru the initiative of the Municipal Accountant for the prompt liquidation of the outstanding cash advances.  Likewise, ensure the strict compliance with COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and RA 11494 pertaining to COVID-19 to ensure the social and economic benefits of funds to the LGU constituents.

We recommended that the Punong Barangays together with the MSWDO issue Joint Certification stating that the person is entitled with the grant to be attached with Social Amelioration Card for reference. Also, the disbursing officers shall provide the Audit Team a written explanation for the non-submission of a copy of any valid IDs during the releases. Non-submission of documents may cause the Audit Team to issue Notice of Suspension/Disallowances, if warranted. Likewise, ensure completeness of the entire documentary requirements set forth in the cited regulation

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make full-disclosures in the financial statements on the impact and overall risk of COVID-19 in LGUs operations to be faithfully represented and be more reliable.

We recommended that the LGU thru the Municipal Accountant make a full disclosure in the Notes to FS in the succeeding report of all donations, subsidies and transfers received and utilized related to COVID-19 outbreak and other related/similar fortuitous events in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014 and COA Circular No. 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020.

We recommended that the LGU submit a one-time report on Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances, instead of monthly reports to be supported by the Acknowledgement receipts of the donations in-kind, proof of receipt by and distribution to beneficiaries, and the inventory of remaining undistributed items for verification and validation of proper valuation of balances. Copy of the report should be submitted to the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council and COA within ten (10) days after the end of each quarter.

We recommended that Management require all officials and employees responsible in the preparation of driver’s trip tickets to record the complete detail of the travels undertaken including the odometer readings before and after the trip for all vehicles of the municipality to ascertain the actual distance travelled for each motor vehicle. Implement strictly the provisions of the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles prescribed in COA Cir. No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 for effective control of gasoline, oil and lubricants expenditures.

Likewise, ascertain its full compliance with the Local Budget Circular (LBC) No. 125 dated April 7, 2020.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Local Sanggunian revisit the locally-enacted resolution if the same are still relevant/reasonable nowadays considering the cheaper, prevailing rates offered by the mobile networks. Otherwise, the elected/municipal officials should decrease the communication allowance to a more conscionable rate using the postpaid plan rates as the benchmarked amounts.  The principle of judicious utilization of government funds as emphasized in Section 2 of PD 1445 must be considered at all times.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant support the transaction noted in writing and adopt internal control measures to preclude the incurrence of unnecessary expenditures in the form of bank service charges to avoid disallowance in audit. Should the bank charges are found in order, the amount should be properly adjusted in the books of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer stamp “PAID” all disbursement vouchers along with all accompanying supporting documents after payment to avoid its possible re-use, thus safeguarding cash from loss thru double payments.

We recommended that the LGU compel the contractor to complete the equivalent deficient amount through work rectification otherwise, the same amount shall be disallowed in audit.

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Engineer strictly monitor implementation of awarded projects to ensure that these projects are accomplished in accordance with the approved plans and specifications, and ensure that all projects are inspected on their scheduled completion dates so that percentage of unfinished work is determined as basis in the computation of liquidated damages to be deducted in the final billing.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the MA to derecognize the Plants and Trees account in the FS in accordance with IPSAS 1 and RCA of GAM Volume III to fairly present the financial position of the Municipality as at year-end; and to recognize an asset only if there is future economic benefit that is expected to flow to the Municipality.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Engineer to include the CSHP as a separate pay item in the preparation of POW in compliance with DPWH DO No. 56 series of 2005 in relation to DOLE DO No. 13 series of 1998 to ensure the safety of workers during construction.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Assessor to prioritize the titling of the 31 parcels of land and/or to register under the Torrens Title System in accordance with Section 148 of the Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property Management in the LGUs as prescribed under COA Circular No. 92-386 by allocating appropriate funds in its proposed budget to facilitate titling of these parcels of land.

We recommended that Management instruct the Tourism Officer-Designate to immediately conduct a thorough evaluation on the sustainability of the Livelihood Development Program considering its profitability and productivity and thereafter submit to the LCE as basis for his decision-making.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Development Council to coordinate with the heads of implementing offices to: (1) implement the PPAs embodied in the Municipal Development Plan to pave way for the timely delivery of the benefits and/or services expected from the implementation of the PPAs; (2) revisit the plans and budget embodied in the MDF and identify a more appropriate and responsive PPAs; and (3) appropriate the savings of funds derived from the previous implementation of PPAs to new PPAs that are aligned to the purpose of MDF.

We recommended that the LCE require:

The Inventory Committee to complete the conduct of physical count of PPE and attach property tag/sticker for easy identification of government property and ensure its proper usage. Thereafter, submit the corresponding inventory report to the Auditor for verification on the accuracy and existence of the reported PPE. Also, the inventory report should be reconciled with the accounting and property records and adjust records based on valid documents to reflect the correct balances of the PPE accounts.

The Municipal Accountant to maintain complete PPE ledger cards and continue tracing back the details of prior years’ acquisitions to support the recorded PPE.

The Municipal Treasurer to maintain property cards to facilitate the physical count of PPE items. Determine the officials/employees to whom the equipment were issued and issue the corresponding PAR to pinpoint the direct person responsible thereof. Moreover, renew all PAR every three years to update the information of the end-user of the equipment listed in the RCPPE.

If the existence of PPE in previous years could not be established despite exhaustion of all diligent efforts, Management may follow the procedures on the disposition for non-existing PPE items for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances of government agencies as provided under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to adhere strictly to Section 350 of RA No. 7160. All lawful expenditures and obligations should be charged to the year these were incurred. Require the Municipal Accountant that all expenses at the end of the year when payment could not be effected yet to obligate the same and set up the corresponding payables on the basis of complete supporting documents.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort to locate the records of these receivables. If such efforts exerted are futile, Management may file a request for authority to write-off said accounts except the accounts Receivables-Disallowances/Charges and Other Receivables arising from cash shortage, to the COA Audit Team Leader and/or Supervising Auditor. The request shall be supported with complete documents enumerated in Section 8.3 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant cause the immediate submission of the lacking documents to facilitate the technical evaluation of the projects and goods and to avoid supension in audit. Oblige the  Municipal Accountant to make sure that all necessary documents pertaining to the procurement of goods and implementation of infrastructrure projects shall be attached to the disbursement voucher before payment is made to establish the validity and propriety of transactions.

We recommended that the LCE require the BAC to strictly comply with the submission of the APP-CSE in consonance with DBM Circular Letter No. 2013-14 dated November 29, 2013 to ensure the inclusion of useful information for consolidation purposes as a basis in its projection of inventory requirements, scheduling of procurement activities and overall management of the central procurement of common-use goods.

We recommended that the LCE require the BAC to strictly observe the posting of requests for price quotation/proposal and the notices of award together with the contract or purchase order for procurement of goods and services through alternative methods of procurement at any conspicuous place in the premises of the Municipality to ensure interested users’ ready access to information.
We recommended that the Municipal Government facilitate the availment of the FINDES of the Landbank to reduce cash advances as a way of strengthening internal control on cash.

We recommended that the LCE cause the prompt implementation of all projects/programs funded out of the tobacco excise tax in order to give the constituents, especially the tobacco farmers the early realization of the benefits that could be derived therefrom.

We recommended that Management exhaust all available remedies provided under the laws, rules and regulations in enforcing the immediate compliance to the audit suspension as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2009-006.

We recommended that Management charge the above-mentioned expenditures to the General Fund. Thereafter, reprogram the amount of ₱127,343.50 to PPAs authorized in the NDRRMC, DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1.

We recommended that the Management:
Direct the Engineering Office to furnish the Accounting Office of the Municipality’s Quarterly Reports on Government PPAs to apprise the Municipal Accountant of the existence of on-going infrastructure projects;
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to make sure that all on-going infrastructure projects are recognized as Construction in Progress in the Financial Statements at year-end; and
Coordinate with their contractors for them to provide progress billings/SWA which will be their basis in recognizing the on-going infrastructure projects in the Financial Statements.
During the exit conference, the Management reiterated that the non-recognition was due to the absence of Progress Billings or Statement of Work Accomplished (SWA) which could have been their basis for recording. Nonetheless, they agreed with the recommendations and assured that it will be under their lookout to make sure that all on-going infrastructure projects will be recognized and recorded in the books of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Management:
Require the Municipal Assessor to expedite the titling of recorded parcels of land to establish ownership thereof and to fully protect the interest of the Municipality; and
Provide the Office of the Audit Team Leader with Quarterly Status Report with supporting documents on the status of the application for land registration.

We recommended that the Management institute and adopt a sound system of internal control in safeguarding the agency’s assets, particularly Cash through directing:
the Municipal Treasurer to prepare all the necessary documents and transact to the Branch Manager of LBP regarding the intention to avail of the bank’s ATM facility, and
the Human Resources Management Officer (HRMO) to enroll employees for LBP automated Payroll Clearing System so that once the ATM is installed, the LGU can start using it and reduce the cash accountability of the DO which would eventually reduce or mitigate the risk of loss of government funds.

We further recommended that the Management effect the increase of the fidelity bonds of the Municipal Treasurer commensurate to their accountabilities, in accordance with the Bureau of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009.

We recommended that the Management:
Convene earlier with the LSB for the timely review and approval of the SEF Budget to avoid delays in the implementation of the PPAs under the said fund;
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to assist the LSB in reviewing SEF budget particularly in the coming up with a more specific and detailed PPAs for appropriation;
Instruct the Bid and Awards Committee to properly plan and schedule all procurement activities pertaining to educational projects for their timely implementation; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to review claims and their supporting documents before effecting payment.

We recommended the Management to direct the BAC Chairman to:
Prepare, consolidate and submit the required Procurement Monitoring Report within the prescribed submission in the GPPB Circular not only to the Department of the Interior and Local Government for purposes of compliance report for the Seal of Good Local Governance but on the obligations of the agency on the full disclosure and sustainability of transparent practice on how the public funds are managed, disbursed and used; and
Monitor each procurement activities specified in the Annual Procurement Plan (APP) and reflect the information in the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) as well as the timeline on all procurement activities.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Assessor to fast track the completion of tax mapping operations of the LGU to facilitate the cleansing and reconciliation of their records on RPT and SET;
Require the Municipal Treasurer to provide the Municipal Accountant the breakdown of all recorded RPT receivables at the beginning of CYs 2016 to 2020 for proper adjustment; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to adjust the RPT and SET Receivables according to the records provided by the Municipal Treasurer, while the reconciliation between the records of the Municipal Assessor and Municipal Treasurer is on-going in order to at least fairly present the balances of the accounts in the financial statements.

The Municipal Treasurer replied that aside from the reconciliation of records between the Assessor’s Office and Treasury Office, further verification of the discrepancies of the uncollected/delinquent taxes is also on-going.

We reiterated the audit recommendation in previous years that Management require the Municipal Assessor to fast track the gathering of all documents and certifications necessary to secure titles or patents over the parcels of land being exclusively used by the LGU to avoid third party claims/land dispute and to ensure that the LGU has legal rights over all recognized assets in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management:
Require the BAC to judiciously prepare and accomplish all the documents needed in the conduct of procurement including the PO and RFQ;
Instruct the BAC secretariat to ensure that the required PhilGEPS posting of procurement opportunities in the agency, and the Notice of Award, Notice to Proceed and contracts be strictly observed; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents to facilitate further review of said transactions.

In reply, Management said that the observations and recommendations were discussed in their executive meetings to determine the necessary actions to correct the procurement practices of the LGU. Management also stated that they are in the process of completing all the lacking documents noted in audit.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer and BAC Chairman, to submit the applicable documentary requirements for each time extension granted to the contractor and all the lacking documents for the procurement and payment for further evaluation and technical review and inspection.

Management agreed to take appropriate actions to comply with the audit recommendations.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Engineer to require the contractors, prior to issuance of Certificates of Final Acceptance, to post/submit warranty security with sufficient amount in any acceptable form; and
Require the ICO-GSO to ensure that suppliers will submit a special bank guarantee before accepting the goods purchased by the LGU; otherwise, instruct the Municipal Treasurer to deduct the retention money from the actual payment equivalent to at least one percent of the contract price.

Management replied that for infrastructure projects, surety bond is required from contractors in lieu of the ten percent (10%) retention money. Nevertheless, they assured the Audit Team that the recommendations will be complied with.
For clarification, the 10 percent retention money required under Section 6.1, Annex E of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 which is to be retained from every progress payment until 50 percent of the value of works is different from the warranty security required under Sections 62.2.3.1(a) and 62.2.3.3 of the same IRR. The former serves to cover uncorrected discovered defects and third-party liabilities until the work is satisfactorily done, while the latter is required to guarantee that the contractor will perform his responsibilities if found liable for any structural defects or failures discovered within one year after the issuance of Certificate of Final Acceptance.

We recommended that Management instruct the BAC to initiate the assessment of 2020 procurement performance of the LGU using the APCPI system and recommend to the HoPE for approval and submission to the GPPB.

The BAC assured the Audit Team to take necessary actions in compliance with the audit recommendation.

We recommended that Management instruct the ICO-GSO to ensure that the AIRs are properly filled up and accomplished to fully establish the compliance of the supplier with the terms of the contract or purchase order before the approval of payment.

We further recommended that Management instruct the ICO-GSO to always require the suppliers to issue delivery receipt upon delivery of the goods and sign the acceptance portion thereof as well as the AIR indicating the same date.

The ICO-GSO, in his reply, committed to rectify all areas needed to be complied with in adherence to the audit observations and recommendations.

We recommended that Management instruct the BAC to strictly observe and monitor the procurement process of the LGU to avoid the practice of requiring advance deliveries.

Management assured the Audit Team to observe the proper procurement procedures to avoid requiring advance deliveries from the suppliers.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare and submit monthly the SAAOB not later than the twentieth day after the end of each month; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit Bank Reconciliation Statements and Pre-closing Trial Balances within ten days and twenty days after the end of each month, respectively.

The Municipal Budget Officer replied that she will coordinate with the Accounting Office and other line offices in order to prepare the SAAOB, Bank Reconciliation Statement and Trial Balance to facilitate the timely review and evaluation of said reports.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to continue sending notices to delinquent taxpayers and making necessary information drive campaign on the awareness of payment in advance of the real property taxes in order to encourage the taxpayers for prompt payment; otherwise, apply the remedies provided under the law in order to generate more revenue that may be appropriated for the implementation of additional programs and projects for the benefit of its constituents.

Management committed to continue exerting effort in the collection of delinquent taxes.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to continue sending demand letters and employ aggressive collection strategies to enforce collection of the loans especially to the borrowers related to the employees of the LGU. In case of non-collection thereof, resort to legal action in the collection of overdue receivables to avoid possible loss of government funds.
We also recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to expedite the gathering of all necessary documents to support the request for write-off of the dormant receivable account.

The Municipal Accountant stated that she had already requested for the Death Certificates of the loan borrowers who already passed away to facilitate the write-off of the outstanding accounts. Likewise, the receivables from the relatives of some employees were already being deducted from their salaries, but they could not increase the periodic deductions due to the threshold set for the Net Take Home Pay.

We recommended that Management require the GAD Focal Person to facilitate the completion of the GAD Database to help the LGU in its gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation in order to serve the very interest or need of its constituents relative to gender-related issues.

The GAD Focal Person said that her office will coordinate with the Municipal Planning and Development Office in order to acquire the necessary data for the establishment of GAD database. The focal person also said that the LGU shares with the same belief that such data could indeed be of great help to attain a more gender-responsive and effective governance, planning, programming and policy formulation.

We recommended that Management require the GAD Focal Person to:
Expedite the creation of the M&E Team and define its detailed functions, responsibilities and accountabilities;
Ensure that M&E activities are included in the GPB and that the same are undertaken by the Team; and
Prepare and submit the required supporting documents of the 2020 GAD Accomplishment Report of the LGU.

The GAD Focal Person assured the Audit Team in taking necessary steps to comply with the audit recommendations.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to require the suppliers to submit the Charge Sales Invoice for the goods procured by the LGU.

Likewise, we recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to release check payments only for goods with proper evidence of payment.

Management agreed to take appropriate actions in compliance with the audit recommendations.
In compliance with the National Internal Revenue Code of 1997, the total amount of ₱8,756,558.47, in addition to the 2019 ending balance of ₱14,016.67, was withheld from compensation as well as from payments on goods and services, and accordingly remitted the amount of ₱8,773,262.98 as of December 31, 2020, leaving a debit balance of ₱2,687.84 due to taxes withheld from suppliers but the checks pertaining thereto were cancelled/dishonored by the bank. Accordingly, tax credit or adjustment in the succeeding remittances will be made.

We recommended that the Collecting Officer in-charge provide a cut-off of her collections during the last working day of the year and allot a time to deposit all collections at the Authorized Government Depository Banks of the Municipality as prescribed under Section 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382 to avoid the exposure of the same to possible risk of loss.	Likewise, the Municipal Accountant ensure the monthly conduct of reconciliation of her Cash-Local Treasury Ledger balances with that of the Cashbook-Collections of the Municipal Treasurer and submit a Statement of Reconciliation to the Audit Team every given date.
observations.managementAction: Deposited the year-end cash balance at the next banking day of the year.
observations.reason: The bulk of undeposited collections are transfer of funds pertaining to unutilized LDRRMF transferred to Special Trust Fund (STF) at year-end. This was deposited on the next banking day following year end closing of books. From August 2020 since the Municipal Treasurer re-assumed responsibility on the cash accountabilities of the LGU, collections for the day are deposited on the next banking day.

Accounting Office will prepare and maintain subsidiary ledger for each taxpayer.
To make the necessary adjustments once the List of Delinquent taxpayers received.
observations.reason:
Assessment Roll was furnished to the Office of the Municipal Treasurer on February 4, 2019. 
The delay in the preparation of List of Delinquencies was due to implementation of activities related to Pandemic Covid-19 response such as relief distribution and other similar activities.
Once the List of RPT delinquencies was prepared the same will be submitted to Office of the Municipal Accountant and COA. Preparation of Summary of Collections is on-going

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to  the Management the following:

the Municipal Accountant to:

Ensure that the officers and employees with outstanding or unliquidated cash advances should not be granted additional cash advances, until the previous ones are settled, to preclude accumulation of unliquidated cash advances.

Ensure that all regulations pertaining to cash advances should be strictly enforced.

Monitor the outstanding cash advances of officials and employees and demand immediate settlement after its purpose has been served.

Issue demand letter to settle the unliquidated cash advances.

the Municipal Treasurer to:

If warranted, withhold the salaries of those who persistently fail or refuse to comply despite repeated demand for liquidation and/or file appropriate administrative and criminal charges against them as instituted in the sanctions provided for under paragraphs 9.2 and 9.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 to compel liquidation.
observations.reason: Partial settlement only due to instalment payment arrangement made by the accountable personnel and some were separated from the agency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management conduct an investigation on the status of these NGOs/POs and various individuals and discuss with the appropriate committee in the Sangguniang Bayan any remedial actions that will be undertaken to the advantage of the Municipality.  Moreover, please be guided with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-05 dated December 19, 2016 for the write-off of dormant receivables.
observations.reason: For the account Due to NGOs/POs the agency was not yet able to complete all the necessary documents for write-off because the agency is still waiting for the response of the Office of the President.  Upon receipt of the Malacañang response, the management will submit it immediately to the Commission.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer, after exerting efforts in collecting the receivables from NGAs such as sending demand letters, submit to the Commission on Audit a request for the write-off of receivables and ensure completeness of required documents to facilitate action thereof pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 9, 2016.
observations.managementAction: Request for write-off was already endorsed to the Commission on Audit on October 28, 2020 and waiting for the result.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator explain the inclusion of the Materials Quality Control Cost in the contract otherwise the amount shall be disallowed in audit.  Likewise, we recommend that the Management ensure compliance with the specific standards in the preparation of program of works and detailed estimates of future infrastructure project proposals in order to avoid incurrence of unnecessary expenditures.
observations.managementAction: The contractor of the said projects complied and refund the amount on January 16, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect the corresponding depreciation as soon as the corresponding useful life of road and road components are identified and make necessary disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements. Moreover, keep and maintain subsidiary records for road and road components for every road network as required in the Circular.
observations.managementAction: Depreciation was already provided in the Financial Statements as of as of December 31, 2020. The Accounting Office is still on process of preparing the subsidiary ledger for Road and Road Complements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that all financial accounts and reports shall be submitted to the Commission on Audit within the prescribed period of ten (10) days from the beginning of the ensuing month. Likewise, the Municipal Mayor consider the possibility of assigning additional personnel in the Treasury and Accounting to facilitate submission of accounts and also improve the Municipality’s operations especially in adherence to pertinent laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: As of this date, latest accounts submitted was for the month of October 2020 only

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management through the Municipal Treasurer send demand letters to delinquent taxpayers and conduct an information campaign for stricter tax collection measures to all real property owners within the Municipality with emphasis of granting discounts when paid before due dates and remind taxpayers of remedies that can be exercised by the Municipality in case of delinquencies with the possibility of losing their property for not paying at all.
observations.managementAction: Since, RPT is a provincial imposition, the Office of the Municipal Treasurer submitted a List of delinquent Taxpayers to the Office of the Provincial Treasurer and through the Provincial Legal Office, they will send demand letters to the top twenty (20) most delinquent RPT owners. As a result some taxpayers complied and paid. The Office of the Municipal Treasurer will adhere to COA’s recommendation to conduct information campaign for stricter tax collection measures.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (a) require the contractor to engage the catch-up program in order to fast track the completion of the project to avoid rescindment of contract as stipulated in Section 68 of RA 9184; (b) conduct an inventory of work accomplished by the contractor and impose liquidated damages at least equal to one-tenth of one percent (0.1%) of the cost of the unperformed portion for every day of delay; and (c) intensify monitoring of project in order to avoid recurrence of delay to other projects.
observations.managementAction: Liquidated Damages (LD) was imposed and collected. The computed LD amounting to P18,936.44 was deducted from the contractors billing on October 30, 2019 as per DV#100-2019-10-2979.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that (a) the Management ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund by fast-tracking the project implementation of on-going projects, reviewing those that may no longer be utmost priority  and reprogramming the same to other more important PPAs economically beneficial to the constituents; and (b) the Municipal Development Council, the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, and the Municipal Engineer closely monitor and strongly pursue the timely and full implementation of all PPAs and regularly submit status reports to the Municipal Mayor in order that appropriate measures can be instituted.
observations.managementAction: Agency has reprogrammed/ realigned other PPAs of the CY2019 20% EDF for COVID-19 Response related activities. As for our LGU an amount of P1,488,596.20 were reprogrammed for the Construction of Isolation Building/ Quarantine Facility. As of this date, the project is on-going with 85% accomplishment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management fast track the prompt implementation of all projects/programs funded out of the tobacco excise tax in order to give the constituents early benefits that could be derived therefrom.
observations.managementAction: Pre-procurement activities are on-going. PPAs will be for bidding on November 2020.
observations.reason: Delay in the implementation of PPAs was due to COVID-19 response activities. Receipt of NADAI was at the end of the year and continuing appropriations for excise tax fund is pending approval of the realignment by the DBM.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management through the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to:

Monitor the utilization of LDRRMF focusing on programs, projects and activities (PPAs) requiring the highest priority in terms of urgency and/or impact on four (4) thematic areas of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management; 

Ensure efficient and effective implementation of all programmed projects identified during the budget year to optimize the utilization of the fund and to warrant that goals and objectives of reducing disaster risk and enhancing disaster preparedness and response capabilities be achieved; and

Direct the MDRRM Officer to submit monthly the Monthly Report on Utilization of LDRRM Fund to COA and DILG.
observations.managementAction:
PPAs of LDRRM Plan for CY2019 were implemented and utilized at a rate of 58.53% versus total appropriation of P4,444,557.40. LGU has allocated Php3,111,190.18 as 70% Mitigation Fund and of which Php2,601,264.53 was utilized which corresponds to 83.61%. The 30%-QRF was not utilized due to non-declaration or non-occurrence of calamity for CY2019.
The MDRRMO will coordinate with the Office of the Municipal Accountant for the preparation and submission of the Monthly Utilization Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the GAD focal person and technical working group to ensure full implementation of the plans, programs and activities which are gender responsive and compliant with JMC Nos. 2013-01 and 2016-01 to continuously provide socio-economic services to the fund’s intended beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: Balance of the CY2019 GAD appropriation was reprogrammed into COVID-19 response related projects and activities. An amount of Php1,141,403.80 was programmed as additional fund for the Construction of Isolation Building/ Quarantine Facility. As of to date, the project is on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management intensify its efforts to have the 10-Year Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission to achieve its vision of having an economically stable, healthy, safe, adaptive, resilient God loving and law-abiding citizenry living in a peaceful, orderly and ecologically sound environment with a responsive and dynamic leadership.
observations.managementAction: The 10-year solid Waste Management Plan (2018-2027) was duly approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission on September 6, 2019 through Resolution No. 1274 Series of 2019

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) continue being compliant with the revenue regulations on the withholding of taxes from compensation of employees and creditable taxes for payments to suppliers and contractors and the remittance thereof within the prescribed period; (b) support an explanation through appropriate disclosure in the notes to financial statements and detailed computation showing if the balance reported pertains to unremitted taxes withheld for the month of December 2019 otherwise resolve and reconcile any carry-over discrepancy on the Due to BIR account balance and effect immediately the remittance of the overdue balance.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation is on-going.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of current year and prior year’s records. Unavailability of previous year’s records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) continue to (a) support an explanation on the reported balance of Due to GSIS account at year-end as part of disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements; (b) exert further efforts to reconcile employees’ records at GSIS to identify the affected employees on the unremitted accumulated balance of the Due to GSIS account, if any; (c) check with the records whether all Personal Share (PS) deducted from the payroll of employees and Government Share (GS) and Employee’s Compensation Contribution (ECC) for all employees were remitted; and (d) cause the immediate remittance to GSIS for the unremitted premiums and contributions of employees, as warranted by the results of the reconciliation.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation is on-going. The unremitted amount pertains to the contributions of the newly hired employees remitted on August 15, 2020.
observations.reason: Delay was due to reconciliation of LGU Employees records and the Agency Remittance Advice (ARA) to GSIS and the non-face to face transaction to GSIS

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) support the amount with an explanation or disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements together with detailed computation per employee which may form part as working paper; (b) exert best efforts to reconcile employees’ records with HDMF to be able to trace the affected employees on the unremitted accumulated balance of the Due to Pag-IBIG account, if any; (b) check with the records whether all Pag-IBIG premiums, Multi-Purpose Loan repayments and Housing Loan repayments deducted from the payroll of employees and Government Share (GS) for all employees were remitted; and (c) cause the immediate remittance to Pag-IBIG for the unremitted premiums and contributions of employees, as warranted by the result of inquiry and reconciliation with HDMF.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of accounts done. Remitted before December 31, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management continue to (a) withhold and remit the mandatory PHIC contributions and (b) direct the Municipal Accountant to support the year-end balance a disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements if it pertains to December 2019 withheld amounts or from prior years balance and conduct review or reconciliation for the noted balance in order that services to be availed of by the employees from Philhealth will not be delayed or affected, if any.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation is on-going. The unremitted amount pertains to the contributions of the newly hired employees remitted on August 15, 2020.
observations.reason: Delay was due to reconciliation of LGU Employees records and the PHIC Online Billing and the non-face to face transaction to PHIC

observations.managementAction:

The Property-Officer Designate (now GSO-Designate) has prepared the Report of Local Road Network.

The Municipal Engineer has submitted to the Municipal Accountant an Inventory of Local Road Network with complete description of every road component.

The Municipal Accountant disclosed properly the Road Network System in the Notes to 2020 Financial Statements.

All concerned officials and employees were enjoined to comply with the requirements set forth in COA Circular No. 2015-008

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Local Finance Committee and Municipal School Board stop the utilization of SEF to programs, projects and activities not included in the provisions under RA No. 7160 and the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1-2017 dated January 19, 2017 in order to maximize the benefits that the students and teachers could avail therefrom.
observations.managementAction: The Local Finance Committee and Municipal School Board have stopped the utilization of SEF for PPAs not included in the provisions under RA 7160 and the DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1-2017 dated January 19, 2017

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) open the bids received immediately after the deadline for the submission of bids as scheduled, unless postponed due to justifiable reasons; and
