Performed periodic reconciliations of said records to the actual inventory results.
observations.reason:
Property Cards were already maintained.

The reconciliation was conducted at year-end during the actual inventory-taking.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) stop granting cash advances to individuals who are not officials nor employees of the Municipality and comply with pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-002 and PD No. 1445.

We further recommended that the Municipality could still provide financial assistance to other agencies through fund transfer as provided under COA Circular No. 1994-013 to ensure that the funds are recognized in the books of the recipient agency and used accordingly as intended.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2018 | Informed concerned personnel.

No more cash advances were granted to individuals who are not officials/ employees of the Municipality in 2019.
observations.reason:
No more cash advances granted to individuals who are not officials/
Employees of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Management:

Adhere to the principles of Imprest System in maintaining the Petty Cash Fund set forth under Section 48, Volume I of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997; and
Refrain from using the Petty Cash Fund for the payment of regular expenditures like the Financial Assistance, the Honorarium of the Head of the Senior Citizens Association and the Refill of Oxygen Tanks which could be paid out of other regular disbursement process.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 | Switched to imprest system this 2019.
observations.reason: Imprest system implemented since January 2019.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that in order to enforce collection, Management send demand letters to the last known addresses of borrowers and thereafter exhaust all remedies to collect the receivables.  Further, for fair presentation of receivables, Management assess the actual status of the dormant receivables, confirm the existence of debtors and if applicable, request for the write-off of these loans in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2018 | Sent demand letters.

Collected P5,125.00 during the year.
observations.reason: Review of accounting records reveal that an amount of P5,125 was collected.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management stricty adhere to prescribed accounting policies and provisions on government disbursements and reporting.  Further, the accounting unit ensure that the required reports are prepared and submitted as provided under Sections 53, 54 and 55 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs).
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 | Regularly submitted the reports.
observations.reason: Reports submitted regularly and were on file.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Accounting Office to facilitate the prompt and regular preparation and submission of monthly bank reconciliations pursuant to the abovementioned provisions in order to facilitate the early detection and correction of errors affecting the Cash in Bank account.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 | Submitted all BRS within 10th day of the following month.
observations.reason: Reports were received on time and were on file.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management:

Stop the practice of using the RATA as payment for the monthly loan amortizations  of their employees; and

Strictly comply with the pertinent provisions of DBM Local Budget Circular No. 103 dated May 15, 2013.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 | Deducted the loan amortizations to the basic salary payroll.
observations.reason: Audit of the Municipality’s payrolls show that the practice of using the RATA as payment for the monthly loan amortizations of their employees was already stopped.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management discontinue the practice of using Accounts Payable in recognizing the amounts withheld from the monthly salaries of employees representing their monthly loan amortization to the Land Bank of the Philippines which is a Government-Owned and Controlled Corporation, instead the appropriate account of Due to GOCCs should be used. Henceforth, observe the provisions of Sections 111 and 112 of PD 1445 and Sections 141 and 155 of the Manual of the NGAS for LGUs, Volume III.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 | Practice already discontinued.
observations.reason: Complied as recommended as per review of journals.

observations.recommendation:
a.We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive as Chairman of the Local School Board, strictly comply with the above DepEd Memoranda directing the discontinuation of hiring of teachers using LGU/LSB Funds.  However, in exceptional cases where there is a justifiable need to hire additional teachers using the LSB funds; it shall be supported by an approval from DepEd Central Office and that only teachers included in the list found in the Registry of Qualified Applicants are to be hired.

b.We have recommended that Management observe strict compliance with the DepEd, DBM, DILG Joint Circular on the utilization of Special Education Fund. Further, we recommended that the Local School Board prioritize programs, projects and activities as stipulated in the abovementioned provisions.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 | Discontinuance of the practice.
observations.reason: Complied as recommended.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive refrain from designating Contract of Service as collector, instead designate an accountable officer who is a holder of permanent position and adequately bonded as required under the above-cited regulation to ensure protection of government funds in case of misappropriation.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 | Stoppage of the practice and an accountable officer who is a holder of permanent position and adequately bonded was designated
observations.reason: Complied as recommended.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management require the coordination between the accounting and the treasury units to facilitate the implementation of the perpetual inventory system to ensure proper accounting and promote better control in the procurement, receipt and issuance of supplies and materials.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 | Coordination between the two units already took place
observations.reason: Complied as recommended as per review of journals.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management strictly comply with the above-cited provisions of RA 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations and COA Circular No. 2012-001.  In addition, the Management is required to submit the lacking documents noted to ensure the legality and validity of the procurement of infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 | Complied.
observations.reason: Complied as recommended.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Management:

Ensure monitoring of the implementation of priority programs, projects and activities which aim to address the needs of their constituents; and 

Review prior year’s unimplemented projects and revise their Annual Investment Plan which are directly supportive of the Philippine Development Plan and Public Investment Program.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 | Programs mostly implemented
observations.reason: Complied as recommended as per review of Quarterly Report on PPAs.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the members of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board (a) monitor the SWMP and oversee its implementation; (b) comply with the requirements prescribed under RA 9003 with regard to establishment of sanitary landfill and enforcement of waste segregation at source; and (c) intensify dissemination and enhancing awareness of constituents on RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2018 | SWMP approved.

Information education campaigns to various groups already conducted.

SWM Equipment already purchased and are currently being used in the Materials Recovery Facility.

Thereafter, strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 for the administration of LDRRMF to ensure that the government funds are being used properly and legally.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 | Report on the Utilization of DRRMF was submitted monthly.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management revert back the unutilized LDRRMF for calendar year 2012 which remained unutilized for five years to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund and made available for other social services after subsequent enactment by the local sanggunian.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 | Reverted back any amount actually left on the said unexpended balance.
observations.reason: Reverted in January 2019.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management submit all perfected Contracts and Purchase Orders along with all the required attachments within five (5) days from perfection as provided under COA Circular No. 2009-001 to enable the Audit Team to conduct timely evaluation of contracts and review of items purchased and thereafter immediately communicate to management any defects/deficiencies noted thereon.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 | Disseminated to the different departments of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Complied as recommended. POs and contracts were received and on file.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the lacking supporting documents as shown in the attached Annex.

Thereafter, the Municipal Accountant being the one certifying on the completeness of documents in the disbursement voucher ensure that all claims are duly supported before effecting payment.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 | Complied immediately.
observations.reason:
Complied as recommended.

Lacking documents were submitted and on file.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Chairman of the Municipal Inventory Committee review all the Property Acknowledgement Receipts of all properties of the LGU and cause the renewal thereof when necessary especially those that were issued three years ago. Likewise, we recommended that Property Numbers be assigned to the fifty-seven (57) properties noted to facilitate identification of the said properties.
observations.managementAction: PAR were already renewed and property numbers were already assigned to the properties.
observations.reason: Review of documents disclosed that the Supply Officer had already pushed through the renewal of PARs, as applicable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the immediate renewal of the Property Acknowledgement Receipts of the government vehicles assigned to personnel under the “job order” status to protect the interest of the government in case of loss or damage to property and shall henceforth refrain from issuing government properties to job order personnel.
observations.managementAction: The municipal vehicles were already transferred to the custody of permanent employees.
observations.reason: Verification of the Property Acknowledgement Receipts disclosed that no personnel under the “job order” status were assigned with vehicles owned by the agency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the officials concerned to transfer the total unexpended balance of LDRRMF – MOOE to Trust Fund under Trust Liability – DRRM Account to present accurately the status of the fund balances in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made sure that all unexpended LDRRMF-MOOE and QRF as of December 31, 2019 were already transferred to Trust Liability account.
observations.reason: Review of the financial records revealed that the unexpended LDRRMF as of December 31, 2019 was already transferred in the trust fund books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the officials concerned to strictly follow the provisions of Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 by submitting the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF within the prescribed period to facilitate timely review of charges to and balances of LDRRMF specific appropriations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and LDRRMO exerted effort to comply with the submission of the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund within the reglementary period.
observations.reason: Based on our monitoring, the agency has complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

Strictly adhere to the requirements of the DENR by not reusing the closed dump site to prevent any harmful environmental and health effects from the continuous operation of an open dump site and to avoid possible government sanctions; and

Consider the agreement made with the City of Urdaneta for the temporary disposal of residual wastes; otherwise, prioritize filling up the vacated position in the management of MRF in order for the facility be used as containment area.
observations.managementAction: The Sanitary Landfill of the agency is already operational.
observations.reason: Due to the diligent effort of the Management, the DENR has already approved the operation of the Municipality’s Sanitary Landfill to replace the dumpsite previously being operated by the agency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management, through the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, expedite the preparation of the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan in accordance with RA 9003 and other pertinent regulations issued by the DENR and DILG to ensure the protection of the public from health and environment hazards.
observations.managementAction: The Solid Waste Management Board expedited the preparation of the revised 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan in accordance with RA 9003 and other line agencies by developing the draft of the said plan.
observations.reason: Based on the interview conducted, the revised plan is still under the review of DENR-EMB.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit in writing his comment/justification on the deficiencies noted and his knowledge on the matter.  Likewise, compel him to deposit the understatement of collection amounting P264,935.00 and reflect the transactions in his cashbook.

As evidences may warrant, file charges to the erring official/s for the acts done which are punishable under Article 171 of the Revised Penal Code, without prejudice to the administrative sanctions that may be imposed by the Management.

Require all collecting officer/s to strictly adhere the provisions of Sections 68 and 69 of PD 1445 and Section 72 Paragraph 2 of GAAM Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer cancel the stale checks after exhaustion of all remedies such as written notices to the payees and corresponding DVs and supporting documents were submitted.  If cancellation is not possible despite written notices, the corresponding amounts of the stale checks may be reverted back to the book balances with the recording of accounts payable.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the following department heads for the compliance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015:

a.	Municipal Accountant

Prepare the Journal Voucher to record the beginning balance of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card (Annex D of the Circular), respectively;

Support the JV with the Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads, working paper on the distribution of costs for the road components, and working paper on the determination of the depreciated replacement cost for road components with no available cost per registry;

Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; and

Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.

b.	Municipal General Services Officer

Maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map; and

Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components. (Annex E of the Circular)

c.	Municipal Engineer

Provide the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.
observations.managementAction: Schedule or list provided for the Road Networks does not tally with the reported total.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant establish controls on the transfer of fund to the schools to initially recognize Due from National Government Agencies instead of having it expensed immediately to maintain accountability of the funds.  Likewise, ensure that the funds are utilized in accordance with its intended purpose by requiring them to submit a duly certified liquidation report with supporting documents as stated in the Memorandum of Agreement executed.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Designated Disbursing & Liquidating Officer ensure that the disbursement vouchers pertaining to grant of cash advances be based on the net amount of the payroll and the same shall be used as supporting documents of the disbursement vouchers before submission to the Audit Team for post-audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant thoroughly review the disbursements pertaining to financial assistance/donations and ensure that these transactions are supported with complete documentary requirements. All grants must always be supported by request/solicitation letters and certified true copies of a valid Identification Card to determine the beneficiaries, together with other documentary evidences necessary for each type of assistance to establish the necessity of its granting in compliance with Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

We recommended that all financial accounts and reports shall be submitted to the Commission on Audit within the prescribed period of ten (10) days from the beginning of the ensuing month. Likewise, the Municipal Mayor consider the possibility of assigning additional personnel in the Treasury and Accounting to facilitate submission of accounts and also improve the Municipality’s operations especially in adherence to pertinent laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Management was not able to comply fully with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management ensure that copies of contracts be furnished to the Auditor within five (5) working days from its execution and shall be supported with complete documents as required in COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

Further, we recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to furnish the Audit Team with the lacking documents specified in the Annex B and regularly observe completeness of contract documents submitted.
observations.managementAction: While Management tried to comply fully with the audit recommendation, a number of perfected contracts were only submitted upon completion/ implementation of the project.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC exercise with prudence its function in checking/screening the eligibility requirements of every prospective contractors/bidders of infrastructure projects of the Municipality, and to comply strictly with RA 9184 and its 2016 Revised IRR.

We recommended that the Management ensure that the Certificate of Acceptance on completed projects be issued after the lapse of the defects liability period and after all defects and failures, if any have been repaired by the contractor and inspected by the Municipal Engineer or one year from project completion up to final acceptance of the project in order to hold the contractor for the repair of defects pursuant to Section 62.2.2 of 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.  Furthermore, we recommended that the Performance Security of the Contractor be made to remain valid until the issuance by the Mayor of the final Certificate of Acceptance.

We recommended that the Municipality fully observe the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 on the use of 20% Development Fund, in order that proper utilization and implementation of the developmental projects be carried out effectively and efficiently. Ensure that the utilization of the 20% Development Fund be limited to programs, projects and activities that will contribute to the attainment of socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes and shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures and yield greater benefit to the people.
We also recommend that the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator  follow the guidelines prescribed in DILG-DBM JC No. 2017-1 to ensure coordination of activities and proper implementation and utilization of 20% Development Fund by aligning expenditures with identified activities in the approved Annual Investment Plan.

We recommended that Management revisit the grant of financial assistance to other LGUs especially to those belonging to higher income classification considering the financial capacity of the LGU.  If warranted, Management may also create proper representation with other LGUs in the Province of Isabela for the evaluation of the practice in exchanging financial assistance between and among the LGUs in the province.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management cause the retrieval of the simulated ARE of unaccounted equipment (water pump) and identify the person/s deemed liable thereof requiring them explanations so that appropriate remedial measures could be taken in order to avoid the occurrence of similar event.

Further, we recommended that Management install a sound internal control system on the distribution of procured equipment and ensure appropriate segregation of duties and adequate provisions for safeguarding assets.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant implement the monthly net take home pay for each employee pursuant to Section 48 of General Provisions of the General Appropriations Act (GAA) for FY 2018 also known as Republic Act No. 10964 and comply for the succeeding GAA issuances to maintain the required net take home pay as it changes from time to time.  

Likewise, we recommended that the Authorized Approving Officer (AAO) for various loans coordinate with the Municipal Accountant before endorsing loans to consider the loan applicant’s Certificate of Net Pay duly certified by the Municipal Accountant in order to avoid instances wherein pay slips are modified in order to make his/her net pay appear to have increased to accommodate additional loan deductions in cases of applying of a specific loan.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant affix her signature/certification on the computation of the HRMO of the terminal leave benefits of officials and employees to signify that computation was properly reviewed by the Accounting Unit as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer, in coordination of the General Services Officer, submit to the Audit Team proofs of posting on the programs/projects undertaken in CY 2018, if any.  Likewise, ensure full compliance on the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 relative to the information and publicity of the Programs/Projects/Activities implemented by the Municipality, their monthly updates and the display of signboards in conspicuous places within the location of the project regardless of nature, irrespective of amount.

We recommended that the management fast track the implementation of programs/projects/activities sourced out through fund transfers from NGAs since the resources are readily available and in order not to deprive the Municipality of the PPAs where the funds are intended for and immediately return to the Source Agency any unutilized amounts of projects funds that were fully implemented in compliance with COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.

We recommended that the Municipal Government maximize the implementation of the LDRRMF programs and see to it that all expenses incurred are in accordance with the approved budget/investment program to strengthen the capacity of the LGU to build the disaster resilience of communities and enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities at all levels.

Also, we recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer, LDRRMO and the Municipal Accountant see to it that the programs/projects/activities indicated in the LDRRMFIP and the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF are aligned to each other in order to ascertain the propriety of utilization of the LDRRMF vis a vis the LDRRMFIP.

We recommended and Management agreed to create an Inspection/ Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of the PPE owned by the municipality and submit the Report on the Physical Count of the Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Auditor pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1 and to instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Property Officer to maintain, reconcile and update the records of the PPE.

We recommended and Management agreed to allocate funds and designate or assign a focal person/official responsible to facilitate the processing of the application for land title with the Land Registration Authority. Necessary actions should be taken to transfer the title of the lot under the name of the LGU to establish the validity and propriety on the acquisition and to avoid the risk of facing future legal issues which may cause additional expense to the government.

We recommended that Management require the concerned offices to effect reconciling items of the cash balances. Assign personnel in each office to make monthly reconciliation of accounts prior to the preparation of the financial statements and bank reconciliation statements on a regular basis to refrain from submission of the required reports beyond the deadline period and accumulation of reconciling items.

For the outstanding checks that are already stale, these should be cancelled after exhaustion of all remedies such as written notices to the payees.  After which the Municipal Treasurer issue of a new check upon presentation/surrender of the cancelled stale check and the corresponding DV and supporting documents be returned by the Municipal Treasurer to the Municipal Accountant. Further, the corresponding amounts cancelled stale checks should be reverted back to the book balances.
observations.managementAction: CY2017 AAR, Observation No. 2, pages 39-42
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.managementAction: CY2017 AAR, Observation No. 3, pages 42-46

observations.recommendation: We recommended that   the Management through the Municipal Accountant expedite the request for write-off to the Commission on Audit the account balances which were considered dormant and could no longer be collected pursuant to Section 10 of COA Circular No. 97-001.
observations.managementAction: CY2017 AAR, Observation No. 5, pages 51-53
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant conduct a thorough review and analysis of all outstanding accounts payable and if found to have no valid claims against the local government unit, cause the reversion of the dormant and long outstanding payables to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund to ensure that only valid claims remain in the books and a more reliable presentation in accounting.
observations.managementAction: CY2017 AAR, Observation No. 9, pages 60-62

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality make necessary actions requiring the recipient to strictly comply with the provisions stated in the Memorandum of Agreement specifically the stated items nos. 1 to 3 in order to help safeguard the Recirculating Dryer from any damage or impairment.  Likewise, include in the terms and provisions, penal clauses and remedies in case of non-compliance with the terms of the MOA.
observations.managementAction: CY2017 AAR, Observation No. 16, pages 75-76

observations.recommendation: We   recommended that the MPDC- then OIC/Municipal Accountant submit   the  lacking   and   necessary   corrective/explanatory  documents for verification, for the transactions namely: (1) Recirculating Dryer with Biomass Furnace amounting to P1,347,343.75; (2) Beauty & Massage Therapy amounting to P351,110.00; and (3) Meat and Fish Processing amounting to P470,160.00 otherwise, the transaction may be suspended or disallowed in audit. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant to ascertain the validity and propriety of the disbursements based on the documents to be submitted by the MPDC prior to submission to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: CY2017 AAR, Observation No. 80-86
observations.reason: Per monitoring, no updated/reports showing compliance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Property Officer – Designate conduct a complete inventory of all the assets of the municipality and prepare properly the Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment by classifying the properties counted according to its type. Likewise, the concerned employees reconcile the result of the physical inventory with the accounting records. All discrepancies between physical and book inventories be investigated, cleared and reconciled immediately.

We recommended that Management adhere existing rules and regulations in the granting of cash advances in order to protect the government funds from possible loss, misuse and/or misappropriations. Further, we recommended that Management refrain from granting cash advances to employees in behalf of their co-employees in order to establish and monitor properly the accountability of each and every employee.

We recommended that management stop the practice of granting special purpose cash advances to elected municipal officials. Instead, grant such cash advances only to properly designated employees covered with sufficient bond to prevent possible exposure of government funds to misuse and/or misappropriations.

We recommended to the management the following:
The SB Secretary to furnish the Human Resource Management Office (HRMO) Certificate of Service for the period covered of the payroll of the elected officials under the legislative department for consolidation;
The HRMO to: (a) check the correctness of the entries and signatures in the DTR; (b) ensure the completeness of all employees’ DTRs and CS Form 6, if any; and (c) consolidate the same, together with the Certificate of Service/Attendance submitted by the SB Secretary, before forwarding to the Accounting Office for processing; and
The Municipal Accountant to process only payrolls with complete supporting documents in order to prove that services had been rendered by the officials/employees.

We recommended that Management strictly abide with the provisions of Section 23 of the Omnibus Rules on Leave. Also, we recommended that Municipal Mayor instruct the HRMO to allow monetization of sick leave credits if and only if the employee applying for monetization has exhausted first his vacation leave credits. We further recommended that HRMO restate the vacation and sick leave credits of the employees who availed the monetization of both vacation and sick leave to properly reflect the correct balances of their leave credits.

We recommended that Management stop issuing financial assistance directly to persons identified to be suffering from schizophrenia and similar conditions, but rather, identify guardians who are more competent to manage the assistance given to the client-beneficiary to ensure that the assistance will be used for its intended purpose.
We recommended that Management submit to the Office of the Auditor a copy of perfected contract and its supporting documents within five (5) days following its perfection for review.

We recommended that Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant maintain a separate cashbook and ledger, respectively for the DBP Savings Account 00-5-13530-545-3 to determine its actual balance in any given period. Further, we recommended that Municipal Accountant prepare monthly bank reconciliation statement for the said bank account.

We recommended that Municipal Treasurer deposit intact all collections made by her and/or remitted to her by the collectors daily or not later than the next banking day, to prevent possible loss of government funds and/or misuse of the money held in her trust.

We recommended that Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer refrain from paying the salaries and wages of casual employees on a monthly rate, instead use the daily rate as indicated in the plantilla of casual appointments, multiplied by the number of days that the casual employee actually worked and any applicable special non-working holiday/s, for the subsequent payrolls.

We recommended to the Management the following:
Require the concerned municipal employees to immediately refund the PIB received covering the CY 2017 and any subsequent disbursement, if any, for the same employee benefit;
Discontinue appropriating funds for the payment of PIB for the succeeding years to avoid audit disallowances; and
Grant Step Increment/s Due to Meritorious Performance instead of PIB to municipal employees who exemplify outstanding performances provided the provisions of CSC-DBM Joint Circular 1-2012 are met.

We recommended that Management: (1) stop the practice of allowing these Job Orders to attend trainings and seminars and claim the corresponding travelling allowances and per diems; (2) grant only the actual fare whenever they are directed to perform tasks outside their official station; and (3) require the Job Orders listed in Annexes C and D to refund immediately the claimed per diems, incidental expenses and travelling allowances on their respective travels.

We recommended that Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 21.1 of the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184 on the validity, accuracy and correctness of the entries published in the PhilGEPS website to avoid any possibility that prospective bidder/s submit an incorrect proposal.

We recommended that BAC and TWG properly evaluate the qualifications of prospective suppliers to enable the agency to source the most qualified supplier to implement projects of the agency and to ensure that all procurements adhered with the requirements of the bidding.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee Members faithfully observe and ensure that the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 are strictly complied with so as to avoid questioning the validity and legality of any procurement activity. Also, we recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer certify obligations only when there is a sufficient document evidencing the amount requested for obligation. Further, the Municipal Accountant to carefully examine the correctness and completeness of the supporting documents before effecting payment.

We recommended that management: (a) revert back to unappropriated surplus of the general fund the unexpended balances of LDRRM Fund for CYs 2011 and 2012 with a total amount of P753,666.81 and make it available for other social services by a subsequent enactment of the local sanggunian; and (b) the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting entry for the same to properly reflect the correct balance of the Trust Liabilities-DRRM account as of year-end.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Inventory Committee to conduct the required annual physical count of the PPE as of December 31 of every year and to submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to the Auditor for verification. Likewise, the prescribed records/forms for PPE accounts should be maintained by the Property Officer and the Accounting Office to conform with the provisions on the Supply and Property Management in the LGUs.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer strictly adhere to the above cited provisions of law in the handling of government funds. Collections must be deposited daily and intact or not later than the next banking day considering that the location of the depository bank is just few kilometers away from the Municipal Hall.

Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer and ICO-Municipal Accountant should strictly follow the procedures in the recording of deposits to conform with the afore- cited rules and regulations.
We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant require all the necessary documents for the purchase of the land to ascertain the legality, validity and propriety of the transaction.

We recommended that Management furnish the Auditor copies of perfected contracts and purchase orders together with all its supporting documents within five (5) days as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2009-001 for a timely contract review and in order that noted deficiencies therein could be immediately complied with.

We recommended that the LGU adhere to the provisions of the existing laws, rules and regulations regarding the payment of RATA of members of the local sanggunian and other municipal officials and employees.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant see to it that disbursement vouchers are duly supported with the required basic documents to ensure their legality, validity and propriety.

We recommended that the LDRRM Council pass a resolution reverting the CYs 2011 and  2012  LDRRMF  unspent  balances  to  the  unappropriated  surplus  of  the  General Fund,  for adoption  by  the  Sangguniang Bayan. Further,  that   the   ICO-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer prepare  the  necessary  transfer  of  funds  and  accounting  reports  to facilitate the said reversion and certify to the availability of said funds for subsequent reappropriation for social services.

We recommended that the MDRRMO submit monthly Report on Source and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund on time in compliance with COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate in the LGU’s Annual Budget one percent (1%) of its IRA for the LCPC and allocate the fund for programs, projects and activities for the protection of children.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the prompt implementation of all projects/programs funded out of the tobacco excise tax in order to give the constituents, especially the tobacco farmers the early benefits that could be derived therefrom.

We recommended that management properly plan the implementation of programs/projects/activities so that the accomplishments and benefits that are expected to be derived therefrom are obtained/ achieved on time.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Chairperson of the GAD Focal Point System to fully implement programs/projects/activities funded under the GAD Fund in order to fully address gender issues/problems of the constituents in the municipal level and to help the national government attain its goal in addressing gender issues in the country.

We recommended that the Municipality require the settlement of the disallowances of the two employees. Likewise, the ICO-Municipal Accountant should ensure that supporting documents of transactions are complete, proper and valid in order to prevent the same from being suspended/ disallowed in post-audit.

We have recommended that Management:
Strictly adhere to the Accounting and Reporting Guidelines for the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund set forth under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 20012 in which incurrence of expenses charged to the previous year’s LDRRMF should be recorded under the Trust Fund Book and closed with the Trust Liability-DRRMF Account at year-end; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the following entries to correct the errors made:

We have recommended that Management:
Strengthen the controls on the handling and recording of PPEs by maintaining perpetual inventory records (Property Cards and  PPE Ledger Cards) and by performing periodic reconciliation of the said records and the actual inventory results; and
Investigate the causes for the missing PPE items and identify the persons deemed liable thereof.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) stop granting cash advances to individuals who are not officials nor employees of the Municipality and comply with pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-002 and PD No. 1445.
We further recommended that the Municipality could still provide financial assistance to other agencies through fund transfer as provided under COA Circular No. 1994-013 to ensure that the funds are recognized in the books of the recipient agency and used accordingly as intended.

We have recommended that the Management:
Adhere to the principles of Imprest System in maintaining the Petty Cash Fund set forth under Section 48, Volume I of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997; and
Refrain from using the Petty Cash Fund for the payment of regular expenditures like the Financial Assistance, the Honorarium of the Head of the Senior Citizens Association and the Refill of Oxygen Tanks which could be paid out of other regular disbursement process.

We have recommended that in order to enforce collection, Management send demand letters to the last known addresses of borrowers and thereafter exhaust all remedies to collect the receivables.  Further, for fair presentation of receivables, Management assess the actual status of the dormant receivables, confirm the existence of debtors and if applicable, request for the write-off of these loans in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We have recommended that Management stricty adhere to prescribed accounting policies and provisions on government disbursements and reporting.  Further, the accounting unit ensure that the required reports are prepared and submitted as provided under Sections 53, 54 and 55 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs).

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Accounting Office to facilitate the prompt and regular preparation and submission of monthly bank reconciliations pursuant to the abovementioned provisions in order to facilitate the early detection and correction of errors affecting the Cash in Bank account.

We have recommended that Management:
stop the practice of using the RATA as payment for the monthly loan amortizations  of their employees; and
strictly comply with the pertinent provisions of DBM Local Budget Circular No. 103 dated May 15, 2013.

We have recommended that Management discontinue the practice of using Accounts Payable in recognizing the amounts withheld from the monthly salaries of employees representing their monthly loan amortization to the Land Bank of the Philippines which is a Government-Owned and Controlled Corporation, instead the appropriate account of Due to GOCCs should be used. Henceforth, observe the provisions of Sections 111 and 112 of PD 1445 and Sections 141 and 155 of the Manual of the NGAS for LGUs, Volume III.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive as Chairman of the Local School Board, strictly comply with the above DepEd Memoranda directing the discontinuation of hiring of teachers using LGU/LSB Funds.  However, in exceptional cases where there is a justifiable need to hire additional teachers using the LSB funds; it shall be supported by an approval from DepEd Central Office and that only teachers included in the list found in the Registry of Qualified Applicants are to be hired.

We have recommended that Management observe strict compliance with the DepEd, DBM, DILG Joint Circular on the utilization of Special Education Fund. Further, we recommended that the Local School Board prioritize programs, projects and activities as stipulated in the abovementioned provisions.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive refrain from designating Contract of Service as collector, instead designate an accountable officer who is a holder of permanent position and adequately bonded as required under the above-cited regulation to ensure protection of government funds in case of misappropriation.

We have recommended that Management require the coordination between the accounting and the treasury units to facilitate the implementation of the perpetual inventory system to ensure proper accounting and promote better control in the procurement, receipt and issuance of supplies and materials.

We have recommended that Management strictly comply with the above-cited provisions of RA 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations and COA Circular No. 2012-001.  In addition, the Management is required to submit the lacking documents noted to ensure the legality and validity of the procurement of infrastructure projects.

We have recommended that the Management:
Ensure monitoring of the implementation of priority programs, projects and activities which aim to address the needs of their constituents; and
Review prior year’s unimplemented projects and revise their Annual Investment Plan which are directly supportive of the Philippine Development Plan and Public Investment Program.

We have recommended that the members of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board (a) monitor the SWMP and oversee its implementation; (b) comply with the requirements prescribed under RA 9003 with regard to establishment of sanitary landfill and enforcement of waste segregation at source; and (c) intensify dissemination and enhancing awareness of constituents on RA No. 9003.

We have recommended that the Management submit copy of the LDRRMFIP and monthly reports on the receipts and utilization of the LDRRMF.  Thereafter, strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 for the administration of LDRRMF to ensure that the government funds are being used properly and legally.

We have recommended that Management revert back the unutilized LDRRMF for calendar year 2012 which remained unutilized for five years to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund and made available for other social services after subsequent enactment by the local sanggunian.

We have recommended that Management submit all perfected Contracts and Purchase Orders along with all the required attachments within five (5) days from perfection as provided under COA Circular No. 2009-001 to enable the Audit Team to conduct timely evaluation of contracts and review of items purchased and thereafter immediately communicate to management any defects/deficiencies noted thereon.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the lacking supporting documents as shown in the attached Annex.
Thereafter, the Municipal Accountant being the one certifying on the completeness of documents in the disbursement voucher ensure that all claims are duly supported before effecting payment.

We recommended the management to:
 Require the Chairman of the Inventory Committee, in coordination with the
Municipal Engineer, to prepare and maintain an Inventory of all Local
Roads and a Road Map. Likewise, the preparation of Report of Local Road
Network must be observed yearly.
 Create a sub-committee or group under the Municipal Inventory Committee
that will primarily handle the inventory of local road network system.
 Direct the Municipal Accountant to provide adjusting journal entries to
recognize changes upon completion of the required inventory reports and
reconciliation with the Registry of Public Infrastructures. Further,
depreciation shall be provided accordingly.

We recommended that management exhaust all possible means to document
the legality of ownership over the parcels of lands through Transfer Certificates of
Title (TCT) in the name of the Municipality to avoid third party claims/land dispute
and to ensure that the LGU has legal rights over all recognized assets in the
financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive provide a stockroom for
the use of the Municipal Treasurer to have a proper and centralized system of
inventory purchases and issuances.
Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to
strictly observe the requirements under Chapter 7, Volume I of the NGAS Manual
for LGUs by maintaining and/or preparing stock cards, stock ledger cards,
Requisition Issue Slips and Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued for the
proper recording of related expenses and inventory balances using the moving
average method of costing.

We recommended that management request for write-off of the dormant
accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016
in order to reconcile and clear the books of accounts of dormant receivable accounts
and fund transfers for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Chairman of the Municipal Inventory Committee
review all the Property Acknowledgement Receipts of all properties of the LGU and
cause the renewal thereof when necessary especially those that were issued three
years ago. Likewise, we recommended that Property Numbers be assigned to the
fifty-seven (57) properties noted to facilitate identification of the said properties.

We recommended that management dispose the unserviceable properties of
the agency in accordance with Sections 76 and 79 of PD 1445 to generate additional
funds, when sold, to be used for the agency’s operations, prevent said unserviceable
properties from further deterioration and loss of value, and allow the agency to use
the space occupied by the unserviceable properties.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the immediate
renewal of the Property Acknowledgement Receipts of the government vehicles
assigned to personnel under the “job order” status to protect the interest of the
government in case of loss or damage to property and shall henceforth refrain from
issuing government properties to job order personnel.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the officials
concerned to transfer the total unexpended balance of LDRRMF – MOOE to Trust
Fund under Trust Liability – DRRM Account to present accurately the status of the
fund balances in the financial statements.

We reiterated the previous audit recommendation that the Local Chief
Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to
reconcile the balances of fund transfers in order to effect the liquidation of the fund
transfers from National Government Agencies and the immediate return of the
unexpended balances of the same to the concerned source agencies pursuant to COA
Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.

We recommended that management require the immediate submission of the
lacking documents to the audit team for verification and audit purposes in
accordance with Section 4 (6) of PD 1445 and Section 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-
001.

We recommended that management instruct the officials concerned to
strictly follow the provisions of Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated
September 12, 2012 by submitting the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of
DRRMF within the prescribed period to facilitate timely review of charges to and
balances of LDRRMF specific appropriations.

We recommended that management:
 Strictly adhere to the requirements of the DENR by not reusing the closed
dump site to prevent any harmful environmental and health effects from the
continuous operation of an open dump site and to avoid possible government
sanctions; and
 Consider the agreement made with the City of Urdaneta for the temporary
disposal of residual wastes; otherwise, prioritize filling up the vacated
position in the management of MRF in order for the facility be used as
containment area.

We recommended that Management, through the Municipal Solid Waste
Management Board, expedite the preparation of the 10-year Solid Waste
Management Plan in accordance with RA 9003 and other pertinent regulations
issued by the DENR and DILG to ensure the protection of the public from health
and environment hazards.

We recommended that Management observe strictly the requirements of
COA Circular No. 2013-004 by installing signboards at the locations of all
government projects for transparency and to secure the right of the people to
information on matters of public concern.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer increase the amount
withheld from the salaries of the delinquent employees to settle the loans within this
year since these were already long outstanding. Likewise, we recommended to
continue sending demand letters and employ aggressive collection strategies to
enforce collection of the loans especially to the borrowers related to the employees of
the LGU. If all means failed, resort to legal action in the collection of overdue
receivables to avoid possible loss of government funds.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Budget
Officer and the GAD Focal Person, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant,
to reconcile their respective records to provide a properly accomplished GAD
Accomplishment Report including the presentation of total expenditures and the
portion attributable to GAD.

We recommended that the management ensure that deduction of 10%
retention money be made mandatory from the progress payment to the contractor
to serve as warranty for hidden defects during the construction period and/or
delivery of goods and shall only be released upon final acceptance of the project or
upon request for substitution in accordance with Section 6.2 of Annex E of the 2016
Revised IRR of RA 9184 in lieu of the said retention money.

We recommended that management ensure the submission of all the
perfected contracts together with the complete supporting documents within the
period prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 to
enable the auditor to conduct timely review and evaluation of the transactions.

We recommended that the Bids and Award Committee (BAC) observe
strictly the transparency requirements of the Procurement Law by inviting
observers from both private and NGO groups in all procurement activities. We
further recommended that they secure information of private groups not only
within the premises of the Municipality but also from other towns to have a higher
likelihood of inviting eligible private group as observer of the procurement
activities.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer include the inventory of
checks maintained and issued in the preparation of the Report of Accountability for
Accountable Forms in order to prepare an accurate and reliable report.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to:
 Require the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare and submit monthly
Statement of Appropriations, Allotments, Obligations and Balances not later
than the twentieth day after the end of each month;
 Instruct the Budget Officer and Accountant to perform quarterly
reconciliation of the budget and actual amounts in order to prepare
periodically the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts;
and
 Ensure that the year-end set of financial statements are submitted on or
before the 14th day of February of the ensuing year.

We recommended that the Municipality ensure that school heads, as
recipients of the funds from the local school board budget, will issue corresponding
Official Receipts for proper fund accounting and monitoring. In the absence of
official receipts, enter into a formal agreement to lay out the duties and
responsibilities of the school heads and the Municipality, and to require liquidation
or utilization report if the former are tasked to implement the PPAs.
Likewise, require the recipients of 2018 financial assistance to submit
liquidation reports to ensure that the related expenses and original documents were
not submitted to DEPED as charges to their regular MOOE.

We recommended that management require the suppliers/contractors to
issue Sales Invoice and/or Official Receipts on various procurement of goods made
in compliance with Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 2-2014 dated January 13,
2014 and likewise, strictly observe said regulations on future disbursements to be
made.

We recommended that Management:
 Require the Municipal Treasurer to continue and improve the collection
strategies on taxes due and collectible by sending RPTOP’s and notice of
delinquency to taxpayers and to conduct an information dissemination
regarding the importance of paying taxes promptly and the consequences of
non-payment of taxes; and
 If circumstances warrant, impose the provisions of Sections 258 and 260 of
the Local Government Code of 1991 in the collections of delinquent taxes to
satisfy taxes, penalties and costs with the following:
1. Seizure of personal property of the taxpayer and sale thereof at public
auction;
2. File civil suit with the proper court; and
3. Levy on real property and sale of delinquent property or a usable
portion thereof at public auction.

We recommended that the Management:
Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit in writing his comment/justification on the deficiencies noted and his knowledge on the matter.  Likewise, compel him to deposit the understatement of collection amounting P264,935.00 and reflect the transactions in his cashbook.
As evidences may warrant, file charges to the erring official/s for the acts done which are punishable under Article 171 of the Revised Penal Code, without prejudice to the administrative sanctions that may be imposed by the Management.
Require all collecting officer/s to strictly adhere the provisions of Sections 68 and 69 of PD 1445 and Section 72 Paragraph 2 of GAAM Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer cancel the stale checks after exhaustion of all remedies such as written notices to the payees and corresponding DVs and supporting documents were submitted.  If cancellation is not possible despite written notices, the corresponding amounts of the stale checks may be reverted back to the book balances with the recording of accounts payable.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant expedite the request for write-off from the Commission on Audit the account balances which was considered dormant for ten (10) years or more upon satisfying all the conditions and requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Management conduct an investigation on the status of these NGOs/POs and various individuals and discuss with the appropriate committee in the Sangguniang Bayan any remedial actions that will be undertaken to the advantage of the Municipality.  Moreover, please be guided with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-05 dated December 19, 2016 for the write-off of dormant receivables.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the following department heads for the compliance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015:
Municipal Accountant
Prepare the Journal Voucher to record the beginning balance of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card (Annex D of the Circular), respectively;
Support the JV with the Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads, working paper on the distribution of costs for the road components, and working paper on the determination of the depreciated replacement cost for road components with no available cost per registry;
Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; and
Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.
Municipal General Services Officer
Maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map; and
Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components. (Annex E of the Circular)
Municipal Engineer
Provide the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide for depreciation on recognized Road Networks accounts following the straight-line method in conformity with COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015 in line with Section 59 of PPSAS 17.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant establish controls on the transfer of fund to the schools to initially recognize Due from National Government Agencies instead of having it expensed immediately to maintain accountability of the funds.  Likewise, ensure that the funds are utilized in accordance with its intended purpose by requiring them to submit a duly certified liquidation report with supporting documents as stated in the Memorandum of Agreement executed.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Designated Disbursing & Liquidating Officer ensure that the disbursement vouchers pertaining to grant of cash advances be based on the net amount of the payroll and the same shall be used as supporting documents of the disbursement vouchers before submission to the Audit Team for post-audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant thoroughly review the disbursements pertaining to financial assistance/donations and ensure that these transactions are supported with complete documentary requirements. All grants must always be supported by request/solicitation letters and certified true copies of a valid Identification Card to determine the beneficiaries, together with other documentary evidences necessary for each type of assistance to establish the necessity of its granting in compliance with Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

We recommended that all financial accounts and reports shall be submitted to the Commission on Audit within the prescribed period of ten (10) days from the beginning of the ensuing month. Likewise, the Municipal Mayor consider the possibility of assigning additional personnel in the Treasury and Accounting to facilitate submission of accounts and also improve the Municipality’s operations especially in adherence to pertinent laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that Management ensure that copies of contracts be furnished to the Auditor within five (5) working days from its execution and shall be supported with complete documents as required in COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
Further, we recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to furnish the Audit Team with the lacking documents specified in the Annex B and regularly observe completeness of contract documents submitted.

We recommended that the BAC exercise with prudence its function in checking/screening the eligibility requirements of every prospective contractors/bidders of infrastructure projects of the Municipality, and to comply strictly with RA 9184 and its 2016 Revised IRR.
We recommended that the Management ensure that the Certificate of Acceptance on completed projects be issued after the lapse of the defects liability period and after all defects and failures, if any have been repaired by the contractor and inspected by the Municipal Engineer or one year from project completion up to final acceptance of the project in order to hold the contractor for the repair of defects pursuant to Section 62.2.2 of 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.  Furthermore, we recommended that the Performance Security of the Contractor be made to remain valid until the issuance by the Mayor of the final Certificate of Acceptance.

We recommended that the Municipality fully observe the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 on the use of 20% Development Fund, in order that proper utilization and implementation of the developmental projects be carried out effectively and efficiently. Ensure that the utilization of the 20% Development Fund be limited to programs, projects and activities that will contribute to the attainment of socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes and shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures and yield greater benefit to the people.
We also recommend that the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator  follow the guidelines prescribed in DILG-DBM JC No. 2017-1 to ensure coordination of activities and proper implementation and utilization of 20% Development Fund by aligning expenditures with identified activities in the approved Annual Investment Plan.

We recommended that Management revisit the grant of financial assistance to other LGUs especially to those belonging to higher income classification considering the financial capacity of the LGU.  If warranted, Management may also create proper representation with other LGUs in the Province of Isabela for the evaluation of the practice in exchanging financial assistance between and among the LGUs in the province.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management cause the retrieval of the simulated ARE of unaccounted equipment (water pump) and identify the person/s deemed liable thereof requiring them explanations so that appropriate remedial measures could be taken in order to avoid the occurrence of similar event.
Further, we recommended that Management install a sound internal control system on the distribution of procured equipment and ensure appropriate segregation of duties and adequate provisions for safeguarding assets.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant implement the monthly net take home pay for each employee pursuant to Section 48 of General Provisions of the General Appropriations Act (GAA) for FY 2018 also known as Republic Act No. 10964 and comply for the succeeding GAA issuances to maintain the required net take home pay as it changes from time to time.
Likewise, we recommended that the Authorized Approving Officer (AAO) for various loans coordinate with the Municipal Accountant before endorsing loans to consider the loan applicant’s Certificate of Net Pay duly certified by the Municipal Accountant in order to avoid instances wherein pay slips are modified in order to make his/her net pay appear to have increased to accommodate additional loan deductions in cases of applying of a specific loan.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant affix her signature/certification on the computation of the HRMO of the terminal leave benefits of officials and employees to signify that computation was properly reviewed by the Accounting Unit as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer, in coordination of the General Services Officer, submit to the Audit Team proofs of posting on the programs/projects undertaken in CY 2018, if any.  Likewise, ensure full compliance on the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 relative to the information and publicity of the Programs/Projects/Activities implemented by the Municipality, their monthly updates and the display of signboards in conspicuous places within the location of the project regardless of nature, irrespective of amount.

We recommended that the management fast track the implementation of programs/projects/activities sourced out through fund transfers from NGAs since the resources are readily available and in order not to deprive the Municipality of the PPAs where the funds are intended for and immediately return to the Source Agency any unutilized amounts of projects funds that were fully implemented in compliance with COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.

We recommended that the Municipal Government maximize the implementation of the LDRRMF programs and see to it that all expenses incurred are in accordance with the approved budget/investment program to strengthen the capacity of the LGU to build the disaster resilience of communities and enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities at all levels.
Also, we recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer, LDRRMO and the Municipal Accountant see to it that the programs/projects/activities indicated in the LDRRMFIP and the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF are aligned to each other in order to ascertain the propriety of utilization of the LDRRMF vis a vis the LDRRMFIP.

        internal control in the checking of the Collectors’ remittances for
        early detection of delayed and incomplete remittance. The Municipal
        Treasury Office shall enforce daily turn-over by the  Collector  to
        the  Liquidating  Officer  and  implement  the  regular  period  of
        deposits  to  the  depository  banks  to   avoid   possible   loss,
        misuse/misappropriation of government funds and  understatement  of
        collection at a given month/period.


    2.13      Management   committed   strict   adherence   to   the   audit
        recommendation.

           and equipment  duly  documented  through  incorporating  in  the
           Report on Physical Count on Property, Plant and Equipment.

    4.11     Management   committed   strict   adherence   to   the    audit
    recommendations.

        settlement of accounts/obligations  and  return/remit  the  unspent
        fund as soon as possible to the source agency.


    5.9       Management   committed   strict   adherence   to   the   audit

6.4     We recommended and Management agreed to strictly adhere with the
        requirements and provisions of existing laws concerning  Accounting
        for Supplies and Materials to strengthen the internal  control  for
        supplies and materials and:


        a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to stop the practice  of  using
           expense method in recording  supplies  and  materials  purchases,
           maintain an inventory account using perpetual  inventory  system,
           prepare supplies ledger card per stock number and  recognize  and
           measure reliably the unconsumed or unused supplies and  materials
           as part of the inventory account and adjust to its  corresponding
           expense account;




        b. Instruct the Municipal Procurement/Property Officer  to  maintain
           supplies and materials stock cards per stock number  and  prepare
           Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued and submit the  same  to
           the Municipal Accountant for recording purposes to fairly present
           its inventory accounts in the financial statements;



        c. Create an Inventory Committee who shall conduct the required semi-
           annual physical count of supplies and materials; and


        d. Supervise the proper  implementation  of  the  inventory  process
           regularly to ensure its effectiveness.

        statements for the year ended December 31, 2018.
