observations.recommendation: We have recommended  that the Municipal Mayor  require the Acting Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to submit the revenue and disbursement documents  and required reports within the first ten days of the ensuing month.
observations.managementAction: Complied with the Audit Team’s recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Acting Municipal Accountant submit to the Audit Team all cancelled checks for inspection and proper monitoring.  Likewise, cancelled checks shall be attached to the Report of Checks Issued and reported chronologically with the other checks issued and the word “cancelled” shall be indicated on the report.
observations.managementAction: Complied with the Audit Team’s recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the officials and employees concerned prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels   and the Report of Fuel Consumption for all vehicles/heavy equipment owned by the municipality and submit the same to the Auditor for verification within the first ten days of the succeeding month.
observations.managementAction: Complied with the Audit Team’s recommendation.
Management implemented some of the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor  require the Acting Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the amount of  P5,688,115.95  from the General Fund to Trust Fund bank account as cash back up of the  account Trust Liabilities -DRRMF.  Likewise, the cash to back up the unexpended balances of QRF and DRRM – MOOE at the end of the year starting 2017 should be transferred from General Fund to Trust Fund to support the Trust Liabilities – DRRMF account under the Trust Fund.
observations.managementAction: Complied with the Audit Team’s recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We   have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer  intensify the collection of Real Property and Special Education Taxes.  Notices of collection should be sent to the delinquent taxpayers to remind them of their obligation to pay their taxes. Likewise, conduct massive tax campaign in all the barangays and offer tax amnesty in order to motivate delinquent taxpayers to avail of the offer.  In addition, seek the cooperation of the barangay officials, anyway, the barangays have also shares in the collection of realty property taxes.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 8, page 36 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the municipal officials and employees concerned  implement fully all the programs/ projects / activities embodied under the 20% Development Fund.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 11, page 40 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan appropriate immediately all funds received from tobacco excise taxes.  Likewise, the Municipal Mayor should cause the prompt implementation of all projects/programs funded out of the tobacco excise taxes in order to give the tobacco farmers who worked hard for the generation of the funds the benefits they rightfully deserve.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 9, page 37 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the prompt implementation of all funds received from other national government agencies in order to deliver on time the benefits due to the intended beneficiaries and to avoid returning the unutilized funds to the donor agencies.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 12, page 40 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to exert extra effort to collect such receivables so that the fund could be used by other deserving individuals.  If such efforts exerted are futile, the Municipal Mayor may file the request for authority to write –off dormant accounts to the COA Audit Team Leader and/or Supervising Auditor.  The request shall be supported by complete documents enumerated in Section 8.3 of COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: None

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor  require the Chairperson of the GAD Focal Point System to fully implement programs/projects funded under the GAD Plan in order to fully address gender issues/problems of the constituents in the municipal level and to help the national government attain its goal of addressing gender issues in the country.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 15, page 43 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Acting Municipal Accountant to see to it that all disbursements are supported with valid and complete documents. Further, management should adhere to Sections 9.4 and 10.4 of COA Circular 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 regarding the settlement of suspensions and disallowances.  Audit suspensions should be settled within ninety (90) calendar days from receipt of the NS to avoid maturity of these suspensions to disallowances and disallowances should be settled within six (6) months from receipt of the ND by the persons liable.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 16, page 43 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and the BAC agreed to:

Continue to update  themselves with the existing guidelines specifically affecting every procurement; and 

Ensure that requisite conditions necessary for the mode of procurement adopted are fully complied with.
observations.managementAction: All BAC members including Secretariat and Technical Working Group already attended RA 9184 seminar

observations.recommendation: Considering the significance of timely submission of contracts and Purchase Orders, we have recommended that management henceforth submit all perfected contracts and Purchase Orders along with all the required attachments within five (5) days from perfection as provided under COA Circular No. 2009-001 to enable the Audit Team to conduct timely evaluation of contracts and review of items purchased and thereafter immediately communicate to management any defects/deficiencies noted thereon.
observations.managementAction: Issued an Accounting Memo reminding the requirement and procedures before  procurement

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and the HOPE directed the BAC to prepare the PMR using the format available in the GPPB website for his approval and submit the same to the GPPB not only in compliance with Section 12.2 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 but most importantly to avoid any sanction in the future.
observations.managementAction: The BAC prepared the PMR but not yet able to submit the same

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to fully settle the issues on the ROW in order to implement the projects and henceforth, ensure that issues on ROW are fully resolved prior to the commencement of the procurement process not only to support the timely implementation of projects but most significantly, deliver the expected beneficial impact to the constituents.
observations.managementAction: Fully settled the issues on the ROW

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended  that the LGU, thru the  Municipal Agriculturist:

Conduct regular monitoring and evaluation on the handling and utilization of the machineries to encourage the Associations to perform their responsibilities explicitly cited in the MOA;

Require the Associations to submit their written policies for the operation and maintenance of farm equipment for accountability, availability and future maintenance; and

Require that the Associations provide appropriate storage spaces to safeguard the machineries from the weather elements, pilfering/vandalism with the end in view of ultimately prolonging their  usefulness in attaining food security and income enhancement.
observations.managementAction: MOAs containing the policies and pictures showing storage of each machinery were already submitted

observations.recommendation: We further recommended that Management consider providing additional personnel to enable the office of the municipal treasurer to conduct massive tax campaigns in the different barangays with the end in view of collecting from delinquent tax payers.
observations.managementAction: The municipal treasurer already conducted tax campaigns and offering discounts for those paying in advance
Lot was already acquired for the proposed ESLF, Inspected and recommended by EMB for DENR Geological Study

a. Physical inventory count for CY 2017 was conducted. Likewise, the matrix of reconciliation was already prepared and submitted to the Office of the Auditor.
b. Lack of complete and updated Property/Subsidiary Ledger Cards were already submitted.
c. Necessary documents for the titling of government lots were already submitted.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 1, pages 29-30 of the report.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to:
1. Secure immediately property insurance for the non-insured government assets including their contents; and
2. Ensure that the updated RPCPPE duly verified by the Auditor shall be submitted to the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS upon renewal of the insurance policies.
observations.managementAction: Properties of the LGU were already insured.
Lacking documents were already submitted and a copy of bank standby letter of credit was attached to every DV for the final payment of LGU projects as substitute for the 10% retention money.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that posting of notices to the public of relevant information on all awarded projects be made where the programs, projects and activities are located or carried out in accordance with COA Circular No. 2013-004 in order to promote good governance through transparency and accountability and to encourage public participation.
observations.managementAction: Publicity of the projects was implemented.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that no additional cash advances be granted unless the previous cash advance was fully liquidated as required under Section 89 of Presidential Decree No. 1445, as implemented in COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to provide certification that the previous cash advances have been liquidated and accounted for in the books in compliance with the documentary requirements required under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office see to it that a particular employee has no existing unliquidated cash advance before granting a new cash advance. Likewise, a certification of the same is issued and attached to the DV.

a. Disallowed per diems were already refunded and only allowed rates were given CY 2017.
b. Job order personnel were no longer allowed to attend trainings in 2017.
c. Refresher course of Day Care Workers for CY 2017 was conducted within the premises of the LGU.
observations.reason: There were still job order personnel claiming per diems per verification of disbursements.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the Local Chief Executive that the amount necessary for payment of honoraria and overtime as enumerated on Section 3.1 of DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-3 be paid to the Members of BAC in conformity with Section 3.4 of the same Budget Circular.
observations.managementAction: BAC Honoraria for CY 2017 was only sourced from sales documents of CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive, as the head of the Local School Board, adhere strictly with the regulation in the prioritization and utilization of the SEF, particularly, for the construction and repair of school buildings and establishment of new extension classes or acquisition of IT equipment, books and instructional materials of students and teachers as required under DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1 and No. 1-A in order to ensure an effective educational development within the locality.
observations.managementAction: Disbursements out of the Special Education Fund were based on the approved PPAs of the Local School Board and in accordance with the revised guidelines of SEF.

To further improve the collection, the Treasury Office conducted information dissemination thru distribution of leaflets and posting of tarpaulins on public places regarding schedule and payment of RPT.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 8, pages 38-39 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to immediately transfer the unexpended/unobligated balance of DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund under the Trust Liability-DRRM account in the Trust Fund books.
observations.managementAction: The unexpended balances of the LDRRM Fund for CY 2016 and CY 2017 were already transferred to Trust Fund last March 2017 and December 2017, respectively.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to enforce strict collection of the loans receivable that have been long overdue by sending demand letters in order to give opportunity to others who wish to avail of the loan assistance program. Likewise, interest and penalty should be imposed on the unpaid balances to comply with the terms of the loan. We also have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to withhold a fixed amount from the salaries of the delinquent municipal employees until their loans are fully paid.
observations.managementAction: Letters were continuously sent to borrowers and co-borrowers.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 9, pages 39-40 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Accounting Office strictly comply with the submission of reports and supporting documents evidencing the transactions on time in accordance with Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 which requires submission within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month as well as with Section 122 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Standard measures and procedures were implemented in Accounting and Number of days delayed were minimal compared to CY 2016.
observations.reason: Number of days delayed were minimal compared to CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive bring the Materials and Recovery Facility (MRF) in operation for the intended purpose in accordance with RA 9003. Utilization Thereof may result to: 1. proper disposal of solid waste, thus, minimize wastes within the municipality; 2. provision of jobs to its local residents; and 3. generation of income from sale of recyclable items for additional funds of the agency. Likewise, we have recommended that budgetary requirement should be set aside for the improvement of the Municipality’s sanitary landfill that will meet the criteria provided under Section 41 of RA 9003 to prevent leachate and hazardous waste that would contaminate the environment and the health of the people. Lastly, we have recommended that the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan be prepared by the Municipal Solid Waste Board and be submitted to the National Solid Waste Management Commission for approval in compliance to Section 16 of RA 9003.
observations.managementAction:
Proper disposal of solid waste through the Implementation of the amended Local Comprehensive Solid Waste Management Ordinance as agreed by the Municipal Solid Waste Board.
MRF was operational, as well as the bioreactor and shredder.
An Amount of ₱6.5 million was allocated for CY 2018 for the construction of sanitary landfill.
The 10-year plan was revised and submitted to the National Solid Waste Management Commission but it was returned because it is not in conformity with the prescribed format. But the final revision is already in progress.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 11, pages 42-43 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management adhere strictly with COA Circular 97-002 requiring payments for all government transactions to be made by check and payable directly to the creditor/supplier or by the Disbursing Officer thru his cash advance. We also have recommended that management stop the practice of disbursing government funds thru reimbursement, except for petty expenses which may be paid thru the petty cash fund.
observations.managementAction: Management pointed out that reimbursements were not allowed except expenses for immediate repair of motor vehicles and other petty expenses. Review of the check disbursement reports disclosed that there were some transactions other than repair of motor vehicles claimed thru reimbursement.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that:
1. Management adhere strictly with the mandated use of government markings on motor vehicles pursuant to the provisions under COA Circular No. 77-61;
2. Driver’s Trip Ticket bear proper approval from the Head of the Agency or his authorized representative, be serially numbered and properly accomplished pursuant to the same Circular; and
3. The Local Chief Executive direct the driver/s to prepare the monthly report of fuel consumption and summary of official travel for proper control and monitoring and submit the same to this office pursuant to same Circular.
observations.managementAction: Vehicles have no markings yet up to this date. Per validation, an employee in the Office of the mayor was assigned to prepare the monthly report of fuel consumption pursuant to the provisions under COA Circular No. 77-61.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconciles the reciprocal accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds, thus, fairly present the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the LDRRMO to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF for the period August to December as basis to ascertain the accuracy of the balance of the account Trust Liabilities-LDRRMF.   
Correspondingly, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the reports on time and immediately furnish the LDRRMO copies thereof. The Municipal Accountant may also exert effort to remind the LDRRMO of the submission of the said report to COA.
observations.reason: Delayed submission of accounts

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management ensure that all procurement activities of the municipality be supported with documentation as  prescribed. It is emphasized that complete documentation does not only mean the submission of documents but also the completeness and consistency of the details thereof. 

As to the disbursements totaling P730,232.12 the lacking and the necessary  corrective documents should be submitted to ascertain the validity and propriety thereof. Otherwise, the said transactions may be suspended or disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure timely submission of accounts. Accordingly, we recommended that the unsubmitted accounts of the LGU for CY 2016 be submitted to facilitate the audit of the transactions as well as to avoid the suspension or disallowance of the same and the imposition of administrative disciplinary sanctions.
observations.reason: Delayed submission of accounts

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit the lacking and necessary corrective/explanatory documents for verification, thus, ascertain the validity and propriety of the disbursement for the dryer amounting to P1,347,343.75. Emphasis is given to the date of the inspection and acceptance of the dryer to establish the validity of the warranty. Otherwise, the transaction may be suspended or disallowed in audit.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer’s Office post or display a tarpaulin or signboard for all its projects at the project location pursuant to COA Circular No. 2013-004, thus, promote transparency. Also, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive inform the SA and ATL concerned within 10 days after the appropriate signboard and/or public notices are already posted as basis for validation.

We recommended and Management agreed to create an Inspection / Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of the PPE owned by the municipality and submit the Report on the Physical Count of the Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Auditor pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1 and to instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Property Officer to maintain, reconcile and update the records of the PPE.

We recommended and Management agreed to immediately designate an employee who shall be responsible person to facilitate the registration and insurance of all motor vehicles owned by the Municipality. Apply and/or renew the registration of all serviceable motor vehicles of the municipality with LTO and contracts of insurance with the GSIS to avoid additional expenses in terms of penalties and payment of damages to third party liabilities in case of vehicular accidents.

We recommended and Management agreed to comply strictly with the rules and regulations in the disbursement of government funds. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to set up receivables for the funds transferred and demand liquidation from concerned association, thereafter, record all subsequent fund transfers to the appropriate asset accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed to strictly adhere with the provisions of COA, DBM, DILG, Governance Commission for GOCCs and DND Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015.

We further recommended and Management agreed to stop the granting of additional cash advance for Confidential Fund to the Municipal Mayor or Special Disbursing Officer unless the liquidation of cash advances for CY 2015 have been submitted to the COA-ICFAU for audit.

We recommended and Management agreed to allocate funds and designate or assign a focal person/official responsible to facilitate the processing of the application for land title with the Land Registration Authority. Necessary actions should be taken to transfer the title of the lot under the name of the LGU to establish the validity and propriety on the acquisition and to avoid the risk of facing future legal issues which may cause additional expense to the government.

We recommended and the Management agreed to ensure that paid expenditures be supported with the necessary and complete documents as required under Section 4 (6) of PD 1445, otherwise, disbursements without supporting documents shall be suspended in audit.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure the efficient and effective implementation of the ESWM Plan to achieve its objectives and convene regular meetings for the monitoring and review of the targets and strategies as stated in the ESWM Plan.

Stop the practice of charging to 20% development fund the expenses which are not related to and do not partake the nature of investment/capital expenditures. The Municipal officials should strictly adhere to the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the DBM and DILG in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization. Any further charges to 20% Development Fund which are unauthorized as above discussed shall be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that management evaluate the unimplemented PPAs and re-appropriate the fund to current priority projects. The fund should be optimally utilized to help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the HRMO to prepare Contract of Service for job order personnel, ensure that conditions are clearly stated in the contract and specify the following: a) expected outputs to facilitate comparison with the accomplishment report; b) specific period within which the work is to be accomplished; and c) source of funds for the payment of salaries. Furnish the Audit Team with copies of contract for JOs within five days after its approval in order to facilitate review thereof and comply with the guidelines set by existing rules and regulations.

We recommended that the BAC conduct pre-procurement and pre-bid conferences or as need arises to clarify terms and conditions and specifications stipulated in the bidding documents. The Committee should set or create a shortlist of suppliers and contractors as a guide to canvassers to attend and observe the proceedings of the conference and special meetings to enhance transparency, in compliance with the implementing rules and regulations of RA 9184.

We have recommended that management dispose the unserviceable properties of the agency in accordance with Sections 76 and 79 of PD 1445 in order to prevent them from further deterioration and loss of value and to have utilized the space being occupied by said properties.
observations.managementAction: Appraisal Committee was already created this January 2017.
observations.reason:
Not yet disposed upon witness of the physical inventory count of the management.  Likewise, these were not yet appraised for disposal.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to exert effort to correct the deficiency by applying the excessive variance against the billing of the contractor and to be guided by the established standards in the preparation and calculation of the Approved Budget for the Contract prescribed.

We have recommended that management strictly implement our previous year audit recommendations in the conduct of actual physical inventory of the Property, Plant and Equipment, to wit:
Issue an order/memorandum for the conduct of physical inventory taking and be completed within a determined period of time. Require the inventory committee to prepare and submit the Report of Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) within ten (10) days from completion of the inventory as required under Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System, Vol. I.
A complete  Property,  Plant  and Equipment and Supplies Ledger Cards be maintained by the Accounting Office to support the details and breakdown. In the same manner, the GSO also maintain Property and Stock Cards to all pertinent information of deliveries and acceptance of fixed assets/supplies. The accounting records be reconciled against the  property records.
Extra effort be exerted by both the Accounting and General Services Office to determine the details of the said fixed asset accounts.
Update/renew Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR) on equipment/properties issued to various municipal officials and employees.
All properties of the municipality must have a property tag or sticker pasted on each item for proper identification.
Drop the unserviceable properties in the books totalling P2,379,793.10  to ensure the correctness and accuracy of the Property, Plant and Equipment  accounts.
We have recommended that the Accounting Office look into the balances of the said accounts and reconcile the same for proper presentation in the financial statements.

We have recommended that the Accounting and Treasury Offices trace the  discrepancies noted and reconcile their records immediately.  Any discrepancy noted be verified and adjusted so that the cashbook balances of the cash in bank are reconciled with the ledger balances. Moreover, prepare reconciliation more often to avoid accumulation of differences at year end.

We have recommended  that the Municipal Treasurer secure an authority from the Department of Finance to maintain deposits with the Philippine Veterans Bank in order to ensure that government funds are adequately safeguarded and duly protected in case of bankruptcy.

We have recommended that the BAC strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations as regards the prohibition of using brand name in its procurement. The BAC use technical specifications, relevant characteristics and performance requirement of the item to be procured rather than by its name.

We have recommended that Management adhere with the Annual Procurement Plan in the identification of the more suitable method of procurement to be used in the procurement process. We have further recommended that the BAC Secretariat consolidate PPMPs from various units of the procuring entity to make them available for review as indicated in Section 7 of the IRR of RA 9184, prepare the Annual Procurement Plan and copy furnish the Audit Team for reference.
We have recommended that management utilize the 20% Development Fund during the year to address the priority needs of the people and to attain maximum benefits for the intended recipients.

We have recommended that management ensure the submission of the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and RA 10121 in order to evaluate whether the utilization of the fund is within the identified programs, projects and activities in the plan.

We have recommended that the Local Finance Committee and the Municipal School Board stop the utilization of the SEF to programs and projects not included in the provisions under RA 7160 and the new DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01 s. 2017 dated January 19, 2017 in order to maximize the benefits that the students and teachers could avail under this fund.
We have recommended that the prescribed minimum employee’s monthly net take home pay under General Appropriations Act (GAA)  be observed.

We have recommended that management refrain from procuring accountable forms from private entity without first securing the services of recognized government printers as required under Section 23 of the General Appropriations Act 2016.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Acting Municipal Assessor to obtain Transfer Certificate of Title of all the parcels of land acquired by the Municipality as required under Section  449, Volume I of GAAM and Section 39(2) of PD 1445 to serve as proof of absolute ownership over these properties.  Moreover, the Municipal Mayor should instruct the Acting Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer to cause for the transfer from the name of the seller to the Municipality of Burgos of the Tax Declaration of the two parcels of land acquired in the years 1999 and 2013 with area of 4,271 square meters and 139,036 square meters respectively.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor  require the Municipal Treasurer   to deposit collections intact with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day.  The amount collected for the day should be the amount to be deposited in order not to expose the funds for possible misuse or misappropriation and at the same time this will enable the Municipality to earn additional income in the form of interest on bank deposits.

We have recommended that the Acting Municipal Accountant  exert extra effort to locate records of these receivables.  If such efforts exerted are futile, the Municipal Mayor may file the request for authority to write –off said accounts except the accounts Receivables – Disallowances/Charges and Other Receivables arising from cash shortage, to the COA Audit Team Leader and/or Supervising Auditor.  The request shall be supported by complete documents enumerated in Section 8.3 of COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We have recommended the the BAC Chairman and the Municipal Engineer require the BAC Secretariat to furnish the Auditor a copy of the contracts and all supporting documents within five (5) working days after execution thereof.

We have recommended  that the Municipal Mayor  require the Acting Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to submit the revenue and disbursement documents  and required reports within the first ten days of the ensuing month.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Acting Municipal Accountant  submit to the Audit Team all cancelled checks for inspection and proper monitoring.  Likewise, cancelled checks shall be attached to the Report of Checks Issued and reported chronologically with the other checks issued and the word “cancelled” shall be indicated on the report.

We have recommended that the officials and employees concerned prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels   and the Report of Fuel Consumption for all vehicles/heavy equipment owned by the municipality and submit the same to the Auditor for verification within the first ten days of the succeeding month.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor  require the Acting Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the amount of  P5,688,115.95  from the General Fund to Trust Fund bank account as cash back up of the  account Trust Liabilities -DRRMF.  Likewise, the cash to back up the unexpended balances of QRF and DRRM – MOOE at the end of the year starting 2017 should be transferred from General Fund to Trust Fund to support the Trust Liabilities – DRRMF account under the Trust Fund.

We   have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer  intensify the collection of Real Property and Special Education Taxes.  Notices of collection should be sent to the delinquent taxpayers to remind them of their obligation to pay their taxes. Likewise, conduct massive tax campaign in all the barangays and offer tax amnesty in order to motivate delinquent taxpayers to avail of the offer.  In addition, seek the cooperation of the barangay officials, anyway, the barangays have also shares in the collection of realty property taxes.
We have recommended that the municipal officials and employees concerned  implement fully all the programs/ projects / activities embodied under the 20% Development Fund.

We have recommended that  the Sangguniang Bayan  appropriate immediately all funds received from tobacco excise taxes.  Likewise, the Municipal Mayor should cause the prompt implementation of all projects/programs funded out of the tobacco excise taxes in order to give the tobacco farmers who worked hard for the generation of the funds the benefits they rightfully deserve.

We have recommended  that the Municipal Mayor  cause the prompt implementation of all funds received from other national government agencies in order to deliver on time the benefits due to the intended beneficiaries and to avoid returning the unutilized funds to the donor agencies.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor  require the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to exert extra effort to collect such receivables so that the fund could be used by other deserving individuals.  If such efforts exerted are futile, the Municipal Mayor may file the request for authority to write –off dormant accounts to the COA Audit Team Leader and/or Supervising Auditor.  The request shall be supported by complete documents enumerated in Section 8.3 of COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor  require the Chairperson of the GAD Focal Point System to fully implement programs/projects funded under the GAD Plan in order to fully address gender issues/problems of the constituents in the municipal level and to help the national government attain its goal of addressing gender issues in the country.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor  require the Acting Municipal Accountant to see to it that all disbursements are supported with valid and complete documents. Further, management should adhere to Sections 9.4 and 10.4 of COA Circular 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 regarding the settlement of suspensions and disallowances.  Audit suspensions should be settled within ninety (90) calendar days from receipt of the NS to avoid maturity of these suspensions to disallowances and disallowances should be settled within six (6) months from receipt of the ND by the persons liable.

We have recommended and the BAC agreed to:
Continue to update  themselves with the existing guidelines specifically affecting every procurement; and
Ensure that requisite conditions necessary for the mode of procurement adopted are fully complied with.

Considering the significance of timely submission of contracts and Purchase Orders, we have recommended that management henceforth submit all perfected contracts and Purchase Orders along with all the required attachments within five (5) days from perfection as provided under COA Circular No. 2009-001 to enable the Audit Team to conduct timely evaluation of contracts and review of items purchased and thereafter immediately communicate to management any defects/deficiencies noted thereon.

We have recommended and the HOPE directed the BAC to prepare the PMR using the format available in the GPPB website for his approval and submit the same to the GPPB not only in compliance with Section 12.2 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 but most importantly to avoid any sanction in the future.

We have recommended and Management agreed to fully settle the issues on the ROW in order to implement the projects and henceforth, ensure that issues on ROW are fully resolved prior to the commencement of the procurement process not only to support the timely implementation of projects but most significantly, deliver the expected beneficial impact to the constituents.

We have recommended  that the LGU, thru the  Municipal Agriculturist:
Conduct regular monitoring and evaluation on the handling and utilization of the machineries to encourage the Associations to perform their responsibilities explicitly cited in the MOA;
Require the Associations to submit their written policies for the operation and maintenance of farm equipment for accountability, availability and future maintenance; and
Require that the Associations provide appropriate storage spaces to safeguard the machineries from the weather elements, pilfering/vandalism with the end in view of ultimately prolonging their  usefulness in attaining food security and income enhancement.

We have recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed:
Continue to formulate plans and strategies and implement the same to enhance collection of its delinquent real property taxes;
Send demand letters to delinquent taxpayers to remind them of their obligations and avail of the administrative and legal remedies provided by law to enforce collection of the delinquent real property taxes.
We further recommended that Management consider providing additional personnel to enable the office of the municipal treasurer to conduct massive tax campaigns in the different barangays with the end in view of collecting from delinquent tax payers.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to:
Secure immediately property insurance for the non-insured government assets including  their contents; and
Ensure that the updated RPCPPE duly verified by the Auditor shall be submitted to the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS upon renewal of the insurance policies.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that posting of notices to the public of relevant information on all awarded projects be made where the programs, projects and activities are located or carried out in accordance with COA Circular No. 2013-004 in order to promote good governance through transparency and accountability and to encourage public participation.

We have recommended that management request for write-off of the dormant accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 in order to reconcile and clear the books of accounts of dormant receivable accounts and fund transfers for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that no additional cash advances be granted unless the previous cash advance was fully liquidated as required under Section 89 of Presidential Decree No. 1445,as implemented in COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.  Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to provide certification that the previous cash advances have been liquidated and accounted for in the books in compliance with the documentary requirements required under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We have recommended the Local Chief Executive that the amount necessary for payment of honoraria and overtime as enumerated on Section 3.1 of DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-3 be paid to the Members of BAC in conformity with Section 3.4 of the same Budget Circular.

We have recommended that the Municipality continue to appropriate at least 5 per cent fund requirement in the annual budget proposal and to incorporate only GAD-related programs and activities in the GAD Plan to address gender issues and concerns affecting the welfare and full development of women in order to eradicate gender-based inequalities and enable women and men to equally contribute and benefit from development.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive, as the head of the Local School Board, adhere strictly with the regulation in the prioritization and utilization of the SEF, particularly, for the construction and repair of school buildings and establishment of new extension classes or acquisition of IT equipment, books and instructional materials of students and teachers as required under DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1 and No. 1-A in order to ensure an effective educational development within the locality.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to immediately transfer the unexpended/unobligated balance of DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund under the Trust Liability-DRRM account in the Trust Fund books.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to enforce strict collection of the loans receivable that have been long overdue by sending demand letters in order to give opportunity to others who wish to avail of the loan assistance program.  Likewise, interest and penalty should be imposed on the unpaid balances to comply with the terms of the loan.
We also have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to withhold a fixed amount from the salaries of the delinquent municipal employees until their loans are fully paid.

We have recommended that the Accounting Office strictly comply with the submission of reports and supporting documents evidencing the transactions on time in accordance with Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 which requires submission within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month as well as with Section 122 of PD No. 1445.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive bring the Materials and Recovery Facility (MRF) in operation for the intended purpose in accordance with RA 9003.  Utilization thereof may result to:
proper disposal of solid waste, thus, minimize wastes within the municipality;
provision of jobs to its local residents; and
generation of income from sale of recyclable items for additional funds of the agency.
Likewise, we have recommended that budgetary requirement should be set aside for the improvement of the Municipality’s sanitary landfill that will meet the criteria provided under Section 41 of RA 9003 to prevent leachate and hazardous waste that would contaminate the environment and the health of the people.
Lastly, we have recommended that the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan be prepared by the Municipal Solid Waste Board and be submitted to the National Solid Waste Management Commission for approval in compliance to Section 16 of RA 9003.

      Section 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382 to  avoid  the  accumulation  of
      cash in his hands and the exposure of the same  to  possible  risk  of
      loss and misappropriation.

required    to liquidate their outstanding cash  advances  to  settle  their
respective  accountabilities  and  to  reflect  the  fair  balances  of  the
Government Equity      and the related asset and expense accounts.

      determine  the  basis  of  recording,  the  officials  and   employees
      concerned, and the real age, or if not possible, the  approximate  age
      of the account-Due from GOCCs amounting  to  P320,407.66.  Thereafter,

We recommended that  the  Municipal  Accountant  reconciles  the
      reciprocal accounts Due from Other Funds  and  Due  to  Other  Funds,
      thus, fairly present the accounts in the financial statements.

We  recommended  that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  direct  the
      inventory committee  to  complete  the  physical  count  of  all  the
      properties of the LGU and to submit the corresponding Report  on  the
      Physical Count of PPE to the Office of the Auditor for verification.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the  LDRRMO
      to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF for  the

      thereof. The Municipal Accountant may also exert effort to remind  the
      LDRRMO of the submission of the said report to COA.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the LGU’s
      records with that of the Provincial Government of Isabela to establish
      the correct balances of the reciprocal accounts.

1.6		We recommend the management to create an Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical inventory of all properties owned by the Municipality and prepare a report thereon in accordance with Section 124 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I to determine the existence and accuracy of the PPE owned by the LGU. Moreover, direct the Municipal Treasurer to maintain Property Cards and issue Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment, and the Municipal Accountant to maintain PPE Ledger Cards as required in Section 114 of the same Manual.

2.7		We recommend that Management require the Municipal Accountant to adhere strictly the provisions of Sections 4(e) and 5 of Volume I of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I and Section 31 of the same Manual, Volume II. Likewise, direct him to prepare and submit immediately the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs), Check Disbursement Journal (CkDJ), Cash Disbursement Journal (CDJ) and Cash Receipts Journal (CRJ).
2.8	Otherwise, failure on the part of the officials concerned to submit the documents and reports mentioned herein shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission.

3.7	We recommend the Municipal Local School Board to prepare Annual School Board Budget for the SEF and include in the Annual Budget those prioritize programs, projects and activities pursuant to R.A No. 5447, Section 272 of RA 7160 and Joint Circular No. 01 series of 1998 of DECS, DBM and DILG.  Stop using the SEF for expenses not within the list of priority expenses as provided in the provision. Further, Direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Treasurer to stop disbursing without approved appropriation pursuant to Section 4 (1) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

4.4 In view of the above, we recommend the Local School Board to maximize the utilization of the SEF based on RA 5447 as well as Section 272 of RA 7160 and LOI 1462 for the benefits of its intended beneficiaries. Further, we recommend that the Local School Board initiate the inventory of local school buildings that need urgent construction/repairs, operation of such number of extension classes as may be needed to accommodate all children of school and other school needs like books, science and mathematics aids, and simple laboratory devices to help identify the priority PPAs.

5.5	We recommend that Management to strictly adhere RA 9003 to adopt a systematic, comprehensive and ecological solid waste management programs. Further, direct the barangay officials to strictly enforce the MRF in every barangay to adhere proper sorting, segregation, composting, and recycling of solid waste.

8.4	We recommend the management to implement and utilize the LCPC Fund to its programs, projects and activities (PPAs). Direct the members of the LCPC to prepare Local Development Plan for Children and Local Investment Plan for Children, and maximize the allocated LCPC Fund to identified PPAs provided for in DILG MC No 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012. Likewise, submit the LDPC and LIPC with utilization report CY 2016.

9.4		We recommended that management strictly adhere Section 7, Rule II of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and prepare PPMP and consolidated APP. Require the BAC to furnish the Office of the Auditor of the updated PPMP and APP yearly for us to validate and ensure that all procurement undertaken by the LGU were based on the approved annual procurement plan.

10.5	We recommend that management to require the GAD Focal Person to prepare the Annual GPB using the prescribed form and in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer, submit it to the Provincial Planning and Development Office then to the DILG Regional Office for review and approval. Further, develops GAD PAPs to strengthen the Municipality’s gender-responsive governance and ensure that GAD Fund is utilized in align with the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Circular No. 2013-01. Likewise, submit to the Office of the Auditor a copy of the Approved GAD.
.
10.6     The Management assured compliance in the ensuing year.

We have recommended that management should:

Issue an order/memorandum for the conduct of physical inventory taking and shall be completed within a determined period of time. Require the inventory committee to prepare and submit the Report of Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) within ten (10) days from completion of the inventory as required under Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System, Vol. I. 

A complete Property, Plant and Equipment and Supplies Ledger Cards should be maintained by the Accounting Office to support the details and breakdown. In the same manner, the GSO should also maintain Property and Stock Cards to all pertinent information of deliveries and acceptance of fixed assets/supplies. The accounting records should be reconciled against the  property records.

 Extra effort should be exerted by both the Accounting and General Services Office to determine the details of the said fixed asset accounts. 

 Update/renew Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR) on equipment/properties issued to various municipal officials and employees.

All properties of the municipality must have a property tag or sticker pasted on each item for proper identification.

Drop the unserviceable properties in the books totalling P2,379,793.10  to ensure the correctness and accuracy of the Property, Plant and Equipment  accounts.

Record the nine (9) offsprings of breeding animals in the books.
observations.managementAction: Partially Complied. The 9 offsprings of breeding animals have been taken up in the books.
observations.reason: The 9 offsprings were already recorded as shown in Note 10 of the Notes to Financial Statements. Other recommendations were not yet complied hence, reiterated in Finding No. 1 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer should deposit intact daily all collections with authorized depository bank as prescribed by the provisions of Section 32 of the Manual on the , for LGUs Vol. to prevent and avoid possible misuse, misappropriation or even loss of government funds.
observations.managementAction: Partially complied
observations.reason: For CY 2016, not all collections were deposited intact daily with AGDB.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that cash advances for salaries, wages, allowances, honoraria and other similar payments should equal with the net amount of the payroll for a pay period as required under Section 4.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
observations.managementAction: Complied
observations.reason: Cash advances for salaries, wages, allowances, honoraria and other similar payments were already equal to the net amount of the payrolls for a pay period.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that for CY 2016 and thereafter, the Municipality should adhere strictly to DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 in order to bring the optimum benefits to the constituents of the Municipal Government. Valid operating expenses which do not fall within the scope of the aforesaid Joint Memorandum Circular should be properly charge against the General Fund proper.
observations.managementAction: Complied
observations.reason: Management already adheres  to DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that future procurement of equipment, retention money will be deducted from the contract price or a special bank guarantee deposit will be issued by the supplier to cover  the obligation for any manufacturing defects of the equipment purchased and to avoid suspension in audit.
observations.managementAction: Complied
observations.reason: Retention money were already deducted to cover  the obligation for any manufacturing defects of the equipment purchased.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that agency officials concerned should require contractors to post  warranty security, one year after completion of the project, for the protection of the interest of the municipality in case of  structural defects and failures that may arise. The amount shall be released only after the lapse of the applicable warranty periods pursuant to the above regulations. Likewise, the Municipal Engineer being incharge in the implementation of  infrastructure projects of the municipality should monitor compliance by contractors of this requirements to avoid suspensions/disallowances in audit.
observations.managementAction: No action
observations.reason: The municipality had not yet complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that each department head should file in advance their requisitions for supplies needed for each quarter based on their approved Annual Procurement Plan to ensure that all requisitions supplies are approved by authorized officials. Likewise, the BAC Secretariat/GSO should consolidate the requisitions for each department in order to minimize procurement costs.
observations.managementAction: Partially complied
observations.reason: Supplies not available in the Procurement Service were procured through shopping method. This was reiterated in Finding No. 5.2 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the BAC should strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations in order to attain the objectives of competitive bidding.
observations.managementAction: Complied
observations.reason: The BAC strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the BAC should strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations in order to attain the objectives of competitive bidding.
observations.managementAction: Complied

observations.managementAction: Complied

Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant should check and verify all supporting documents to all infrastructure projects. observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to obtain Transfer Certificate of Title of all the parcels of land acquired by the Municipality as required under Section o 449, Volume I of GAAM  and Section 39(2) of PD 1445 to serve  as proof of absolute ownership over these properties. Moreover, the Municipal Mayor should instruct the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer to cause for the transfer from the name of the seller to the Municipality of Burgos  of the Tax Declaration of the two parcels of land acquired in the years 1999 and 2013 with area of 4,271 square meters and 139,036 square meters respectively.
