observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor  require the Municipal Engineer to see to it that materials testing  for infrastructure projects  be done by  DOST/BRS accredited laboratories to uncover any possible sub-standard construction materials used or components supplied by the contractor  in order to ensure the quality of the projects.  Such cost of materials testing should be shouldered by the contractor as this is included in the computation of the ABC.  Likewise, the Acting Municipal Accountant should not process claims of contractors without the materials test results.  Moreover, management should conduct inspection of the completed projects before the end of the one year defect liability period and before the issuance of the Final Certificate of Acceptance.  In case there are structural defects attributable to improper construction and/or use of inferior quality materials, the contractor should be held liable.
observations.managementAction: Complied.
observations.reason: The results of Materials Tests were attached to the disbursement vouchers of infrastructure projects.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management require future contractors to submit  duly accomplished performance security with a validity period that include the one year defects liability period pursuant to Section 39 of the IRR of RA 9184. The validity period shall commence from the effectivity of the contract up to after one year from date of completion of the project or until the issuance of the Certificate of Acceptance.  Moreover, the Municipal Accountant being the internal auditor of the municipality should see to it that all supporting documents of disbursement vouchers are properly accomplished to ensure validity and legality of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by management.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 10, page 41 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We  have recommended  the Acting Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort to locate records that would support these collectibles.  If records could no longer be found, a Resolution should be passed requesting the Commission on Audit Central Office for the dropping of these receivables from the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by management.
observations.reason: The  receivables not substantiated with any records are still included in the 2016 year-end financial statements. Reiterated in Observation No.  4, page 36 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Treasurer to adhere strictly to Section 35.e of the Manual on NGAS, Volume I.  He should forward to the Accounting Office a copy of the validated deposit slips in order to facilitate the recording of transactions in the books of accounts and to enable the Accounting Office to prepare and submit the accounts and financial statements on time.
observations.managementAction: Partially complied.
observations.reason: Not all deposit slips are submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Acting Municipal Accountant should submit to the Audit Team all cancelled checks for inspection and proper monitoring.  Likewise, cancelled checks shall be attached to the Report of Checks Issued and reported chronologically with the other checks issued and the word “cancelled” shall be indicated on the report.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the Municipal Treasurer who assured the Audit Team that the cancelled checks will be submitted to COA.
observations.reason: As of this time, the cancelled checks are not yet submitted to COA. Reiterated in Observation No. 8, page 40 of the report.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended  the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Acting Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to adhere strictly to the provisions of Sections 271 (d) and  164 (c) of RA 7160.  They should release directly to the barangay treasurer the share of each barangay on realty taxes 
and community taxes on a quarterly basis within five (5) days after the end of each quarter.
observations.managementAction: Partially complied.
observations.reason: Only the shares of the barangays on realty taxes for the 1st and 2nd quarters of 2016 were released as of year-end. The shares of the barangays for the 3rd and 4th quarters of 2016 were released in March 2017.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the municipal officials and employees concerned should implement fully all the programs/ projects / activities embodied under the 20% Development Fund.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by management.The Municipal Engineer claimed  that the delay in the implementation of infrastructure projects was due to  lack  of personnel in the Municipal Engineering Office to assist him in the  preparation of program of work and plans and specifications and monitoring of the implementation of infrastructure projects.
observations.reason: The municipality did not implement 83.39% of the 20% Development Fund for 2016.  Reiterated in Observation No. 13, page 47 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to fully implement on time the funds for Supplemental Feeding Program.
observations.managementAction: Complied.
observations.reason: The funds for the Supplemental Feeding Program for 2016 were fully implemented.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the concerned municipal officials and employees to fully implement programs/projects funded under the GAD Plan in order to fully address gender issues/problems of the constituents in the municipal level and to help the national government attain its goal of addressing gender issues in the country.
observations.managementAction: The concerned municipal employees reasoned out that the funds allotted for some programs  under the GAD fund were not utilized because the municipality received funds from the national government agencies for the implementation of the same programs/projects.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No.  17, page 51 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to see to it that all disbursements are supported with valid and complete documents. Further, management should adhere to Sections 9.4 and 10.4 of COA Circular 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 regarding the settlement of suspensions and disallowances which require that audit suspensions should be settled within ninety (90) calendar days from receipt of the NS to avoid maturity of these suspensions to disallowances and disallowances should be settled within six (6) months from receipt of the ND by the persons liable.
observations.managementAction: Management caused the deduction from the payroll of the disallowed Productivity Enhancement Incentives (PEI) of municipal officials and employees.  However, some disbursements were not completely documented.
observations.reason: Some disbursements lacked complete documentation which were suspended in audit.  Reiterated in Observation No. 18, page 52 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Acting Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer continue their laudable effort of strictly withholding taxes due the government and remitting the same to the BIR promptly.
observations.managementAction: Complied.
observations.reason: The total taxes withheld during 2016 including beginning balances amounted to P5,329,731.96 of which the amount of P4.686.970.67 was remitted as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to fully implement on time the funds for Supplemental Feeding Program.
observations.managementAction: Complied.
observations.reason: The  liquidation report for the amount of P100,000.00 had been submitted to DPWH, Candon City, Ilocos Sur.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that  the  Municipal Accountant:
Immediately submit the lacking documents as identified in Appendix A to avoid suspension in audit; and 

Henceforth, ensure the completeness of required supporting documents in compliance with Section 4 (6) of P.D. 1445 and Sections 3, 9.1 and 9.2 of COA Circular No. 2012 – 001 prior to the processing of claims.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the lacking documents.  The Accounting Unit has been requiring all supporting documents from the concerned departments.
observations.reason: Submitted the lacking documents on April 6, 2016.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and management agreed to:

Use the proper account as provided under COA Circular No. 2015-009; and
Make necessary adjusting entries to correct the account balances.
observations.managementAction: The Accountant and the Budget Officer has already conferred with each other as to the accounts to be used
observations.reason: TB of first quarter did not contain Other MOOE

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer:

Always require posting or warranty security in any acceptable form for goods delivered and infrastructures implemented; and 

Henceforth establish a system and maintain records on specific warranty periods and security for each procurement of goods and civil works.
observations.managementAction: Warranty for goods were already required. As to infrastructure projects, a record is maintained in the accounting department (utilizing information from the General Ledger of Public Infrastructures.  Warranties for the projects will be required starting January, 2017
observations.reason: Observed the imposition of warranties in current transactions

observations.managementAction: The LGU is already in the process of acquiring a site in Barangay Agpay for a sanitary landfill
observations.reason:
Noted initial actions by management

Reiterated under Observation No. 7, page 55 of this report

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that:

•	The Local Chief Executive enjoin the Inventory Committee to include periodic inventory of the municipality’s infrastructures and other community facilities and prepare the corresponding report; and 

•	The Municipal Accountant initiate the recording of projects completed in prior years in the Registries of Public Infrastructure in preparation for PPSAS financial reporting.
observations.managementAction: In June, 2016, the Municipal Accountant and Engineer has already started the ocular inspections at Bilis, Tumapoc and New Poblacion-Agpay-Libtong roads
observations.reason: Initial inventory available

observations.managementAction:
The Treasurer’s Office conducted the following:
Tax campaigns in March, 2016
Sent demand letters to delinquent taxpayers during the first semester
Entered into some compromise agreements during the year
Offered discounts as early as December 2016
observations.reason:
Evidence of tax campaigns and demand letters on file

Reiterated under Observation No. 6, page 54 of this report

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and management agreed to formulate and adopt a CPES-based system of monitoring and contractors evaluation to ensure that infrastructure projects conform with the specified requirements of projects.
observations.managementAction: The BAC is still researching on how to implement a CPES-based system of monitoring
observations.reason: Initial data for implementation of the recommendation was available

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the members of the WATSAN/PMU:

•	Either include in the Plans and Targets,  programs for trainings/capacity building for  individuals for the day to day operation of the project to support the establishment of  a qualified WSP to whom operation can be turned over to; or
•	Propose an ordinance for sales and collection from existing service connections for the sustainability of the operation and maintenance of the project.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer has already provided the SB a copy of the relevant documents needed in preparing an applicable ordinance.  During the turn over ceremony, the outgoing officials included this issue in the list of Key Challenges for the next administration
observations.reason: Noted a copy of the information provided to the SB

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant undertake immediate reconciliation of the PPE account between the balances per books and the inventory report (as counted), establish the cause/s of discrepancies, and make appropriate adjustments as warranted.

	We have also recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to maintain complete and updated Equipment Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively.
observations.managementAction: Physical Inventory of all LGU Assets were already conducted.  The Inventory team had already assigned Property Numbers and Property Tags to each LGU asset.  Books will be adjusted as a result of the Physical Inventory count.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 1.a and 1.b, page29 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that no cash advance shall be granted to non-bonded official/s for purposes other than for travelling expenses in conformity with COA Circular No. 97-002 and COA Circular No. 2009-002.
observations.managementAction: We only allow Cash Advances for TEV and Training to non-bonded officials after this observation was discussed to the agency.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to check and review the supporting documents before preparation of vouchers to ascertain completeness of requirements and to ascertain the validity and legality of claims against government funds pursuant to Section 4 (6) of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: We made sure that we thoroughly review documentary requirements before payment.
observations.reason:
There are still documentary requirements not attached in the vouchers. 

Reiterated in Observation No. 4, pages 32-35 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Agency adhere to the provisions of Section 48.2 and 54.1 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 as well as the imposition of control measures to ensure that procurement is effected in an efficient, economical and effective manner that is most advantageous to the government and to prevent possible disallowances for such violation.
observations.managementAction: Procurement of Supplies and Equipment were already done through public bidding except for Emergency Purchases or small value procurement.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) strictly adhere to the provisions of Republic Act No. 9184 and Administrative Order No. 17 in order to promote transparency in the procurement process aimed at reducing procurement costs.

We have also recommended that a dedicated office in-charge of procuring activities be designated to ensure that effective planning and implementation of procurement activities be achieved.
observations.managementAction:
Often times the requested common use supplies were not available in the Procurement Service and it takes time before its availability.

Common-use supplies were already being procured thru public bidding except for emergency cases.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that management adhere strictly with COA Circular 97-002 requiring payments for all government transactions to be made by check and payable directly to the creditor/supplier or by the Disbursing Officer thru his cash advance.

We also have recommended that management stop the practice of disbursing government funds thru reimbursement, except for petty expenses which may be paid thru the petty cash fund.
observations.managementAction: We really don’t allow reimbursements except for the expenses for the immediate repair of motor vehicles and other petty expenses.
observations.reason: Upon review of the check disbursement reports, there are some transactions other than repair of motor vehicles that are not considered petty expenses that were claimed thru reimbursement.  But expenses claimed thru reimbursement were minimize this CY 2016.

observations.managementAction: Demand letters and notices of delinquency were sent to delinquent taxpayers.
observations.reason:
Per validation, the amount of delinquent RPT increase to P1,920,126.02 as of December 31, 2016, despite the continuous effort of the Treasury department in reminding the delinquent taxpayers to pay their taxes on time.

Reiterated in Observation No. 12, pages 44-45 of the report.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that:

Management adhere strictly with the mandated use of government markings on motor vehicles pursuant to the provisions under COA Circular No. 77-61;
Driver’s Trip Ticket bear proper approval from the Head of the Agency or his authorized representative, be serially numbered and properly accomplished pursuant to the same Circular; and
The Local Chief Executive direct the driver/s to prepare the monthly report of fuel consumption and summary of official travel for proper control and monitoring and submit the same to this office pursuant to same Circular.
observations.managementAction:
Government Vehicles have no markings yet up to this date.
An employee in the Office of the mayor was assigned to prepare the monthly report of fuel consumption.
observations.reason: Monthly report on fuel consumption were already attached in the vouchers.  However, per validation, the motor vehicles of the agency still had no government markings on motor vehicles pursuant to the provisions under COA Circular No. 77-61.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF to the NDRRMC through the Office of the Civil Defense on the 5th day following the end of each month and furnish the same to this office.
observations.managementAction: We only prepare the report at the end of the year.
observations.reason: Quarterly report was already submitted to this office for CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management should continue to incorporate and reflect GAD issues and concerns in the annual budget proposal and work and financial plans in order to eradicate gender-based inequalities and enable women and men to equally contribute and benefit from development.
observations.managementAction: Continued the GAD related issues of the agency.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No.10, pages 42-43 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management bring the Materials and Recovery Facility (MRF) in operation by utilizing the MRF building and the bioreactor with two shredders for the intended purpose in accordance with RA 9003 in order that the facility could be used efficiently and effectively.
observations.managementAction: The management is already preparing the MRF for its operation this CY 2017.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 16, pages 47-49 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned LGU personnel to cause submission of the accounts of the LGU in conformance with COA rules and regulations.
observations.reason: Accounts in 2017 were not still submitted on time, hence, reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Accountant and BAC to have the deficiencies/lacking documents submitted in support of the DV covering the payment of 40.98% progress accomplishment for the Repair and Upgrading of Evacuation Center in the amount of P349,461.37 in conformance with existing regulation, otherwise the transaction will be suspended in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the contractor to post additional surety bond to complete the 30% requirement of the performance bond.
observations.managementAction: The contractor was required to submit an additional surety bond to complete the 30% requirement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to conduct periodic reconciliation of the reciprocal account balances with the PGI.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office started the reconciliation of the reciprocal accounts.
observations.reason: However, said accounts remained unreconciled as of December 31, 2016, hence, reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned LGU officials to submit a copy of the DILG approved annual GAD Plan and Budget and the corresponding GAD Accomplishment Report to the COA Audit Team assigned to the agency within the period prescribed under COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned LGU officials to submit the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day of the following month in compliance to COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.reason: The monthly reports in 2017 were still not submitted within the prescribed period, hence, reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit his collections intact daily or not later than the following banking day with the LGU’s depository bank in conformity with the requirement prescribed under Section 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Accounting Office to prepare and submit the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements regularly to the audit for verification.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accounting Office has been preparing and submitting Bank Reconciliation Statements for 2016 to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned municipal officials to: a) cause the immediate liquidation of their cash advances in compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002; b) require that no additional cash advances be granted to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance given is first settled; c) to ensure that all cash advances shall be fully liquidated at the end of each year; and d) to direct that elected officials be granted cash advance only for their official travelling expenses. We further recommended that the Local Chief Executive directs the Municipal Accountant to send demand letters to officials and employees with outstanding cash advances and enforce prompt settlement and liquidation within the period prescribed by existing regulation.
observations.reason: Cash advances amounting to P1,653,074.80 remained unliquidated as of December 31, 2016, hence, reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the inventory committee to conduct annual physical count of its properties and submit the corresponding Annual Inventory report on PPE/Report on the Physical Count of PPE on or before the 31st day of January of each year in conformance with existing regulation.
observations.managementAction: Management conducted partial physical inventory.
observations.reason: The Report on the partial Physical Count of PPE conducted was not yet submitted to COA, hence, reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the setting up of the inventory account following the perpetual inventory method to properly account for purchases of office supplies, accountable forms and other materials in conformance to the provisions of NGAS on accounting for supplies.

We recommended that management adhere to the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2014-83 in order that contractual and job order personnel will be entitled to the benefits and protection against hazards of disability, sickness, maternity, old age, death and other contingencies that the SSS offer.
observations.managementAction: Management conducted a dialogue with the Job Order employees to enroll them to SSS.
observations.reason: The Job Order employees refused to be enrolled to SSS because the contribution/premium will be deducted from their wages.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to comply strictly with the rules and regulations in the disbursement of government funds. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to set up receivables for the funds transferred and demand liquidation from concerned association, thereafter, record all subsequent fund transfers to the appropriate asset accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that in cases of lump sum appropriations containing general terms, a separate SB appropriation ordinance or resolution always be secured by the Municipal Mayor to serve as an authority to enter into contract in behalf of the Municipal Government. Further, Management should evade from practicing lump sum appropriations containing generic terms in accordance to Section 22 (c) of Republic Act No. 7160 and the afore-cited jurisprudence.
observations.managementAction: Management to comply

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to stop the practice of allowing job order personnel to travel outside the Municipality with travel allowances and/or per diems. Authority to travel should be granted only when these are urgent, extremely necessary, involves the minimum expenditure and are beneficial to the agency concerned and/or the country without resulting to depletion of LGU resources, increased MOOE and wastage of government funds.
observations.managementAction: Management agree to comply

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to stop the practice of making advance payment for services not yet rendered or supplies and materials not yet delivered under any contract entered into by the LGU. Ensure that services or goods have been completely delivered before payment could have been made in compliance to the pertinent provisions of RA 7160, PD 1445 and RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: Management agree to comply

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to adhere strictly to COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012 and Section 2 of PD 1445. Management should be prudent in disbursing government funds and this should be safeguarded against loss or wastage through improper disposition. Refrain from spending government funds not supportive of the implementation of the objectives and mission of the LGU.

We further recommended and Management agreed to exert effort for the repair of the meat grinder. Compel the supplier to fix it since this is still under warranty.
observations.managementAction: Management agree to comply

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the BAC conduct pre-procurement and pre-bid conferences or as need arises to clarify terms and conditions and specifications stipulated in the bidding documents. The Committee should set or create a shortlist of suppliers and contractors as a guide to canvassers to attend and observe the proceedings of the conference and special meetings to enhance transparency, in compliance with the implementing rules and regulations of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: Management agree to comply

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to conduct public bidding as the regular mode of procurement in accordance with the provisions of the revised IRR of RA 9184. Further, the Bids and Awards Committee shall ensure that the supporting RFQs have complete details as to the identity of the bidders and terms and conditions in strict adherence to the provisions of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: Management already practice Public Bidding

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to stop the practice of disbursing government funds without appropriation. This renders the transactions illegal resulting to risk of loss of government funds. Cause the refund of the amount of the cellular phones purchased.
observations.managementAction: The recipients of the units were already refunded the said disbursement

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to adhere strictly to the provisions of Philippine Commission on Women, Department of Interior and Local Government, Department of Budget and Management and National Economic and Development Authority Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01. GAD Focal Point System shall be established tasked to promote gender-responsive governance, protect and fulfill women’s human rights, and promote women’s economic development.
observations.managementAction: The Management already establish GAD Focal Point System

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management immediately remit to the Bureau of the Treasury the unexpended balance of P100,000.00 and to strictly adhere to the provisions of the Memorandum of Agreement, Section 10 of RA 10633 of the GAA FY 2014 and Executive Order No. 338 dated May 17, 1996.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality utilized the fund for the construction of a garage but documents authorizing the agency to utilize the fund for such were not yet submitted to the audit team.
